ADSP_w002_exception.d: A processor exception was detected by the Audio Digital Signal Processor (ADSP) exception handler.
ADSP_w002_exception.m: Audio system functionality may be degraded or unavailable. Exception may indicate a critical error that requires processor reset.

ADSP_w022_emacErr.m: The Active Noise Canceling (ANC) feature may be unavailable or may not operate as expected.

ADSP_w033_a2baDiscoveryFailed.m: Audio system functionality may not operate as expected.

ADSP_w050_extSpkFault.d: The Audio Digital Signal Processor (ADSP) has detected an electrical condition condition preventing functionality of the external speakers.

ADSP_w066_rearPwsFault.d: The Audio Digital Signal Processor (ADSP) has detected a fault condition preventing functionality of the Rear Pedestrian Warning System (RPWS).

ADSP_w067_baseamp0Fault.m: Compromised audio performance, signal degradation, or complete loss of audio functionality.

ADSP_w071_versionMismatch.m: The audio system may not operate as expected if there is a difference in features between the reported versions.

ADSP_w080_micInitError.m: Microphones may not function as expected, impacting voice recognition and audio input features.

AIR_a001_dcLinkVoltageFault.d: Air Compressor high voltage is below or above normal.

AIR_a002_logicVoltageFault.d: Air Compressor low voltage is below or above normal.

AIR_a004_softwareFault.d: Air Compressor internal software fault detected.

AIR_a005_hardwareFault.d: Air Compressor internal software fault detected.

AIR_a008_temperatureCutback.m: Air Compressor may have reduced capability

AIR_a009_driverBridgeFault.d: Air Compressor short circuit detected on motor phase or IGBT.

AIR_a010_motorCutback.d: Air Compressor voltage or temperature cutback active.
AIR_a010_motorCutback.m: Air Compressor may have reduced capability

AIR_a011_motorSpeedFault.d: Air Compressor over speed has occured.
AIR_a011_motorSpeedFault.m: Air Compressor may have reduced capability

AIR_a013_encoderFault.d: Air Compressor motor encoder open circuit detected.

AIR_a014_thermistorFault.d: Air Compressor thermistor wire open circuit detected.

AIR_a015_compressingNotCapable.d: Air Compressor failed to startup due to payload reason.

AIR_a016_vcMIA.d: Air Compressor is missing VC Message.
AIR_a016_vcMIA.m: Air Compressor may have reduced capability

AIR_a017_iapuMIA.d: Air Compressor is missing IAPU Message.

AIR_a018_airCompressorMiscAlert.d: Air compressor internral fault mapping, used for logging.

AIR_a020_motorTempWarning.d: Air compressor has exceeded maximum motor temperature.
AIR_a020_motorTempWarning.m: If motor temp continues to increase, compressor may stop producing air

APP_w004_accDisabled.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) has detected a condition on the vehicle which limits Traffic-Aware Cruise Control (TACC) feature availability.
APP_w004_accDisabled.m: Traffic-Aware Cruise Control (TACC) will not be available.

APP_w009_aebFault.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detects that Automatic Emergency Braking (AEB) is unavailable due to a condition affecting communication with the Drive Inverter (DI) or DI functionality.

APP_w010_radcCalibIssue.d: The radar is vertically and/or horizontally misaligned, requiring a calibration.

APP_w011_scaEvent.d: A side collision event is detected as imminent, which causes the activation of Side Collision Assist (SCA).

APP_w025_LC_Steering_Override.d: The driver has applied sufficient torque to the steering wheel to cancel Autosteer / Lateral Control (LC).
APP_w025_LC_Steering_Override.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when the alert is present.

APP_w033_gtwMia.m: Autopilot features will be unavailable, or may not function as expected.

APP_w034_sccmMia.m: Autopilot features that require messages from the SCCM, such as Autosteer and Autopark, may be limited or unavailable.

APP_w040_autopilotAngleSaturated.m: Autosteer is temporarily unavailable, and a chime is played through the User Interface (UI).

APP_w048_APP_Features_Disabled.d: User notification that Autopilot features are unavailable. This alert is set in addition to one or more alert(s) indicating a specific condition affecting Autopilot functionality.

APP_w051_radcEcuIssue.m: Traffic-Aware Cruise Control (TACC) is unavailable. Other Autopilot features may be limited or unavailable.

APP_w058_epbMia.m: Autopilot features may be unavailable, or may not function as expected.

APP_w182_mainCameraStreamExit.m: Autopilot functionality may be limited or unavailable.

APP_w183_narrowCameraStreamExit.m: Autopilot functionality may be limited or unavailable.

APP_w184_fisheyeCameraStreamExit.m: Autopilot functionality may be limited or unavailable.

APP_w186_rPillarCameraStreamExit.m: Autopilot functionality may be limited or unavailable.

APP_w191_autopilotAborting.d: Alert indicating autopilot aborted from active state.

APP_w192_emergencySpeakerFail.d: The emergency speaker encountered a condition that prevents it from initializing or playing the emergency chime.
APP_w192_emergencySpeakerFail.m: Autopilot notifications may not occur, or may not occur as expected.

APP_w194_accDriverResumeRqrd.d: While Traffic-Aware Cruise Control (TACC) is enabled, the driver stops the vehicle. TACC enters a HOLD state and requires driver input before resuming. This alert is primarily informational.
APP_w194_accDriverResumeRqrd.m: Press accelerator pedal to resume cruise control

APP_w195_scwUnavailable.d: There is a condition making Autopilot features unavailable. This causes the Side Collision Warning (SCW) feature to be temporarily unavailable.

APP_w199_apcAbort.d: Autopark is aborted by the driver, drive inverter (DI) electronic control unit (ECU), or Park Assist (PARK) ECU. The driver must finish parking the vehicle manually.
APP_w199_apcAbort.m: Since Autopark was aborted, the driver must finish parking the vehicle manually.

APP_w203_apcActivation.d: Autopark has been activated. The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) will start controlling steering, brakes, and acceleration to park the vehicle.
APP_w203_apcActivation.m: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) will control steering, brakes, and acceleration to park the vehicle.

APP_w204_apcFinalFront.d: This alert is primarily for informational purposes and reports the front measurements when Autopark has been completed or has aborted.
APP_w204_apcFinalFront.m: This alert is primarily for informational purposes, and it does not indicate any expected impact on vehicle functionality.

APP_w205_apcFinalRear.d: This alert is primarily for informational purposes and reports the rear measurements when Autopark has been completed or has aborted.
APP_w205_apcFinalRear.m: This alert is primarily for informational purposes, and it does not indicate any expected impact on vehicle functionality.

APP_w206_autosteerNotEnabled.d: The driver tries to engage Autosteer when it is not available / enabled in the User Interface (UI).

APP_w207_autosteerUnavailable.d: An attempt is made to engage Autosteer, but Autosteer is not available. Autosteer will not be activated.

APP_w210_camObstrcted.d: One or more of the forward-facing cameras (main, narrow, and fisheye cameras) is obstructed. A camera may be blocked by debris, frost, or condensation.

APP_w211_accNoSeatBelt.d: While Traffic-Aware Cruise Control (TACC) is active, the driver unbuckles their seat belt, which causes Autopilot to slow down the vehicle.

APP_w212_lcUnavailableStrikeOut.d: The Autopilot Automatic Lane Change (ALC) feature is disabled for the rest of the drive because the driver's hands were not detected on the steering wheel / yoke after 3 audible reminders. To re-enabl

APP_w213_autosteerStruckOut.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the driver's hands are not on the steering wheel after three consecutive reminders. Autopilot is not available for the remainder of the drive.
APP_w213_autosteerStruckOut.m: Autopilot is unavailable for the remainder of the current drive cycle.

APP_w216_driverOverriding.d: The driver overrides the Traffic-Aware Cruise Control (TACC) feature by using the accelerator pedal. This results in Autopilot not applying the brakes, favoring the driver's input instead.

APP_w217_lcUnavailableSpeeding.d: Lateral Control (LC) is unavailable due to the vehicle being driven over 95mph (160kmph). This alert is not applicable to vehicles driving in Germany.
APP_w217_lcUnavailableSpeeding.m: Autosteer unavailable for the rest of this drive

APP_w263_lssAbort.m: Lane Support Systems (LSS) features may not be available, or may not function as expected. The vehicle may no longer stay in its lane without assistan

APP_w306_lssUltrasonicsBlocked.m: Lane Support System (LSS) features, including Lane Departure Avoidance (LDA), may be limited or unavailable.

APS_w010_radcCalibIssue.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects forward radar not calibrated.

APS_w019_ECU_Stack_Overflow.m: Some Autopilot features may not work as expected.

APS_w020_ECU_checkstopError.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) receives CPU instruction execution error.

APS_w021_ECU_Temperature_Issue.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects Electronic Control Unit (ECU) temperature out of nominal range.
APS_w021_ECU_Temperature_Issue.m: System may shut down to prevent part damage. Autopilot features will be unavailable.

APS_w024_ECU_Watchdog_Reset.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) watchdog timer expires.

APS_w027_ECU_Reset_Fault.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) / Aurix resets unexpectedly.

APS_w030_Heater_Issue.d: Autopilor Processor Secondary (APS) detects a problem with the windshield heater grid connection.
APS_w030_Heater_Issue.m: Mist / fog may accumulate in front of the forward facing cameras and impact Autopilot functionality.

APS_w033_gtwMia.d: One or more of the messages that the Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) receives periodically from the vehicle gateway (GTW) is not received over the Controller Area Network (CAN), which may cause Au
APS_w033_gtwMia.m: AP features that depend on GTW messages will be unavailable.

APS_w034_sccmMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects steering column control module (SCCM) is missing in action.
APS_w034_sccmMia.m: Features that depend on SCCM messages will be disabled.

APS_w035_espMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects Electronic Stability Program (ESP) is missing in action.
APS_w035_espMia.m: Features that depend on ESP messages will be disabled.

APS_w036_bdyMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects body controller is missing in action.
APS_w036_bdyMia.m: Features that depend on body controller messages will be disabled.

APS_w039_sdm_rcm_Mia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects Supplemental Deployment Module (SDM) / Restraints Control Module (RCM) are missing in action.
APS_w039_sdm_rcm_Mia.m: Features that depend on SDM / RCM messages will be disabled.

APS_w045_radMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects radar is missing in action.
APS_w045_radMia.m: Features that depend on radar messages will be disabled.

APS_w048_DAS_Features_Disabled.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) warns when all Autopilot (AP) features are disabled.

APS_w051_radcEcuIssue.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects radar Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has internal error.

APS_w052_radcSensorIssue.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) receives warning that the radar sensor has experienced an issue.

APS_w053_radcCommIssue.d: Forward radar has not received valid vehicle signals from Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS).

APS_w055_radcVersionMismatch.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) warns that expected radar firmware (FW) version does not match the actual radar FW version.

APS_w056_diMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects drive inverter (DI) is missing in action.

APS_w058_epbMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects Electronic Park Brake (EPB) is missing in action.

APS_w059_parkMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects Park Assist (PARK) is missing in action.

APS_w061_tasMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) Tesla Air Suspension (TAS) is missing in action.

APS_w063_parkVersionMismatch.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary warns that expected Park electronic control unit (ECU) firmware (FW) version does not match the actual Park ECU FW version.
APS_w063_parkVersionMismatch.m: Autopark and summon functions will be disabled.

APS_w064_epasMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects electronic power assist steering (EPAS) is missing in action.

APS_w070_apcIncompleteCal.d: Park electronic control unit (ECU) detects Autopark calibration incomplete.

APS_w071_selfparkStarted.d: Selfpark Maneuver Started alert that logs when a Summon sequence is initiated.
APS_w071_selfparkStarted.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when this alert is present.

APS_w072_selfparkComplete.d: Selfpark Maneuver Complete alert that logs at the end of a Summon maneuver.
APS_w072_selfparkComplete.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when this alert is present.

APS_w077_pmmActive.m: Autopilot will brake / prevent acceleration to avoid potential collision.

APS_w081_pmmActiveBraking.d: Pedal Misapplication Mitigation (PMM) applies brakes to avoid collision.
APS_w081_pmmActiveBraking.m: Autopilot will brake to avoid potential collision.

APS_w129_ECU_Power_Issue.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary warns when one or more voltage rails fail to reach expected voltage level.
APS_w129_ECU_Power_Issue.m: Some Autopilot components may not be powered correctly.

APS_w130_ECU_Thermal_Issue.d: Autopoilot Processor Secondary warns when temperature of Autopilot (AP) board exceeds thresholds. .
APS_w130_ECU_Thermal_Issue.m: AP will shutdown, leading to loss of AP features if temperature goes above threshold.

APS_w132_appVersionMismatch.d: Autopilot Processor Primary (APP) firmware (FW) version does not match the Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) version, making Autopilot (AP) features unavailable.

APS_w133_ETHSwitchError.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not initialized the Ethernet switch correctly.
APS_w133_ETHSwitchError.m: Autopilot communication over Ethernet will be broken.

APS_w134_appMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more messages from the Autopilot Processor Primary (APP), making Autopilot (AP) features unavailable.

APS_w135_appCritical.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects that the Autopilot Processor Primary (APP) is not functioning as expected, making Autopilot (AP) features unavailable.

APS_w136_gpsDisabled.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition affecting the functionality of the GPS receiver.
APS_w136_gpsDisabled.m: Autopilot features depending on GPS may not be available, or may not function as expected.

APS_w137_gpsFaultReset.d: The Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects the GPS receiver was reset, causing a temporary reduction in location accuracy.
APS_w137_gpsFaultReset.m: Autopilot features depending on GPS may be unavailable, or may not function as expected. The GPS marker may be temporarily inaccurate.

APS_w138_gpsComIssue.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects communication issues with the GPS receiver.
APS_w138_gpsComIssue.m: Navigation and Autosteer will be temporarily broken.

APS_w140_vcfrontMiaVehicleBus.m: If this condition occurs at startup, AP will not boot completely. If this condition occurs while AP is already running, ALC will be unavailable.

APS_w141_rcmMiaPartyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w142_diMiaPartyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w143_espMiaPartyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w144_vcleftMiaPartyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w145_vcrightMiaPartyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w146_epas3pMiaPartyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w147_vcleftMiaVehBus.m: Autopilot features will be unavailable since APS forwards these messages to other electronic control units, including radar.

APS_w148_vcrightMiaVehBus.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from the right vehicle controller (VCRIGHT) on the Vehicle Bus, making Autopilot (AP) features u
APS_w148_vcrightMiaVehBus.m: AP features will be unavailable because APS forwards these messages to other electronic control units, including radar.

APS_w149_epblMiaVehBus.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from Electronic Park Brake left (EPBL) on Vehicle Bus.
APS_w149_epblMiaVehBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w150_sccmMiaVehBus.d: APS has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from Steering Column Control Module (SCCM) on Vehicle Bus.
APS_w150_sccmMiaVehBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w151_apRecovered.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) status monitor detects Autopilot Processor Primary (APP) to be functioning as expected again after previously detecting APP performance was not optimal. Autopilot (

APS_w153_eacInhibit.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects automatic steering is inhibited.
APS_w153_eacInhibit.m: Autopilot features like Autosteer and side collision assist will be disabled.

APS_w155_ethLwipError.d: Lightweight Internet Protocol (LWIP) software detects stack error.
APS_w155_ethLwipError.m: Autopilot Processor Secondary may drop messages from Autopilot Processor Primary.

APS_w157_autopilotReboot.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary recieves Autopilot (AP) board reboot is requested through user interface (UI).

APS_w186_TurboB_A72_watchdog.m: All AP features will be unavailable.

APS_w189_vcfrontMiaPartyBus.m: Some Autopilot features like Autowiper will be impacted.

APS_w190_iboosterMia.m: Some Autopilot features like Traffic Aware Cruise Control will be impacted.

APS_w192_CANFault.d: Driver Assistance System (DAS) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a safety management subsystem (SMS) / Autopilot (AP) ring buffer error.
APS_w192_CANFault.m: Some Autopilot features will be unavailable.

APS_w199_apcAbort.d: Autopark Aborted alert that logs when an Autopark or Summon maneuver has aborted due to driver actions or a condition internal or external to the vehicle.
APS_w199_apcAbort.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when this alert is present.

APS_w203_apcActivation.d: Autopark Activated alert that logs when an Autopark maneuver is successfully initiated.
APS_w203_apcActivation.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when this alert is present.

APS_w204_apcFinalFront.d: Autopark Front Measurements alert that logs when an Autopark or Summon maneuver is completed.
APS_w204_apcFinalFront.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when this alert is present.

APS_w205_apcFinalRear.d: Autopark Rear Measurements alert that logs when an Autopark or Summon maneuver is completed.
APS_w205_apcFinalRear.m: This alert is for logging purposes only. The vehicle is operating as expected when this alert is present.

APS_w209_autoSummonRequest.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary receives Auto Summon request by a double-press of the park button on the stalk lever.

APS_w235_a72LoopDetected.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects Autopilot Processor Primary (APP) or Autopilot Processor Backup (APB) boot loop.
APS_w235_a72LoopDetected.m: Backup camera, Autopilot, and safety features unavailable.

APS_w237_faultMgrEventDetected.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects a fault in the Security Gate Keeper (SGK) fault manager.

APS_w242_tripMBISTFailure.d: Indicates the reason for the Tesla Inference Processor (TRIP) issue reported by the memory built-in self-test (MBIST). Autopilot features are unavailable.

APS_w243_tripHWErrorStatus.d: The Tesla Inference Processor (TRIP) reports a hardware condition. Autopilot features are unavailable.

APS_w244_gpuHWFaultStatus.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) reports a graphic processing unit (GPU) hardware condition. Autopilot features are unavailable.

APS_w245_eloopRxFromUnexpectedSourceMAC.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) received a frame from an unexpected Media Access Control (MAC) source. The payload has a 6 byte MAC address of an unknown source and the address is in the decimal n
APS_w245_eloopRxFromUnexpectedSourceMAC.m: APS may receive ethernet frames from an unknown sender.

APS_w246_ethloopSwitchPortLinkDown.d: The APS detects that a port of the etherloop switch has a link down, indicating a condition affecting the cabling or the electronic control unit (ECU) connected to that port. On turbo A, Port 2 is con
APS_w246_ethloopSwitchPortLinkDown.m: Communication between the various electronic control units (ECUs) on the etherloop bus may be disrupted.

APS_w247_ethernetFrameError.d: The Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) detects that it received a frame with errors. These frames are not processed.
APS_w247_ethernetFrameError.m: Frequent occurrence of this alert indicates a hardware condition that could affect operation of the vehicle.

APS_w248_ethloopSwitchPoorSqi.d: The Autopilot Secondary (APS) detects poor signal quality on one or more ports of the etherloop switch.
APS_w248_ethloopSwitchPoorSqi.m: Communication between the various electronic control units (ECUs) may fail.

APS_w249_ufsFailures.d: The Autopilot Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects an unexpected condition affecting the Universal Flash Storage (UFS).
APS_w249_ufsFailures.m: Autopilot functionality is limited or unavailable. The rear-view (backup) camera feed is unavailable on the Media Control Unit (MCU).

APS_w252_ethSwitchPortLinkDown.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Media Control Unit (MCU) port connected to the ethernet switch has a link down, indicating a condition affecting the cabling or the ECU con
APS_w252_ethSwitchPortLinkDown.m: Communication between the autopilot ECU and MCU will be disrupted.

APS_w253_powerStateTimeout.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) encountered an unusual timeout in power state transition.
APS_w253_powerStateTimeout.m: Autopilot may unexpectedly stay on or off.

APS_w254_sleepStateTimeout.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) encountered an unusual timeout in sleep state transition.
APS_w254_sleepStateTimeout.m: Autopilot may unexpectedly stay on or off.

APS_w255_bootHealth.d: APS electronic control unit (ECU) encountered an unusual error during boot process.

APS_w280_pepsMia.d: One or more of the messages the Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) receives periodically from the Primary Electric Power Steering (PEPS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not received, indicating the

APS_w281_rsaMia.d: One or more of the messages the Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) receives periodically from the Rear Steering Actuator (RSA) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not received, indicating the RSA ECU ma

APS_w284_cam12VBuckBoostAPwrIssue.d: The 12V Power output of the buck-boost converter on the Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) A-side has encountered issues.
APS_w284_cam12VBuckBoostAPwrIssue.m: Autopilot functionality may be limited or unavailable.

APS_w285_cam12VBuckBoostBPwrIssue.d: The 12V Power output of the buck-boost converter on the Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) B-side has encountered issues.
APS_w285_cam12VBuckBoostBPwrIssue.m: Autopilot functionality may be limited or unavailable.

APS_w286_cam12VEFuseAPrimaryIssue.m: No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

APS_w287_cam12VEFuseASecondaryIssue.d: The secondary camera eFuse on the Autopilot Electronic Control Unit (ECU) A-side encounters a condition affecting its functionality.
APS_w287_cam12VEFuseASecondaryIssue.m: No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

APS_w288_brakeboosterMia.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from Brake Booster (BB) on Party Bus.
APS_w288_brakeboosterMia.m: Some Autopilot features like Traffic Aware Cruise Control will be impacted.

APS_w292_epas3pMiaBdyBus.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from Primary Electronic Power Assisted Steering Gear (EPAS3) on Body Bus.
APS_w292_epas3pMiaBdyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w310_epas3pMiaBdyBus.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from Primary Electronic Power Assisted Steering Gear (EPAS3) on Body Bus.
APS_w310_epas3pMiaBdyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

APS_w315_epas3pMiaBdyBus.d: Autopilot Processor Secondary (APS) has not received one or more Controller Area Network (CAN) messages from Primary Electronic Power Assisted Steering Gear (EPAS3) on Body Bus.
APS_w315_epas3pMiaBdyBus.m: Autopilot features will be disabled since APS forwards these messages to radar.

BCCEN_w003_SWAssertion.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w004_CANMIA.m: Other ECUs may report this ECU as being MIA and behaviour will be impacted. This alert won't be seen until CAN communcation has returned.

BCCEN_w075_SCUncalibrated.d: Motor calibration is usually performed as part of routine 0x170 for BCCEN. The calibration data is stored in the module's non-volatile memory.
BCCEN_w075_SCUncalibrated.m: Automatic position recall disabled for affected axes, manual adjustment may stop working

BCCEN_w076_HVACRailError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w077_DriveRailError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w078_GloveBoxHSD.d: The electrical hardware for the glovebox will disable itself if it encounters an 'overtemperature' or 'overcurrent' condition. If this is a recurring problem, a module replacement may eliminate the is
BCCEN_w078_GloveBoxHSD.m: The glovebox will not respond for 15 seconds. May eventually lead to a totally inoperative glovebox if triggered consistently over a long period of ti

BCCEN_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.d: reseting the controller should fix the issue.
BCCEN_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.m: controller may have behaviors that correspond with the wrong side of the car

BCCEN_w080_GloveBoxLatch.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w082_FrontFootwellIllum.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w083_CourtesyLighting.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w090_CrashUnlockError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w091_CrashStateMismatch.m: If any crash signal is active, crash behavior is active

BCCEN_w092_AmbientTmpSensor.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w093_OTPError.m: Genealogy information may not be read.

BCCEN_w094_transponderKeyNotSet.d: The alert is evaluated at drive rail cycle. The session key is needed for transponder operation.
BCCEN_w094_transponderKeyNotSet.m: Transponder operation will not work until a successful drive rail cycle without this alert present (per key)

BCCEN_w095_AlarmTriggered.d: This alert simply tracks the occurrence and cause of an alarm, and does not indicate that the intrusion system is necessarily faulty.

BCCEN_w096_UHFPLLNotlocked.d: If you see this alert active, BCCEN needs a reset to clear off this alert.
BCCEN_w096_UHFPLLNotlocked.m: Charge port door may not open with handle

BCCEN_w097_UHFCalibrationFail.d: If you see this alert active, BCCEN needs a reset to clear off this alert.
BCCEN_w097_UHFCalibrationFail.m: Charge port door may not open with handle

BCCEN_w098_UHFSleepRequestFail.d: No action required with regard to this alert.

BCCEN_w099_UHFWakeRequestFail.d: BCCEN reset should clear this alert and fault.
BCCEN_w099_UHFWakeRequestFail.m: Charge port door may not open with handle

BCCEN_w100_TrailerAuxRailError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCCEN_w105_ECUDebugMode.d: Production vehicle should not have Debug Jumper in place. Remove jumper from ECU.
BCCEN_w105_ECUDebugMode.m: ECU Debug Jumper will disable SBC watchdog, preventing external watchdog reset protection.

BCCEN_w106_HighCPULoad.d: Log contains CPU load for each task.
BCCEN_w106_HighCPULoad.m: High CPU load can indicate ineffecient code.

BCCEN_w107_HighStackUsage.d: Log contains stack usage for each task.
BCCEN_w107_HighStackUsage.m: Stack overflow can occur if there is not enough stack space.

BCCEN_w108_immobOperationFault.d: This alert is for debug. It will indicate why the immobilizer was unable to mobilize.
BCCEN_w108_immobOperationFault.m: If this alert is continuously set, the vehicle may not be able to drive

BCCEN_w109_LFBootloading.m: During the time of bootloading all LF functionality is disabled - location, authentication

BCCEN_w110_LFAntErrorPassenger.d: The exact location of the key cannot be determined. Since the center and left antenna are most important for passive entry, these functions will probably still work. There is a chance that functions a

BCCEN_w111_LFAntErrorFront.d: The exact location of the key cannot be determined. Since the center and left antenna are most important for passive entry, these functions will probably still work. There is a chance that functions a

BCCEN_w112_LFAntErrorRear.d: The exact location of the key cannot be determined. Since the center and left antenna are most important for passive entry, these functions will probably still work. There is a chance that functions a

BCCEN_w113_LFAntErrorCenter.d: The center antenna is responsible for all key fob authentication processes. Without the center console antenna, auto-present doors, key authentication and dead key fob functionality are not available.

BCCEN_w114_LFAntErrorDriver.d: Auto-present of the driver door will not function. Be aware that Auto-close still works because the vehicle doesn't need to know the exact location of the key for that. If the key is out of range of a

BCCEN_w116_SeeYouHomeNoResponse.m: See you home lighting not functioning properly

BCCEN_w125_immobKeyNotGenerated.d: This alert should only be present for virgin central units that have not yet had the immobilizer pair routine run.

BCCEN_w126_tooFewKeysPaired.m: Vehicle has fewer than 2 keys paired

BCFALCD_a001_WatchdogReset.d: Warns if the processor has experienced a reset due to watchdog reset.
BCFALCD_a001_WatchdogReset.m: Controller is MIA for some time.

BCFALCD_a010_ExtSupplyVoltError.d: The vehicle controller detects the supply voltage from 1 or more components is outside of the expected range.
BCFALCD_a010_ExtSupplyVoltError.m: Features depending on the vehicle controller functionality may be unavailable or may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a015_NVMMError.d: An issue is indicated from the non-volatile memory manager.
BCFALCD_a015_NVMMError.m: Non-volatile memory records may be incorrect or missing.

BCFALCD_a034_FalconOpenAtSpeed.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected a falcon door is open and the vehicle speed exceeds 0.5KPH.

BCFALCD_a036_doorUSSInvaliddist.d: Door outward ultrasonic sensor (USS) reports an invalid obstacle distance.
BCFALCD_a036_doorUSSInvaliddist.m: Door USS could be reporting false objects.

BCFALCD_a037_doorUSSPossibleFalseObjectD.d: The rear door's outward ultrasonic sensor is detecting an obstacle at a very close distance for an extended period of time, likely indicating a false object detection.

BCFALCD_a047_roofUSSInvaliddist.d: Roof USS reports an invalid obstacle distance.
BCFALCD_a047_roofUSSInvaliddist.m: Roof USS could be reporting false objects.

BCFALCD_a050_sideConfigError.d: The body controller detects a side configuration error, which may prevent the Falcon door from opening or closing.
BCFALCD_a050_sideConfigError.m: The Falcon door may not open or close.

BCFALCD_a051_BlockedDoorRequest.d: Same successive door request received wihtin short time frame, see alert payload for more information.

BCFALCD_a054_USS_RHSnsIntrnlFail.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an internal condition affecting the passenger-side USS.
BCFALCD_a054_USS_RHSnsIntrnlFail.m: The passenger-side USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a055_USS_RHLinCommError.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects a condition affecting the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) communication with the passenger-side USS.
BCFALCD_a055_USS_RHLinCommError.m: The passenger-side USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a059_inputResistanceHigh.d: A large voltage drop is detected in the input power to the vehicle controller, indicating the resistance on the input power feed to the vehicle controller is higher than expected.
BCFALCD_a059_inputResistanceHigh.m: The vehicle controller may be unavailable due to lack of adequate input power, limiting vehicle functionality or making the vehicle inoperable, with a

BCFALCD_a063_SwitchFault.d: A condition affecting switch functionality has been detected.
BCFALCD_a063_SwitchFault.m: The switch may not function or may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.d: reseting the controller should fix the issue.
BCFALCD_a079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.m: controller may have behaviors that correspond with the wrong side of the car

BCFALCD_a083_FalconPinchFault.d: The Falcon door controller detected a condition affecting the functionality of one or both of the pinch strips.
BCFALCD_a083_FalconPinchFault.m: The Falcon door pinch strips may not perform as designed.

BCFALCD_a086_CalibrationRequired.d: The Falcon door needs to be calibrated, which can be done by holding the B pillar close switch until ~2 seconds after door is latched.
BCFALCD_a086_CalibrationRequired.m: Uncalibrated doors will not respond to movement commands and triple beep.

BCFALCD_a087_UpperSagPrevention.d: Upper door section struts were actuated to prevent the door from sagging (lowering).
BCFALCD_a087_UpperSagPrevention.m: If this occurs on every door opening, it may indicate a strut condition.

BCFALCD_a089_UnexpectedStop.d: The Falcon door controller detects the door stops unexpectedly while moving to the requested position.
BCFALCD_a089_UnexpectedStop.m: The Falcon door will not reach the requested position.

BCFALCD_a092_movementPrevented.d: The Falcon door controller detects requested movement for the door is rejected.
BCFALCD_a092_movementPrevented.m: The Falcon door will not reach the requested position.

BCFALCD_a094_ObstacleOverride.d: Falcon door obstacle detection overriden by user.
BCFALCD_a094_ObstacleOverride.m: Falcon door movement will ignore most obstacle sensor input and door motion will be slower.

BCFALCD_a095_UnexpectLatchClosed.d: Falcon door latch reported closed while the Falcon door is open and not detected near the closed position.
BCFALCD_a095_UnexpectLatchClosed.m: Falcon door will stop and loose its calibration.

BCFALCD_a096_UnexpectedDuty.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected actuators are being driven with an unexpected duty cycle.
BCFALCD_a096_UnexpectedDuty.m: Continued abnormality puts actuators at risk of overheating, potentially damaging components and reducing lifespan.

BCFALCD_a098_LatchCinchRelease.d: Latch cinch has timed out. Releasing the latch to prevent latch hardware fault.
BCFALCD_a098_LatchCinchRelease.m: Latch cinching failed, new door close attempt required to close the door

BCFALCD_a112_LatchEntryTimeout.d: Falcon door latch entry actuation timed out.
BCFALCD_a112_LatchEntryTimeout.m: The Falcon door will not close automatically.

BCFALCD_a121_DoorUSSMIA.d: Falcon door ultrasonic sensor (USS) is not communicating with the USS controller on the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) bus.
BCFALCD_a121_DoorUSSMIA.m: Falcon door obstacle detection and other features relying on ultrasonic sensor data will not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a122_RoofUSSMIA.d: Falcon roof ultrasonic sensor (USS) is not communicating with the USS controller on the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) bus.
BCFALCD_a122_RoofUSSMIA.m: Falcon door obstacle detection and other features relying on ultrasonic sensor data will not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a128_falconMoveRequestInhibited.d: An interior Falcon door move request is inhibited by Autopilot because of a passing pedestrian, vehicle or cyclist. Pressing the interior button a second time will allow the door to move.

BCFALCD_a129_interiorDoorRequestInhibite.d: An interior Falcon door open request is inhibited by Autopilot because of a passing pedestrian, vehicle or cyclist. Pressing the interior open button a second time will allow the door to open.

BCFALCD_a130_upperFrontCurError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect. Note: current in this measurement is absolute (sign doesn't matter).
BCFALCD_a130_upperFrontCurError.m: Current differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a131_upperRearCurError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect. Note: current in this measurement is absolute (sign doesn't matter).
BCFALCD_a131_upperRearCurError.m: Current differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a132_lowerFrontCurError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect. Note: current in this measurement is absolute (sign doesn't matter).
BCFALCD_a132_lowerFrontCurError.m: Current differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a133_lowerRearCurError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect. Note: current in this measurement is absolute (sign doesn't matter).
BCFALCD_a133_lowerRearCurError.m: Current differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a140_upperFrontPosError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect.
BCFALCD_a140_upperFrontPosError.m: Position differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a141_upperRearPosError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect.
BCFALCD_a141_upperRearPosError.m: Position differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a142_lowerFrontPosError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect.
BCFALCD_a142_lowerFrontPosError.m: Position differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a143_lowerRearPosError.d: This alert is our best guess at which actuator is problematic, but this could be incorrect.
BCFALCD_a143_lowerRearPosError.m: Position differences could indicate damaged, miswired, or unplugged actuators

BCFALCD_a144_upperFrontCurLimit.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the upper front actuator experienced a current limit surge lasting for over 100 milliseconds. The electric current flowing through the motor surpassed norm
BCFALCD_a144_upperFrontCurLimit.m: Actuator may experience reduced performance if operating past its current limit for prolonged periods of time.

BCFALCD_a145_upperRearCurLimit.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the upper rear actuator experienced a current limit surge lasting for over 100 milliseconds. The electric current flowing through the motor surpassed norma
BCFALCD_a145_upperRearCurLimit.m: Actuator may experience reduced performance if operating past its current limit for prolonged periods of time.

BCFALCD_a146_lowerFrontCurLimit.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the lower front actuator current limit has been surpassed for more than 100 milliseconds. The electric current flowing through the motor surpassed normal o
BCFALCD_a146_lowerFrontCurLimit.m: Actuator may experience reduced performance if operating past its current limit for prolonged periods of time.

BCFALCD_a147_lowerRearCurLimit.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected lower rear actuator current limit has been surpassed for more than 100 milliseconds. The electric current flowing through the motor surpassed normal operat
BCFALCD_a147_lowerRearCurLimit.m: Actuator may experience reduced performance if operating past its current limit for prolonged periods of time.

BCFALCD_a160_IMUObstacleDetected.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) has detected an obstacle and stopped door motion.

BCFALCD_a165_PinchObsDetected.d: One of the two Falcon pinch strip sensor was pressed and stopped the door movement.
BCFALCD_a165_PinchObsDetected.m: Falcon door will either stop if in motion, or reject movement request if static.

BCFALCD_a170_upperFrontOverTemp.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has estimated section actuator temperatures exceeding 100.0 deg C (212 deg F).
BCFALCD_a170_upperFrontOverTemp.m: Continued movement of the door while the section actuator is overheating can cause damage and lead to automatic shutdown of door motion. Movement comm

BCFALCD_a171_upperRearOverTemp.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has estimated section actuator temperatures exceeding 100.0 deg C (212 deg F).
BCFALCD_a171_upperRearOverTemp.m: Continued movement of the door while the section actuator is overheating can cause damage and lead to automatic shutdown of door motion. Movement comm

BCFALCD_a172_lowerFrontOverTemp.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has estimated section actuator temperatures exceeding 100.0 deg C (212 deg F).
BCFALCD_a172_lowerFrontOverTemp.m: Continued movement of the door while the section actuator is overheating can cause damage and lead to automatic shutdown of door motion. Movement comm

BCFALCD_a173_lowerRearOverTemp.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has estimated section actuator temperatures exceeding 100.0 deg C (212 deg F).
BCFALCD_a173_lowerRearOverTemp.m: Continued movement of the door while the section actuator is overheating can cause damage and lead to automatic shutdown of door motion. Movement comm

BCFALCD_a177_USSDoorResonanceErr.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the resonance period reported by the ultrasonic sensor is out of bounds.
BCFALCD_a177_USSDoorResonanceErr.m: The ultrasonic sensor may be unable to detect obstacles in the door's path.

BCFALCD_a180_UpperCurrentThresh.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the electrical current limit for the upper actuator has been exceeded.
BCFALCD_a180_UpperCurrentThresh.m: An obstruction may be impacting functionality or a strut may be damaged.

BCFALCD_a181_UpperTrajPosition.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the door section is deviating by 6 degrees away from its designated trajectory target path.
BCFALCD_a181_UpperTrajPosition.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCD_a182_UpperTrajSpeed.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected a deviation from the desired speed of the door section during opening at a rate of 9 degrees per second or upon closure at a speed of 7 degrees per second.
BCFALCD_a182_UpperTrajSpeed.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCD_a184_LowerCurrentThresh.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the electrical current limit for the lower actuator's electrical current has been exceeded.
BCFALCD_a184_LowerCurrentThresh.m: An obstruction may be impacting functionality or a strut may be damaged.

BCFALCD_a185_LowerTrajPosition.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the door section is deviating by 6 degrees away from its designated trajectory target path.
BCFALCD_a185_LowerTrajPosition.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCD_a186_LowerTrajSpeed.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected a deviation from the desired speed of the door section during opening at a rate of 9 degrees per second or upon closure at a speed of 7 degrees per second.
BCFALCD_a186_LowerTrajSpeed.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCD_a188_doorMvmtUSSNoChange.d: The rear door's ultrasonic sensor has detected an object that is likely to be a false positive, and that presumably does not exist.
BCFALCD_a188_doorMvmtUSSNoChange.m: The rear door might not move, or might stop unexpectedly while opening.

BCFALCD_a193_openToFitUsed.d: Falcon door is using the open to fit state due to a detected obstacle limiting the door opening space. See alert payload for more information on the limit source.
BCFALCD_a193_openToFitUsed.m: The Falcon door will not open fully and adjust its maximum opening position based on detected obstacles.

BCFALCD_a196_openToFitDueToParkSensor.d: Door is using the open to fit state due to the park sensors detecting obstacles that limits the door opening space.
BCFALCD_a196_openToFitDueToParkSensor.m: The Falcon door will not open fully and adjust its maximum opening position based on detected obstacles.

BCFALCD_a259_USS_LHSnsIntrnlFail.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an internal condition affecting the driver-side USS.
BCFALCD_a259_USS_LHSnsIntrnlFail.m: The driver-side USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a260_USS_LHLinCommError.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects a condition affecting the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) communication with the driver-side USS.
BCFALCD_a260_USS_LHLinCommError.m: The driver-side USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a261_USS_RoofOverTemp.d: CThe Falcon Door Roof Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) over temperature condition detected.
BCFALCD_a261_USS_RoofOverTemp.m: Persistent over temperature conditions in roof USS may result in inaccurate proximity detection, damage to USS, and impaired Falcon Door functionality

BCFALCD_a262_USS_RoofSnsIntFail.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an internal condition affecting the roof USS.
BCFALCD_a262_USS_RoofSnsIntFail.m: The roof USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a263_USS_RoofLinComError.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects a condition affecting the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) communication with the roof USS.
BCFALCD_a263_USS_RoofLinComError.m: The roof USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a264_USS_ECUUnderVoltage.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an undervoltage condition.
BCFALCD_a264_USS_ECUUnderVoltage.m: The USS controller and sensors may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a265_USS_ECUOverVoltage.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an over voltage condition.
BCFALCD_a265_USS_ECUOverVoltage.m: The USS controller and sensors may not function as expected.

BCFALCD_a266_USS_SnsSupplyError.d: An anomaly pertaining to power delivery has been detected in the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor's (USS) circuitry.
BCFALCD_a266_USS_SnsSupplyError.m: A supply error could result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_a267_USS_ECUIntrnlFailur.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has experienced an internal error.
BCFALCD_a267_USS_ECUIntrnlFailur.m: An internal error could result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_a268_USS_calibCRCFailure.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) calibration has experienced a Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) failure condition.
BCFALCD_a268_USS_calibCRCFailure.m: A CRC failure could result in intermittent, total loss, or reduced functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_a269_USS_calibMissing.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) calibration settings are missing.
BCFALCD_a269_USS_calibMissing.m: Missing calibration settings could result in intermittent, total loss, or reduced functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_a270_USS_canBusOff.d: The CAN bus connected to the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) has been detected as offline.
BCFALCD_a270_USS_canBusOff.m: An offline CAN bus can lead to intermittent faltering, complete failure, or decreased performance in ultrasonic sensor functionality.

BCFALCD_a271_USS_hwWatchdogFail.d: Failure of the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) Hardware Watchdog Timer detected.
BCFALCD_a271_USS_hwWatchdogFail.m: Timer failure may cause erratic behavior, total failure, or diminished performance in USS operations.

BCFALCD_a272_USS_LHInwardLINerr.d: Communication through Local Interconnect Network (LIN) with the left-side inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) in the Falcon Door System has been disconnected or interrupted.
BCFALCD_a272_USS_LHInwardLINerr.m: Loss of LIN communication can impact Falcon Door system functionality including obstructive awareness, motion management, and protection mechanisms.

BCFALCD_a273_USS_RHInwardLINerr.d: Communication through Local Interconnect Network (LIN) with the right-side inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) in the Falcon Door System has been disconnected or interrupted.
BCFALCD_a273_USS_RHInwardLINerr.m: Loss of LIN communication can impact Falcon Door system functionality including obstructive awareness, motion management, and protection mechanisms.

BCFALCD_a274_USS_LHInwardIntFail.d: The inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) located on the left Falcon Door has encountered a hardware failure.
BCFALCD_a274_USS_LHInwardIntFail.m: Malfunctioning hardware in the left Falcon Door's internal ultrasonic sensor can result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_a275_USS_RHInwardIntFail.d: The inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) located on the right Falcon Door has encountered a hardware failure.
BCFALCD_a275_USS_RHInwardIntFail.m: Malfunctioning hardware in the right Falcon Door's internal ultrasonic sensor can result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_a276_unexpectedUSSStatus.d: The ultrasonic controller used by the Falcon doors is reported as OFF, or as FAULTED, while it is expected to be ON.
BCFALCD_a276_unexpectedUSSStatus.m: The Falcon door ultrasonic sensors might not be powered on, which could limit the door obstacle detection capabilities

BCFALCD_w001_WatchdogReset.d: This message contains separate signals for each watchdog reset source.
BCFALCD_w001_WatchdogReset.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w002_PowerLossReset.d: Undervoltage detection by the CPU can set this alert.
BCFALCD_w002_PowerLossReset.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w003_SWAssertion.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w004_CANMIA.d: The SBC CAN transceiver has faulted. Alert will be seen if the trasnceiver has recovered from the faulted condition.
BCFALCD_w004_CANMIA.m: Other ECUs may report this ECU as being MIA and behaviour will be impacted. This alert won't be seen until CAN communcation has returned.

BCFALCD_w005_CANHighError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w006_CANLowError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w007_LINTimeout.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w008_LIN2Error.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w009_LIN3Error.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w010_ExtSupplyVoltError.d: The electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the supply voltages from 1 or more components are out of range.

BCFALCD_w011_ECUSupplyVoltError.d: This message contains separate signals for Overvoltage and Undervoltage for each rail.
BCFALCD_w011_ECUSupplyVoltError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w012_CPUReset.m: Potential issue with hardware or software caused the reset.

BCFALCD_w013_Shared13.d: This alert is used to create the template for the raw alert log. Double check before this alert is used for something else.
BCFALCD_w013_Shared13.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w014_BridgeFault.d: Bridge faults are most likely due to configuration error.

BCFALCD_w066_USS_SnsSupplyError.d: An anomaly pertaining to power delivery has been detected in the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor's (USS) circuitry.
BCFALCD_w066_USS_SnsSupplyError.m: A supply error could result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_w067_USS_ECUIntrnlFailur.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has experienced an internal error.
BCFALCD_w067_USS_ECUIntrnlFailur.m: An internal error could result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_w068_USS_calibCRCFailure.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) calibration has experienced a Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) failure condition.
BCFALCD_w068_USS_calibCRCFailure.m: A CRC failure could result in intermittent, total loss, or reduced functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_w069_USS_calibMissing.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) calibration settings are missing.
BCFALCD_w069_USS_calibMissing.m: Missing calibration settings could result in intermittent, total loss, or reduced functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_w070_USS_canBusOff.d: The CAN bus connected to the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) has been detected as offline.
BCFALCD_w070_USS_canBusOff.m: An offline CAN bus can lead to intermittent faltering, complete failure, or decreased performance in ultrasonic sensor functionality.

BCFALCD_w071_USS_hwWatchdogFail.d: Failure of the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) Hardware Watchdog Timer detected.
BCFALCD_w071_USS_hwWatchdogFail.m: Timer failure may cause erratic behavior, total failure, or diminished performance in USS operations.

BCFALCD_w072_USS_LHInwardLINerr.d: Communication through Local Interconnect Network (LIN) with the left-side inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) in the Falcon Door System has been disconnected or interrupted.
BCFALCD_w072_USS_LHInwardLINerr.m: Loss of LIN communication can impact Falcon Door system functionality including obstructive awareness, motion management, and protection mechanisms.

BCFALCD_w073_USS_RHInwardLINerr.d: Communication through Local Interconnect Network (LIN) with the right-side inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) in the Falcon Door System has been disconnected or interrupted.
BCFALCD_w073_USS_RHInwardLINerr.m: Loss of LIN communication can impact Falcon Door system functionality including obstructive awareness, motion management, and protection mechanisms.

BCFALCD_w074_USS_LHInwardIntFail.d: The inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) located on the left Falcon Door has encountered a hardware failure.
BCFALCD_w074_USS_LHInwardIntFail.m: Malfunctioning hardware in the left Falcon Door's internal ultrasonic sensor can result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_w075_USS_RHInwardIntFail.d: The inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) located on the right Falcon Door has encountered a hardware failure.
BCFALCD_w075_USS_RHInwardIntFail.m: Malfunctioning hardware in the right Falcon Door's internal ultrasonic sensor can result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCD_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.d: reseting the controller should fix the issue.
BCFALCD_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.m: controller may have behaviors that correspond with the wrong side of the car

BCFALCD_w083_FalconPinchFault.d: The Falcon door controller detected a condition affecting the functionality of one or both of the pinch strips.
BCFALCD_w083_FalconPinchFault.m: The Falcon door pinch strips may not perform as designed.

BCFALCD_w086_CalibrationRequired.d: The Falcon door needs to be calibrated, which can be done by holding the B pillar close switch until ~2 seconds after door is latched.
BCFALCD_w086_CalibrationRequired.m: Uncalibrated doors will not respond to movement commands and triple beep.

BCFALCD_w087_UpperSagPrevention.d: Firmware compensation for actuator brakes out of spec.
BCFALCD_w087_UpperSagPrevention.m: If this occurs often, struts should be replaced

BCFALCD_w089_UnexpectedStop.d: The Falcon door controller detects the door stops unexpectedly while moving to the requested position.
BCFALCD_w089_UnexpectedStop.m: The Falcon door will not reach the requested position.

BCFALCD_w090_CrashUnlockError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCD_w091_CrashStateMismatch.m: If any crash signal is active, crash behavior is active

BCFALCD_w092_movementPrevented.d: The Falcon door controller detects requested movement for the door is rejected.

BCFALCD_w093_OTPError.m: Genealogy information may not be read.

BCFALCD_w094_ObstacleOverride.d: Falcon door obstacle detection overriden by user.
BCFALCD_w094_ObstacleOverride.m: Falcon door movement will ignore most obstacle sensor input and door motion will be slower.

BCFALCD_w095_UnexpectLatchClosed.d: Falcon door latch reported closed while the Falcon door is open and not detected near the closed position.
BCFALCD_w095_UnexpectLatchClosed.m: Falcon door will stop and loose its calibration.

BCFALCD_w096_UnexpectedDuty.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected actuators are being driven with an unexpected duty cycle.
BCFALCD_w096_UnexpectedDuty.m: Continued abnormality puts actuators at risk of overheating, potentially damaging components and reducing lifespan.

BCFALCD_w098_LatchCinchRelease.d: Latch cinch has timed out. Releasing the latch to prevent latch hardware fault.
BCFALCD_w098_LatchCinchRelease.m: Latch cinching failed, new door close attempt required to close the door

BCFALCD_w101_VbatUnplugged.m: door limited to 1 cycle per minute

BCFALCD_w105_ECUDebugMode.m: ECU Debug Jumper will disable SBC watchdog, preventing external watchdog reset protection.

BCFALCP_a063_SwitchFault.d: A condition affecting switch functionality has been detected.

BCFALCP_a079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.d: reseting the controller should fix the issue.

BCFALCP_a086_CalibrationRequired.m: Uncalibrated doors will not respond to movement commands and triple beep.

BCFALCP_a171_upperRearOverTemp.m: Continued movement of the door while the section actuator is overheating can cause damage and lead to automatic shutdown of door motion. Movement comm

BCFALCP_a177_USSDoorResonanceErr.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the resonance period reported by the ultrasonic sensor is out of bounds.
BCFALCP_a177_USSDoorResonanceErr.m: The ultrasonic sensor may be unable to detect obstacles in the door's path.

BCFALCP_a180_UpperCurrentThresh.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the electrical current limit for the upper actuator has been exceeded.
BCFALCP_a180_UpperCurrentThresh.m: An obstruction may be impacting functionality or a strut may be damaged.

BCFALCP_a181_UpperTrajPosition.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the door section is deviating by 6 degrees away from its designated trajectory target path.
BCFALCP_a181_UpperTrajPosition.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCP_a182_UpperTrajSpeed.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected a deviation from the desired speed of the door section during opening at a rate of 9 degrees per second or upon closure at a speed of 7 degrees per second.
BCFALCP_a182_UpperTrajSpeed.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCP_a184_LowerCurrentThresh.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the electrical current limit for the lower actuator's electrical current has been exceeded.
BCFALCP_a184_LowerCurrentThresh.m: An obstruction may be impacting functionality or a strut may be damaged.

BCFALCP_a185_LowerTrajPosition.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected the door section is deviating by 6 degrees away from its designated trajectory target path.
BCFALCP_a185_LowerTrajPosition.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCP_a186_LowerTrajSpeed.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected a deviation from the desired speed of the door section during opening at a rate of 9 degrees per second or upon closure at a speed of 7 degrees per second.
BCFALCP_a186_LowerTrajSpeed.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCP_a188_doorMvmtUSSNoChange.d: The rear door's ultrasonic sensor has detected an object that is likely to be a false positive, and that presumably does not exist.
BCFALCP_a188_doorMvmtUSSNoChange.m: The rear door might not move, or might stop unexpectedly while opening.

BCFALCP_a193_openToFitUsed.d: Falcon door is using the open to fit state due to a detected obstacle limiting the door opening space. See alert payload for more information on the limit source.
BCFALCP_a193_openToFitUsed.m: The Falcon door will not open fully and adjust its maximum opening position based on detected obstacles.

BCFALCP_a196_openToFitDueToParkSensor.d: Door is using the open to fit state due to the park sensors detecting obstacles that limits the door opening space.
BCFALCP_a196_openToFitDueToParkSensor.m: The Falcon door will not open fully and adjust its maximum opening position based on detected obstacles.

BCFALCP_a259_USS_LHSnsIntrnlFail.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an internal condition affecting the driver-side USS.
BCFALCP_a259_USS_LHSnsIntrnlFail.m: The driver-side USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCP_a260_USS_LHLinCommError.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects a condition affecting the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) communication with the driver-side USS.
BCFALCP_a260_USS_LHLinCommError.m: The driver-side USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCP_a261_USS_RoofOverTemp.d: CThe Falcon Door Roof Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) over temperature condition detected.
BCFALCP_a261_USS_RoofOverTemp.m: Persistent over temperature conditions in roof USS may result in inaccurate proximity detection, damage to USS, and impaired Falcon Door functionality

BCFALCP_a262_USS_RoofSnsIntFail.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an internal condition affecting the roof USS.
BCFALCP_a262_USS_RoofSnsIntFail.m: The roof USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCP_a263_USS_RoofLinComError.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects a condition affecting the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) communication with the roof USS.
BCFALCP_a263_USS_RoofLinComError.m: The roof USS may not function as expected.

BCFALCP_a264_USS_ECUUnderVoltage.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an undervoltage condition.
BCFALCP_a264_USS_ECUUnderVoltage.m: The USS controller and sensors may not function as expected.

BCFALCP_a265_USS_ECUOverVoltage.d: The ultrasonic sensor (USS) controller detects an over voltage condition.
BCFALCP_a265_USS_ECUOverVoltage.m: The USS controller and sensors may not function as expected.

BCFALCP_a266_USS_SnsSupplyError.d: An anomaly pertaining to power delivery has been detected in the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor's (USS) circuitry.
BCFALCP_a266_USS_SnsSupplyError.m: A supply error could result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCP_a267_USS_ECUIntrnlFailur.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has experienced an internal error.
BCFALCP_a267_USS_ECUIntrnlFailur.m: An internal error could result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCP_a268_USS_calibCRCFailure.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) calibration has experienced a Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) failure condition.
BCFALCP_a268_USS_calibCRCFailure.m: A CRC failure could result in intermittent, total loss, or reduced functionality of the USS.

BCFALCP_a269_USS_calibMissing.d: The Falcon Door Ultra-Sonic Sensor (USS) calibration settings are missing.
BCFALCP_a269_USS_calibMissing.m: Missing calibration settings could result in intermittent, total loss, or reduced functionality of the USS.

BCFALCP_a270_USS_canBusOff.d: The CAN bus connected to the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) has been detected as offline.
BCFALCP_a270_USS_canBusOff.m: An offline CAN bus can lead to intermittent faltering, complete failure, or decreased performance in ultrasonic sensor functionality.

BCFALCP_a271_USS_hwWatchdogFail.d: Failure of the Falcon Door Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) Hardware Watchdog Timer detected.
BCFALCP_a271_USS_hwWatchdogFail.m: Timer failure may cause erratic behavior, total failure, or diminished performance in USS operations.

BCFALCP_a272_USS_LHInwardLINerr.d: Communication through Local Interconnect Network (LIN) with the left-side inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) in the Falcon Door System has been disconnected or interrupted.
BCFALCP_a272_USS_LHInwardLINerr.m: Loss of LIN communication can impact Falcon Door system functionality including obstructive awareness, motion management, and protection mechanisms.

BCFALCP_a273_USS_RHInwardLINerr.d: Communication through Local Interconnect Network (LIN) with the right-side inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) in the Falcon Door System has been disconnected or interrupted.
BCFALCP_a273_USS_RHInwardLINerr.m: Loss of LIN communication can impact Falcon Door system functionality including obstructive awareness, motion management, and protection mechanisms.

BCFALCP_a274_USS_LHInwardIntFail.d: The inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) located on the left Falcon Door has encountered a hardware failure.
BCFALCP_a274_USS_LHInwardIntFail.m: Malfunctioning hardware in the left Falcon Door's internal ultrasonic sensor can result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCP_a275_USS_RHInwardIntFail.d: The inward-facing Ultrasonic Sensor (USS) located on the right Falcon Door has encountered a hardware failure.
BCFALCP_a275_USS_RHInwardIntFail.m: Malfunctioning hardware in the right Falcon Door's internal ultrasonic sensor can result in intermittent or total loss of functionality of the USS.

BCFALCP_a276_unexpectedUSSStatus.d: The ultrasonic controller used by the Falcon doors is reported as OFF, or as FAULTED, while it is expected to be ON.
BCFALCP_a276_unexpectedUSSStatus.m: The Falcon door ultrasonic sensors might not be powered on, which could limit the door obstacle detection capabilities

BCFALCP_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.m: controller may have behaviors that correspond with the wrong side of the car

BCFALCP_w083_FalconPinchFault.d: The Falcon door controller detected a condition affecting the functionality of one or both of the pinch strips.
BCFALCP_w083_FalconPinchFault.m: The Falcon door pinch strips may not perform as designed.

BCFALCP_w086_CalibrationRequired.d: The Falcon door needs to be calibrated, which can be done by holding the B pillar close switch until ~2 seconds after door is latched.
BCFALCP_w086_CalibrationRequired.m: Uncalibrated doors will not respond to movement commands and triple beep.

BCFALCP_w087_UpperSagPrevention.d: Firmware compensation for actuator brakes out of spec.
BCFALCP_w087_UpperSagPrevention.m: If this occurs often, struts should be replaced

BCFALCP_w089_UnexpectedStop.d: The Falcon door controller detects the door stops unexpectedly while moving to the requested position.
BCFALCP_w089_UnexpectedStop.m: The Falcon door will not reach the requested position.

BCFALCP_w090_CrashUnlockError.m: These are the repercussions of the alert being set

BCFALCP_w091_CrashStateMismatch.m: If any crash signal is active, crash behavior is active

BCFALCP_w092_movementPrevented.d: The Falcon door controller detects requested movement for the door is rejected.
BCFALCP_w092_movementPrevented.m: The Falcon door will not reach the requested position.

BCFALCP_w093_OTPError.m: Genealogy information may not be read.

BCFALCP_w094_ObstacleOverride.d: Falcon door obstacle detection overriden by user.
BCFALCP_w094_ObstacleOverride.m: Falcon door movement will ignore most obstacle sensor input and door motion will be slower.

BCFALCP_w095_UnexpectLatchClosed.d: Falcon door latch reported closed while the Falcon door is open and not detected near the closed position.
BCFALCP_w095_UnexpectLatchClosed.m: Falcon door will stop and loose its calibration.

BCFALCP_w096_UnexpectedDuty.d: The Falcon Door Body Controller has detected actuators are being driven with an unexpected duty cycle.
BCFALCP_w096_UnexpectedDuty.m: Continued abnormality puts actuators at risk of overheating, potentially damaging components and reducing lifespan.

BCFALCP_w098_LatchCinchRelease.d: Latch cinch has timed out. Releasing the latch to prevent latch hardware fault.
BCFALCP_w098_LatchCinchRelease.m: Latch cinching failed, new door close attempt required to close the door

BCFALCP_w101_VbatUnplugged.m: door limited to 1 cycle per minute

BCFALCP_w105_ECUDebugMode.d: Production vehicle should not have Debug Jumper in place. Remove jumper from ECU.
BCFALCP_w105_ECUDebugMode.m: ECU Debug Jumper will disable SBC watchdog, preventing external watchdog reset protection.

BCFALCP_w106_HighCPULoad.d: Log contains CPU load for each task.
BCFALCP_w106_HighCPULoad.m: High CPU load can indicate ineffecient code.

BCFALCP_w107_HighStackUsage.d: Log contains stack usage for each task.
BCFALCP_w107_HighStackUsage.m: Stack overflow can occur if there is not enough stack space.

BCFALCP_w112_LatchEntryTimeout.m: The door hasn't successfully closed, but the user would have expected it to

BCFALCP_w120_IMUMIA.d: IMU is on the rear door controller, so IMU data comes over CAN.
BCFALCP_w120_IMUMIA.m: IMU obstacle detection will not be active

BCFALCP_w121_DoorUSSMIA.d: Falcon door ultrasonic sensor (USS) is not communicating with the USS controller on the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) bus.
BCFALCP_w121_DoorUSSMIA.m: Falcon door obstacle detection and other features relying on ultrasonic sensor data will not function as expected.

BCFALCP_w122_RoofUSSMIA.d: Falcon roof ultrasonic sensor (USS) is not communicating with the USS controller on the Local Interconnect Network (LIN) bus.

BCFALCP_w186_LowerTrajSpeed.m: Multiple occurrences suggest possible hardware conditions in the door or interference from foreign objects and debris affecting door functionality.

BCFALCP_w188_doorMvmtUSSNoChange.m: The rear door might not move, or might stop unexpectedly while opening.

BCFALCP_w192_eFuseNotReady.m: Doors may not start or may stop unexpectedly

BCFDM_a001_WatchdogReset.d: Warns if the processor has experienced a reset due to watchdog reset.
BCFDM_a001_WatchdogReset.m: Controller is MIA for some time.

BCFDM_a010_ExtSupplyVoltError.d: The vehicle controller detects the supply voltage from 1 or more components is outside of the expected range.
BCFDM_a010_ExtSupplyVoltError.m: Features depending on the vehicle controller functionality may be unavailable or may not function as expected.

BCFDM_a015_NVMMError.d: An issue is indicated from the non-volatile memory manager.
BCFDM_a015_NVMMError.m: Non-volatile memory records may be incorrect or missing.

BCFDM_a059_inputResistanceHigh.d: A large voltage drop is detected in the input power to the vehicle controller, indicating the resistance on the input power feed to the vehicle controller is higher than expected.
BCFDM_a059_inputResistanceHigh.m: The vehicle controller may be unavailable due to lack of adequate input power, limiting vehicle functionality or making the vehicle inoperable, with a

BCFDM_a063_SwitchFault.d: A condition affecting switch functionality has been detected.
BCFDM_a063_SwitchFault.m: The switch may not function or may not function as expected.

BCFDM_a070_LatchFailedToCinch.d: The door latch cinching actuation completed but the latch is not fully closed.
BCFDM_a070_LatchFailedToCinch.m: The door latch cinching function may not work as expected, requiring the door to be closed manually.

BCFDM_a071_LatchFailedToRelease.d: The door latch release actuation completed but the latch is not OPENED.
BCFDM_a071_LatchFailedToRelease.m: The door latch electrical release function may not work as expected.

BCFDM_a072_UnexpectLatchClosed.d: The door latch is closed while the door angle is outside the latch closing zone.
BCFDM_a072_UnexpectLatchClosed.m: The door latch was manually closed, or the door motor encoder is incorrectly reporting the door position.

BCFDM_a073_UnexpectLatchOpen.d: The door latch transitions to open state unexpectedly.
BCFDM_a073_UnexpectLatchOpen.m: The door latch switches may incorrectly report the door latch status.

BCFDM_a074_NoLatchEntryOnClose.d: The front door latch did not detect the striker after the door ramped up to engage the latch.
BCFDM_a074_NoLatchEntryOnClose.m: A condition may prevent the door from closing automatically or the door latch may have a sensing condition.

BCFDM_a075_DoorIceBreakerAttempt.d: The door ice breaker is requested to extend following unsuccessful latch release attempts.
BCFDM_a075_DoorIceBreakerAttempt.m: Door ice breaker will extend to attempt opening the door.

BCFDM_a076_DoorIceBreakerFailure.d: The door ice breaker extended but the door latch is not opened.
BCFDM_a076_DoorIceBreakerFailure.m: Door might not open due to the latch release actuator not working or an external factor blocking the door.

BCFDM_a078_DoorUnzeroed.d: The door zero position is cleared.
BCFDM_a078_DoorUnzeroed.m: The door power operation is disabled.

BCFDM_a081_LatchOpenInMotion.d: The door latch transitions to opened state while the vehicle is in motion.
BCFDM_a081_LatchOpenInMotion.m: Door latch may be opened while vehicle is in motion, or the door latch is incorrectly detected open.

BCFDM_a083_UnexpectedStop.d: The door stopped unexpectedly while opening or closing.
BCFDM_a083_UnexpectedStop.m: Door opening or closing actuation did not complete.

BCFDM_a109_DoorBrakeUncalibrated.d: The door motor brake is not calibrated and using default values.
BCFDM_a109_DoorBrakeUncalibrated.m: The door motor brake might not function as expected. Use Service action 'Write Actuator Brake Calibration' to add it.

BCFDM_a116_DoorConfigError.d: The door controller's left or right side configuration is invalid or unspecified.
BCFDM_a116_DoorConfigError.m: Door controller may not communicate and will not operate as expected. Run the correct 'Side Learn' action to configure it.

BCFDM_a126_MotorOvertemperature.d: Prevents doors from operating when door motors exceeds temperature threshold.
BCFDM_a126_MotorOvertemperature.m: The power door no longer operates

BCFDM_a130_interiorDoorRequestInhibited.d: An interior door release request is inhibited by Autopilot because of a passing pedestrian, vehicle or cyclist. Pressing the interior door release button a second time will allow the door to open.

BCFDM_a310_mirrorFoldStall.d: Mirror stalled unexpectedly while folding or unfolding.
BCFDM_a310_mirrorFoldStall.m: Mirror might be halfway between folded and unfolded

BCFDM_a316_mirrorPrematureFoldStall.d: A side-view mirror started a fold or unfold movement, but then stalled before finishing the movement. The mirror will attempt 1 retry to complete the movement.
BCFDM_a316_mirrorPrematureFoldStall.m: The mirror will stop for 400ms before attempting the retry. If the mirror unexpectedly stalls again during the retry, it may be left partially folded/

BCFDM_a324_mirrorHeatFault.d: Mirror heat undercurrent detected, indicating mirror heating may be limited or unavailable.
BCFDM_a324_mirrorHeatFault.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality

BCFDM_a482_NCV77XXFault.d: The driver integrated circuit (IC) experiences a condition affecting the operation of the loads controlled by the device.
BCFDM_a482_NCV77XXFault.m: Mirror actuations, mirror heating, mirror dimming, or other vehicle operations relying on the driver IC may be inoperative.

BCFDM_w058_DoorConfigError.m: The ECU may not respond to the desired signals and ECU should be reset

BCFDM_w063_LatchSwitchError.d: latch feedback switches are most likely harnessed incorrectly.
BCFDM_w063_LatchSwitchError.m: Door latch status is incorrectly reported and PFD operation becomes erratic

BCFDM_w065_ratchetingDetected.m: Worse user experience as the door

BCFDM_w066_falseLatchCinch.d: Not to be confused with a weak cinch. This alert does not imply problems with seal load or door alignment.
BCFDM_w066_falseLatchCinch.m: Front door may not be able to cinch closed

BCFDM_w067_missedShortDrop.d: The body controller will automatically re-issue a short-drop command if a failure to drop is detected.

BCFDM_w068_missedUndrop.d: The body controller will automatically re-issue an undrop command if a failure to undrop is detected.
BCFDM_w068_missedUndrop.m: Window undrop will be delayed by about 1000 ms as the door closes.

BCFDM_w069_LatchStatusIgnored.d: This alert probably indicates that a firmware bug was encountered. Resetting the front door body controller will temporarily resolve the issue.
BCFDM_w069_LatchStatusIgnored.m: Vehicle will not properly detect that the door is open or closing. May also trigger several other power front door alerts.

BCFDM_w074_NoLatchEntryOnClose.d: The Body Controller Front Driver Door (BCFDM) or Body Controller Front Passenger Door (BCFPM) detects latch is not fully closed after ramping.
BCFDM_w074_NoLatchEntryOnClose.m: Door will not close properly. If vehicle is locked, alarm may sound.

BCFDM_w077_LatchMechReleaseStuck.d: Often caused by a kinked release cable or damaged cable tubing.
BCFDM_w077_LatchMechReleaseStuck.m: Front door may not properly close

BCFDM_w078_DoorUnzeroed.d: The door zero position is cleared.
BCFDM_w078_DoorUnzeroed.m: The door power operation is disabled.

BCFDM_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.d: reseting the controller should fix the issue.
BCFDM_w079_DvrSideCfgMismatch.m: controller may have behaviors that correspond with the wrong side of the car

BCFDM_w080_CurrentDropOut.m: Actuator will temporarily not respond to being driven

BCFDM_w081_LatchOpenInMotion.d: This alert helps to indicate if the latch has water ingress which has damaged or caused undesired behaviors for the vehicle.
BCFDM_w081_LatchOpenInMotion.m: Door may be unlatched while vehicle is in motion.

BCFDM_w083_UnexpectedStop.d: The door stopped unexpectedly while opening or closing.
BCFDM_w083_UnexpectedStop.m: Door opening or closing actuation did not complete.
