CMPT_a001_eggright3MIA.d: EGGRIGHT3 node is Missing-in-Action (MIA) on THERMAL CAN bus.
CMPT_a001_eggright3MIA.m: Inverter may not execute EGGRIGHT3 commands.

CMPT_a004_12vUV.d: The 12V supply of the compressor is below the minimum expected value.
CMPT_a004_12vUV.m: This alert is for diagnostic purposes only. The compressor may reset or stop operating as a result of this undervoltage.

CMPT_a005_12vOV.d: The 12V supply of the compressor is above the maximum expected value.
CMPT_a005_12vOV.m: The compressor hardware may be damaged as a result of an overvoltage condition.

CMPT_a006_5vUV.d: The internal 5V supply has an undervoltage condition.
CMPT_a006_5vUV.m: The compressor may receive incorrect sensor readings.

CMPT_a007_5vOV.d: The internal 5V supply voltage of the compressor has an overvoltage condition.
CMPT_a007_5vOV.m: The compressor hardware may be damaged. Sensors may provide incorrect readings.

CMPT_a008_3v3UV.d: The internal 3V3 supply voltage is too low.
CMPT_a008_3v3UV.m: The compressor processor may shut down. Sensors may provide incorrect readings.

CMPT_a009_3v3OV.d: The internal 3V3 supply voltage has an overvoltage condition.
CMPT_a009_3v3OV.m: The compressor hardware may get damaged. Sensors may report incorrect values.

CMPT_a010_1v2UV.d: The internal 1V2 supply has an undervoltage condition.
CMPT_a010_1v2UV.m: The processor may reboot or stop operating if the condition worsens.

CMPT_a011_1v2OV.d: The internal 1V2 supply has an overvoltage condition.
CMPT_a011_1v2OV.m: The processor may stop operating if this condition continues or worsens.

CMPT_a012_gateDriverFault.d: The compressor high voltage powerstage gate drivers reported an electrical condition preventing their operation.

CMPT_a013_vcrightMIA.d: VCRIGHT node is Missing-in-Action (MIA) on THERMAL CAN bus.
CMPT_a013_vcrightMIA.m: Inverter may not execute VCRIGHT commands.

CMPT_a014_hvBusVoltageTooLow.d: The HV bus voltage is too low for the compressor to start.

CMPT_a015_overTempShutdown.d: At least one of the temperature sensors is detecting an overtemperature. Compressor will be shutting down.

CMPT_a016_failedStart.d: The HV Compressor failed to start and reach the target speed.
CMPT_a016_failedStart.m: The HV Compressor will not produce the required pressure for the duration of this alert.

CMPT_a017_failedStartRepeated.d: High Voltage (HV) compressor does not start multiple times and does not reach the target speed.
CMPT_a017_failedStartRepeated.m: Loss of HVAC heating and cooling. Compressor does not operate for the remainder of the power cycle.

CMPT_a018_overTempWarning.d: At least one of the temperature sensors has reached the overtemp warning threshold.
CMPT_a018_overTempWarning.m: Compressor may shut down if temperature sensors continue to rise in temperature.

CMPT_a019_phaseOverCurrentWarning.d: The motor is operating at high phase currents and risks overheating.

CMPT_a020_gateDrivePowerSupplyFaulted.d: At least one of the gate driver power supplies had to shut down due to an electrical condition.
CMPT_a020_gateDrivePowerSupplyFaulted.m: Compressor will not operate if power to any gate driver is insufficient.

CMPT_a023_lossOfMotorControl.d: The HV Compressor had a loss of motor control causing the compressor to stop spinning.

CMPT_a024_gateDrivePowerSupplyRetriesExp.d: One of the gate driver power supplies expired its available retries.
CMPT_a024_gateDrivePowerSupplyRetriesExp.m: The HV compressor will stop operating as power to the corresponding gate driver becomes insufficient.

CMPT_a025_bmsMIA.d: BMS node is Missing-in-Action (MIA) on THERMAL CAN bus.
CMPT_a025_bmsMIA.m: Inverter may not execute BMS commands.

CMPT_a026_vcsuperMIA.d: One or more of the messages the vehicle controller receives periodically from the supermanifold vehicle controller (VCSUPER) is not received, indicating the VCSUPER may be unavailable (MIA).
CMPT_a026_vcsuperMIA.m: Vehicle functions that depend on the VCSUPER may be unavailable or may not function as expected.

CMPT_a027_hvilSelfTestShortDetected.d: High Voltage Interlock Loop (HVIL) circuitry self-test detected an internal short.
CMPT_a027_hvilSelfTestShortDetected.m: The HVIL cannot identify when there is a disconnect.

CMPT_a028_gateDriverNotReady.d: The compressor high voltage powerstage gate drivers reported improper input voltage preventing their operation.

CMPT_a029_gateDriverRetriesExpired.d: One or more of the gate drivers expired its available retries.
CMPT_a029_gateDriverRetriesExpired.m: The HV compressor will not be able to spin and provide heating/cooling

CMPT_a100_CANbus_MIA.d: More than half of the ECUs on a either the VEH or PARTY bus are missing more than half their messages while the bus should be active.

CMPT_a139_largeCurrentOffset.d: A phase current offset of the compressor multiphase motor is greater than the expected variance. Compressor may not operate as expected.
CMPT_a139_largeCurrentOffset.m: Compressor may not operate as expected. Controller may be damaged.

CP_a004_proximityRationality.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects proximity voltage in an unexpected range.

CP_a005_gbdcLiveDisconnect.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) has detected that a GB (Charging standard used in China) CC1 or CC2 charger disconnected unexpectedly during charge.
CP_a005_gbdcLiveDisconnect.m: Charging may be interrupted unexpectedly, and there could be damage to the charge inlet if the charge cable is disconnected while under load.

CP_a006_lostCommsBMS.d: Expected Battery Management System (BMS) controller area network (CAN) messages are missing. Charging may not start.

CP_a008_memoryError.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory error.
CP_a008_memoryError.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a009_coverOpenAllChgBlocked.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not sense the presence of the high voltage (HV) CP cover.
CP_a009_coverOpenAllChgBlocked.m: Unable to charge - Service is required

CP_a010_pilotRationality.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) receives pilot sensor readings that are outside of the expected range.

CP_a011_eeprom.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an internal memory condition on the electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM).
CP_a011_eeprom.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a012_ledDriver.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an issue with the LED driver.
CP_a012_ledDriver.m: Charge port LEDs may not illuminate

CP_a015_apsVov.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an over voltage condition on the external low voltage (LV) power supply.
CP_a015_apsVov.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted. If the voltage is too high, the CP ECU may become damaged.

CP_a016_apsVuv.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects lower than expected voltage from the low voltage (LV) battery.

CP_a017_fiveVov.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an over voltage condition on the internal 5V power supply.
CP_a017_fiveVov.m: The charge port may not function as expected.

CP_a018_fiveVuv.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an under voltage condition on the internal 5V power supply.
CP_a018_fiveVuv.m: The charge port may not function as expected.

CP_a023_gbdcSessionFailed.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an error during a charge session with a Guobiao direct current (GB DC) charging station, causing the charge session to be aborted.
CP_a023_gbdcSessionFailed.m: GB DC charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a024_ledsUC.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an undercurrent condition with the CP status LEDs.
CP_a024_ledsUC.m: The CP status LEDs may not turn on or may flicker.

CP_a025_ledsOC.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an overcurrent condition with the CP status LEDs.
CP_a025_ledsOC.m: The CP status LEDs may not turn on or may flicker.

CP_a027_doorSensorOutOfSpec.d: The charge port (CP) door hall effect sensor is out of range.
CP_a027_doorSensorOutOfSpec.m: The CP ECU cannot detect the CP door.

CP_a028_insertEnableMismatch.d: The latch drive enable hardline and the corresponding controller area network (CAN) signal do not match.
CP_a028_insertEnableMismatch.m: Inability to engage or disengage latch

CP_a029_doorClosedProxPilot.d: The charge port (CP) door is closed but the CP electronic control unit (ECU) still senses pilot voltage and/or proximity voltage in an unexpected range.
CP_a029_doorClosedProxPilot.m: The CP door may not operate as designed.

CP_a031_doorClosedCommandedOpen.d: Charge port (CP) door is expected to be open but is still closed.
CP_a031_doorClosedCommandedOpen.m: Charge port door may not open correctly.

CP_a032_doorOpenExpectedClosed.d: Charge port (CP) door is expected to be closed but is still open.
CP_a032_doorOpenExpectedClosed.m: Charge port door may not close properly.

CP_a034_calibrationIncomplete.m: The CP ECU may not be able to detects the latch position correctly, and the latch may not engage or disengage.

CP_a035_latchMovement_1.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch has been manually disengaged.
CP_a035_latchMovement_1.m: Charging disabled when using manual release

CP_a036_latchNotDisengaged.d: Charge port (CP) latch is expected to be disengaged but remains engaged. Charge cable may become stuck, or user may be unable to insert charge cable.
CP_a036_latchNotDisengaged.m: The charge port (CP) latch may not disengage, charge cable may become stuck, or user may be unable to insert charge cable.

CP_a037_latchNotEngaged.d: The charge port (CP) latch is expected to be engaged but is not engaged. AC charge rate may be reduced, and DC charging may be unavailable.
CP_a037_latchNotEngaged.m: The charge port (CP) latch may not engage as expected. AC charge rate may be reduced, and DC charging may be unavailable.

CP_a038_latchNotBlocking.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch is not in the blocking position when it is expected to be.
CP_a038_latchNotBlocking.m: The latch does not block cable insertion when it is expected to.

CP_a039_latchMovement_2.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch has been manually disengaged.

CP_a041_doorSensorUnplugged.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge port door sensor is unplugged.

CP_a042_doorAssemblyBroken.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (CPU) detects an issue with the charge port door assembly.

CP_a043_doorPotIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an issue with the CP door potentiometer.

CP_a044_lostCommsHVP.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the high voltage processor (HVP).

CP_a045_lostCommsVCSEC.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the vehicle security controller (VCSEC).
CP_a045_lostCommsVCSEC.m: The charge port does not unlock.

CP_a046_lostCommsEVSE.d: Vehicle unexpectedly lost digital communications with offboard charging equipment. Charging may be interrupted.

CP_a047_lostCommsVCFRONT.m: Charging may not start, charge port door and latch may not actuate, charge port LEDs may not turn on

CP_a049_multipleCablesDetected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects multiple charge cables at the same time.

CP_a050_latchNotConnected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch assembly is unplugged.
CP_a050_latchNotConnected.m: Charge port latch fault - AC charging reduced

CP_a051_doorInductiveSensorMIA.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that communication with the CP door inductive sensor has failed.
CP_a051_doorInductiveSensorMIA.m: Charge port may not open when pressed

CP_a052_chademoNotSupported.d: Vehicle is connected to an offboard CHAdeMO charging station, or CHAdeMO Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE), that is not supported.

CP_a053_proxLatchedNoPilot.d: The charge port (CP) has detected a missing control pilot signal while a charge cable is connected, which usually indicates the charge station does not have power. Charging may not start, and it may b
CP_a053_proxLatchedNoPilot.m: Unable to charge - Charge station not powered

CP_a054_cableNotSecured.d: Charge port (CP) expects the cable to be secured, but the charge port latch has not secured the cable in place. AC charge rate may be reduced, and DC charging may be unavailable.

CP_a055_chargeStoppedNoPilot.d: Control pilot signal from charging station is lost while charging. Charging may be interrupted.

CP_a056_proxDisconnected.d: Proximity circuit was opened unexpectedly while charging. Charging may be interrupted.

CP_a057_ccs1AdapterBtnPress.d: The charge port (CP) detected that button was pressed on the charge cable connected to the Combined Charging System (CCS) Combo 1 Adapter.
CP_a057_ccs1AdapterBtnPress.m: CCS cable button not intended to stop charging

CP_a058_acChargeRetryPending.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) will delay an attempt to AC charge.
CP_a058_acChargeRetryPending.m: Unable to AC charge - System will retry shortly

CP_a059_swcanError.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to establish single wire controller area network (SWCAN) communications. .
CP_a059_swcanError.m: Charging does not start or is interrupted.

CP_a060_lostCommsPCS.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the power conversion system (PCS).

CP_a061_uhfReceiverMIA.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the ultra-high frequency (UHF) receiver is missing (MIA).
CP_a061_uhfReceiverMIA.m: The CP door cannot be opened with the Tesla charge handle button.

CP_a062_scOutOfService.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the vehicle is connected to a Supercharger post that is out of service.

CP_a063_scUpdateInProgress.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the vehicle is connected to a Supercharger post that is updating.

CP_a064_superchargingBlocked.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that Supercharging is blocked because it is not enabled on the vehicle.

CP_a066_proxLatchedIdlePilot.d: Control pilot signal remains in state B1, indicating the charge station is connected to the vehicle but is not ready to charge the vehicle, for too long while a charge cable is plugged into the charge

CP_a067_gbdcConnFault.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that a GB (Charging standard used in China) DC charger has an issue with the connector.

CP_a068_doorSensorMismatch.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the presence sensor and potentiometer readings of the CP door position do not match.
CP_a068_doorSensorMismatch.m: The charge port door may not open when pressed.

CP_a069_doorInductiveSensorError.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a conversion error on the CP door inductive sensor.
CP_a069_doorInductiveSensorError.m: The CP door may not function as expected.

CP_a070_doorInductiveSensorReset.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) reset the CP door inductive sensor due to a I2C communication or conversion error.
CP_a070_doorInductiveSensorReset.m: The CP door may not function as expected.

CP_a071_exiDecodeFailure.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to read the received Combined Charging System (CCS) vehicle to grid (V2G) messages.
CP_a071_exiDecodeFailure.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a072_v2gEvccTimeout.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charging session is taking longer to startup than expected and exceeds the maximum time frame.
CP_a072_v2gEvccTimeout.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a073_iecComboShutdown.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Combined Charging System (CCS) charge session was aborted.
CP_a073_iecComboShutdown.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a074_failedToEstablishV2gComm.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to communicate with the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE).

CP_a075_v2gCommsFailure.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not receive the expected Combined Charging System (CCS) vehicle to grid (V2G) messages within the expected timeframe.
CP_a075_v2gCommsFailure.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a077_invalidMacAddress.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an invalid MAC address.
CP_a077_invalidMacAddress.m: Combined charging system (CCS) DC charging may not start.

CP_a078_latchNotDisengagedCold.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch is not disengaged when it is expected to be disengaged, and may be frozen.
CP_a078_latchNotDisengagedCold.m: Cable blocked - Charge port latch may be frozen

CP_a079_cableNotSecuredCold.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge cable latch is disengaged when it is expected to be engaged, and may be frozen.
CP_a079_cableNotSecuredCold.m: Charge rate reduced - Charge port may be frozen

CP_a082_powerOnReset.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a reset was completed via a power cycle. This alert is for logging purposes and is set every time a power cycle occurs.
CP_a082_powerOnReset.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

CP_a084_proxPeDisconnected_gb.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the GB DC or AC protective earth (PE) busbar is missing.
CP_a084_proxPeDisconnected_gb.m: Unable to DC Fast Charge / Supercharge

CP_a085_dcPinTempFaulted.d: The charge port inlet pin temperature has reached an unacceptable level (greatly exceeds normal operating range), causing DC charging to stop.
CP_a085_dcPinTempFaulted.m: Unable to DC Fast Charge / Supercharge

CP_a086_dcPinTempIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an open circuit on the DC pin thermistor.
CP_a086_dcPinTempIrrational.m: Unable to DC Fast Charge / Supercharge

CP_a089_plcConfigMismatch.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Gateway (GTW) configuration for power line communication (PLC) does not match the CP ECU hardware configuration.
CP_a089_plcConfigMismatch.m: The GTW may be unable to update the PLC modem during firmware updates.

CP_a090_ccsEvseLowIso.d: During a DC fast charging session at a Combined Charging System (CCS) charging station, the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) reports low isolation resistance.
CP_a090_ccsEvseLowIso.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_a091_wrongSuperchargerHandle.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) on a combined charging system (CCS) vehicle detects a Supercharger communicating via single-wire controller area network (SWCAN).

CP_a092_modemAppLoadFailed.d: The Power Line Communication (PLC) modem application for the Charge Port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not load after a maximum number of automatic retries. DC fast charging will be unavaila

CP_a093_modemLoadedWithReset.d: The power line communication (PLC) modem successfully booted its application but first required the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) to reset.
CP_a093_modemLoadedWithReset.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

CP_a094_inductiveResetSuccessful.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects communication between the CP ECU and the inductive sensor was successfully recovered via a reset.
CP_a094_inductiveResetSuccessful.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

CP_a095_thermalDcLimitActive.d: The charge port DC pin temperatures have entered a region where its thermal foldback limits are dynamically changing.
CP_a095_thermalDcLimitActive.m: DC charge current may be limited

CP_a099_cableStateUnknown.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to confirm the state of the charging cable.
CP_a099_cableStateUnknown.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted. Before entering drive, confirm the charge cable is not connected to the vehicle.

CP_a101_wcFoldbackActive.d: The Gen 2 Wall Connector reports a temperature high enough that AC charging should begin to fold back.
CP_a101_wcFoldbackActive.m: Charge rate reduced - Wall connection hot

CP_a102_wcOvertempFault.d: The Gen 2 Wall Connector reports a high temperature at which charging should stop.
CP_a102_wcOvertempFault.m: Unable to charge - Wall connection too hot

CP_a103_proximityPeDisconnected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the protective earth connection may be missing.

CP_a107_chademoFoldbackActive.d: The CHAdeMO adapter reports a temperature at which charging should begin to fold back.

CP_a108_chademoOvertempFault.d: The CHAdeMO adapter reports a temperature at which charging should stop.

CP_a109_thermistorIrrational.d: Thermistor input is open or short circuit.
CP_a109_thermistorIrrational.m: Charging and heating functions do not work as expected.

CP_a110_thermalVelocityHigh.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an unexpected rapid rise in temperature.

CP_a111_inletHeaterUnableToHeat.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the CP inlet heater is not able to heat properly.
CP_a111_inletHeaterUnableToHeat.m: The CP inlet heater does not work and the CP may remain frozen, requiring defrosting by other means.

CP_a112_inletHeaterIrrationalTemperature.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) reads an irrational temperature on the inlet heater temperature pin after the heater has been enabled.
CP_a112_inletHeaterIrrationalTemperature.m: The CP inlet heater does not work and the CP may remain frozen, requiring defrosting by other means.

CP_a113_inletHeaterShortCircuit.d: The charge port (CP) detects a short circuit on the inlet heater hardware.
CP_a113_inletHeaterShortCircuit.m: The CP inlet heater does not work and the CP may remain frozen, requiring defrosting by other means.

CP_a116_misconfigurationDetected.d: The installed charge port (CP) electronic contol unit (ECU) is not compatible with the vehicle.
CP_a116_misconfigurationDetected.m: Unable to charge - Service is required

CP_a117_inletHeaterUnableToRegulate.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is not able to regulate the inlet heater.
CP_a117_inletHeaterUnableToRegulate.m: The CP inlet heater does not work.

CP_a120_comboAdapterFoldback.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detected that the Combined Charging System (CCS) Combo 1 Adapter temperature is higher than expected. The charging rate may be reduced.

CP_a121_PLCRLY_lostCommsCP.d: The power line communication relay (PLCRLY) is no longer receiving messages from the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU). This alert is used only by PLCRLY.
CP_a121_PLCRLY_lostCommsCP.m: PLCRLYs on single-phase CP vehicles will not allow combined charging system (CCS) charging above 370A.

CP_a124_ivledFault.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the IVLED is stuck in an uncontrolled state.
CP_a124_ivledFault.m: The CP IVLED does not function as expected, either illuminating when not intended or not illuminating when intended.

CP_a125_unexpectedLVJuiceState.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit detects that it unexpectedly does not have permission to use high power from the low voltage (LV) bus.
CP_a125_unexpectedLVJuiceState.m: The CP latch, door, and inlet heater do not behave as expected.

CP_a127_lostCommsHVLINK.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the HVLINK.

CP_a129_bldcDriverCommsFailed.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the brushless DC (BLDC) motor driver.
CP_a129_bldcDriverCommsFailed.m: The CP door will not open or close as expected.

CP_a130_bldcDriverCommsRecovered.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) was able to recover communication with the brushless DC (BLDC) motor driver.

CP_a131_evseCommTimeout.d: Communication times out between the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) and the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE).
CP_a131_evseCommTimeout.m: Charging session was not started in time

CP_a132_contractAuthTimeout.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) is still in an authentication state.

CP_a133_proxNeverLatched.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge cable proximity did not latch within the expected timeframe.
CP_a133_proxNeverLatched.m: Unable to charge - Vehicle connection issue

CP_a134_plcModemNotExpected.d: The power line communication (PLC) modem establishes communication successfully when it is expected to be depopulated.
CP_a134_plcModemNotExpected.m: Combined charging system (CCS) charging does not work.

CP_a135_sdpAttemptsFailed.d: The combined charging system (CCS) charging session is not started due to communication failures during the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) discovery protocol (SDP).

CP_a138_swcanCommsAcEvseFaulted.d: AC electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) communicating over single wire controller area network (SWCAN) reports that it has encountered an issue.
CP_a138_swcanCommsAcEvseFaulted.m: Charging does not start or is interrupted.

CP_a139_pilotFaulted.d: An electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) using pulse width modulation (PWM) pilot reports that it has encountered an issue.

CP_a140_superchargerFaulted.d: A Supercharger post communicating over single wire controller area network (SWCAN) reports that it has encountered an issue.
CP_a140_superchargerFaulted.m: Unable to charge - Supercharger reports an error

CP_a141_chademoAdapterFault.d: A CHAdeMO adapter communicating over single wire controller area network (SWCAN) reports that it has encountered an issue.

CP_a142_gbdcScConnFault.d: A GB (Charging standard used in China) DC Supercharger reports an issue with the connector.
CP_a142_gbdcScConnFault.m: Unable to charge - Supercharger reports an error

CP_a143_unsupportedChargeAdapter.d: The charge port detected an unsupported charging adapter.
CP_a143_unsupportedChargeAdapter.m: Charging adapter has electric arc flash hazard

CP_a145_modemAppLoadFailedNA.d: The Power Line Communication (PLC) modem application for the Charge Port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not load after a maximum number of automatic retries. DC fast charging will be unavaila
CP_a145_modemAppLoadFailedNA.m: Cannot CCS/3rd-party NACS DC fast charge

CP_a146_ccsEvseMalfunction.d: The Combined Charging System (CCS) charge station reports that it has experienced an unrecoverable error. Charging may not start or may be interrupted, with a charge cable cycle (disconnect, then reco

CP_a151_badPilotDiodeDetected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects the control pilot voltage is outside of the nominal range while using Tesla electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE), indicating an internal
CP_a151_badPilotDiodeDetected.m: Charge port error detected - Service is required

CP_a152_pilotEdgeDetectionFailed.d: The Charge Port (CP) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is unable to detect the control pilot signal due to an internal condition on the CP ECU. Charging may be unavailable.
CP_a152_pilotEdgeDetectionFailed.m: Charge port error detected - Unable to charge

CP_a153_thermalAcLimitActive.d: The charge port inlet pin temperature has reached a level where limiting AC charge current is necessary until temperature returns to normal operating range.

CP_a154_acChargingStoppedOT.d: The charge port inlet pin temperature has reached an unacceptable level (greatly exceeds normal operating range), causing AC charging to stop.
CP_a154_acChargingStoppedOT.m: AC charging will be interrupted and remain unavailable until a charge cable cycle occurs (disconnect and reconnect) and the charge port inlet pin temp

CP_a155_hallPresenceRationality.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is reading irrational values from the CP door hall effect sensor located in the CP door, which may prevent the CP door from opening when pressed.
CP_a155_hallPresenceRationality.m: The CP door may not open when it is pressed.

CP_a156_gbdcPrechargingConcern.d: The vehicle controller detects that precharging the high voltage (HV) DC bus may be unsuccessful, which may lead to the inability to close the HV contactors and a complete loss of vehicle power. Charg
CP_a156_gbdcPrechargingConcern.m: DC Fast Charging may prevent vehicle restart

CP_a161_badPilotDiodeDetectedCN.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the control pilot voltage is not within its nominal range indicating a possible issue with the ECU.
CP_a161_badPilotDiodeDetectedCN.m: AC charging may not be operational on the next attempt. DC fast charging should remain functional.

CP_a162_pilotEdgeDetectionFailedCN.d: The Charge Port (CP) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is unable to detect the control pilot signal due to an internal condition on the CP ECU. AC charging may be unavailable.
CP_a162_pilotEdgeDetectionFailedCN.m: AC charging may not be operational on the next attempt. DC fast charging should remain functional.

CP_a163_coverOpenDcChgBlocked.d: DC charging is unavailable because the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) has detected that the high voltage (HV) back cover and/or CP AC charging connector are not present on the back of
CP_a163_coverOpenDcChgBlocked.m: DC Fast Charging unavailable - OK to AC charge

CP_a164_latchRedisengageRequested.d: The charge port (CP) receives multiple consecutive user requests to disengage the CP latch and still detects a charge cable connected, possibly indicating a condition affecting latch functionality. Us
CP_a164_latchRedisengageRequested.m: Charge handle still detected after unlatch request

CP_a171_bedOutletDoorIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) detects that the bed outlet door sensor is irrational, resulting in the bed outlets being permanently turned off.
CP_a171_bedOutletDoorIrrational.m: The bed outlets will not be functional.

CP_a173_coverOpenAcChgBlocked.d: AC charging is unavailable because the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) has detected that the CP AC charging connector is not present on the back of the CP inlet.
CP_a173_coverOpenAcChgBlocked.m: AC charging unavailable - OK to DC Fast Charge

CP_a185_pncAuthRejection.d: The charging station reports a payment authorization failure.

CP_a188_v2lEvseRequiresUpdate.d: Vehicle is connected to an unpowered Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) with an incompatible software version for Vehicle To Load (V2L).
CP_a188_v2lEvseRequiresUpdate.m: Update Mobile Connector to use Powershare

CP_a189_resistivePresenceRationality.d: The Charge Port (CP) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is reading irrational values from resistive divider located in the CP door, which may prevent the CP door from opening when pressed.
CP_a189_resistivePresenceRationality.m: The CP may not be able to determine the state of the manual door.

CP_a201_uhfDoorOpenRepeatedlyOverLimit.d: The Charge Port (CP) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects excessive ultra-high frequency (UHF) requests from a nearby charge cable.
CP_a201_uhfDoorOpenRepeatedlyOverLimit.m: Charge port door may not open in response to UHF request until the next drive or charge cycle.

CP_a205_doorPositionUndetectable.d: The Charge Port (CP) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) cannot identify the CP door position due to the position related sensors not operating as expected.
CP_a205_doorPositionUndetectable.m: Charge port door position cannot be detected

CP_d190_backCoverCircuitIssue.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected an issue with the back cover circuit.
CP_d190_backCoverCircuitIssue.m: The vehicle may be unable to charge.

CP_d191_latchConnectionIssue.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected a latch connection issue.
CP_d191_latchConnectionIssue.m: The vehicle may be unable to charge or the charge cable may not be removable.

CP_d192_couplerTempHigh.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected the pin temperature is above the threshold.
CP_d192_couplerTempHigh.m: The vehicle is unable to charge until the charge cable is cycled.

CP_d194_proximityCircuitRangeIssue.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected the proximity voltage is above the nominal range.
CP_d194_proximityCircuitRangeIssue.m: The vehicle may be unable to charge.

CP_d195_couplerLockActuatorIssue.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected the latch is unable to move.
CP_d195_couplerLockActuatorIssue.m: The vehicle may not be able to remove the charge cable.

CP_d196_doorActuatorPerformanceIssue.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected the door is not working as expected.
CP_d196_doorActuatorPerformanceIssue.m: The charge port door may not actuate as expected.

CP_d197_doorSensorCircuitErratic.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected an out-of-range reading from the charge port door potentiometer.
CP_d197_doorSensorCircuitErratic.m: The charge port door may not actuate as expected.

CP_d198_couplerTempSensorCircuitErratic.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected an out-of-range reading from the DC pin temperature sensor.
CP_d198_couplerTempSensorCircuitErratic.m: The vehicle may be unable to DC charge.

CP_d199_hardwareConfigMismatch.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected an internal hardware configuration mismatch.
CP_d199_hardwareConfigMismatch.m: The vehicle may be unable to charge.

CP_d200_pilotCircuitIssue.d: The Charge Port (CP) has detected a damaged pilot circuit.
CP_d200_pilotCircuitIssue.m: The vehicle may be unable to charge.

CP_w004_proximityRationality.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects proximity voltage in an unexpected range.
CP_w004_proximityRationality.m: If the issue is with the vehicle, it will not be able to charge.

CP_w005_gbdcLiveDisconnect.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) has detected that a GB (Charging standard used in China) CC1 or CC2 charger disconnected unexpectedly during charge.
CP_w005_gbdcLiveDisconnect.m: Charging may be interrupted unexpectedly, and there could be damage to the charge inlet if the charge cable is disconnected while under load.

CP_w006_lostCommsBMS.d: Expected Battery Management System (BMS) controller area network (CAN) messages are missing. Charging may not start.

CP_w008_memoryError.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory error.
CP_w008_memoryError.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w009_coverOpenAllChgBlocked.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not sense the presence of the high voltage (HV) charge port cover.
CP_w009_coverOpenAllChgBlocked.m: Unable to charge - Service is required

CP_w010_pilotRationality.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) receives pilot sensor readings that are outside of the expected range.

CP_w011_eeprom.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an internal memory condition on the electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM).
CP_w011_eeprom.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w012_ledDriver.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an issue with the LED driver.
CP_w012_ledDriver.m: The charge port LED may not display colors properly or at all.

CP_w015_apsVov.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an over voltage condition on the external low voltage (LV) power supply.
CP_w015_apsVov.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted. If the voltage is too high, the CP ECU may become damaged.

CP_w016_apsVuv.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects lower than expected voltage from the low voltage (LV) battery.

CP_w017_fiveVov.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an over voltage condition on the internal 5V power supply.
CP_w017_fiveVov.m: The charge port may not function as expected.

CP_w018_fiveVuv.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an under voltage condition on the internal 5V power supply.
CP_w018_fiveVuv.m: The charge port may not function as expected.

CP_w020_threeVuv.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an under voltage condition on the internal 3.3V power supply.
CP_w020_threeVuv.m: The charge port may not function as expected.

CP_w023_gbdcSessionFailed.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an error during a charge session with a Guobiao direct current (GB DC) charging station, causing the charge session to be aborted.
CP_w023_gbdcSessionFailed.m: GB DC charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w024_ledsUC.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an undercurrent condition with the CP status LEDs.
CP_w024_ledsUC.m: The CP status LEDs may not turn on or may flicker.

CP_w025_ledsOC.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an overcurrent condition with the CP status LEDs.
CP_w025_ledsOC.m: The CP status LEDs may not turn on or may flicker.

CP_w027_doorSensorOutOfSpec.d: The charge port (CP) door hall effect sensor is out of range.
CP_w027_doorSensorOutOfSpec.m: The CP ECU cannot detect the CP door.

CP_w028_insertEnableMismatch.d: The latch drive enable hardline and the corresponding controller area network (CAN) signal do not match.
CP_w028_insertEnableMismatch.m: Inability to engage or disengage latch

CP_w029_doorClosedProxPilot.d: The charge port (CP) door is closed but the CP electronic control unit (ECU) still senses pilot voltage and/or proximity voltage in an unexpected range.
CP_w029_doorClosedProxPilot.m: The CP door may not operate as designed.

CP_w031_doorClosedCommandedOpen.d: Charge port (CP) door is expected to be open but is still closed.
CP_w031_doorClosedCommandedOpen.m: Charge port door may not open correctly.

CP_w032_doorOpenExpectedClosed.d: Charge port (CP) door is expected to be closed but is still open.
CP_w032_doorOpenExpectedClosed.m: Charge port door may not close properly.

CP_w034_calibrationIncomplete.m: The CP ECU may not be able to detects the latch position correctly, and the latch may not engage or disengage.

CP_w035_latchMovement_1.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch has been manually disengaged.
CP_w035_latchMovement_1.m: Charging disabled when using manual release

CP_w036_latchNotDisengaged.d: Charge port (CP) latch is expected to be disengaged but remains engaged. Charge cable may become stuck, or user may be unable to insert charge cable.
CP_w036_latchNotDisengaged.m: The charge port (CP) latch may not disengage, charge cable may become stuck, or user may be unable to insert charge cable.

CP_w037_latchNotEngaged.d: The charge port (CP) latch is expected to be engaged but is not engaged. AC charge rate may be reduced, and DC charging may be unavailable.
CP_w037_latchNotEngaged.m: The charge port (CP) latch may not engage as expected. AC charge rate may be reduced, and DC charging may be unavailable.

CP_w038_latchNotBlocking.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch is not in the blocking position when it is expected to be.
CP_w038_latchNotBlocking.m: The latch does not block cable insertion when it is expected to.

CP_w039_latchMovement_2.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch has been manually disengaged.

CP_w041_doorSensorUnplugged.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge port door sensor is unplugged.

CP_w042_doorAssemblyBroken.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (CPU) detects an issue with the charge port door assembly.

CP_w043_doorPotIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an issue with the CP door potentiometer.

CP_w044_lostCommsHVP.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the high voltage processor (HVP).

CP_w045_lostCommsVCSEC.m: Charge port may not unlock, mobile app charge port controls may not work

CP_w046_lostCommsEVSE.d: Vehicle unexpectedly lost digital communications with offboard charging equipment. Charging may be interrupted.

CP_w047_lostCommsVCFRONT.m: Charging may not start, charge port door and latch may not actuate, charge port LED may not work

CP_w048_lostCommsUI.d: Expected user interface (UI) controller area network (CAN) messages are missing. Charge port door may not open when request is made via the user interface (UI) or mobile app.
CP_w048_lostCommsUI.m: Charge port (CP) door may not open with user interface (UI) or mobile app controls.

CP_w049_multipleCablesDetected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects multiple charge cables at the same time.

CP_w050_latchNotConnected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch assembly is unplugged.
CP_w050_latchNotConnected.m: Charge port latch fault - AC charging reduced

CP_w051_doorInductiveSensorMIA.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that communication with the CP door inductive sensor has failed.
CP_w051_doorInductiveSensorMIA.m: Push-to-open does not work, and the charge port does not detect that the high voltage (HV) inlet is covered by the CP door.

CP_w052_chademoNotSupported.d: Vehicle is connected to an offboard CHAdeMO charging station, or CHAdeMO Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE), that is not supported.

CP_w053_proxLatchedNoPilot.d: The charge port (CP) has detected a missing control pilot signal while a charge cable is connected, which usually indicates the charge station does not have power. Charging may not start, and it may b

CP_w054_cableNotSecured.d: Charge port (CP) expects the cable to be secured, but the charge port latch has not secured the cable in place. AC charge rate may be reduced, and DC charging may be unavailable.

CP_w055_chargeStoppedNoPilot.d: Control pilot signal from charging station is lost while charging. Charging may be interrupted.

CP_w056_proxDisconnected.d: Proximity circuit was opened unexpectedly while charging. Charging may be interrupted.

CP_w057_ccs1AdapterBtnPress.d: The charge port (CP) detected that button was pressed on the charge cable connected to the Combined Charging System (CCS) Combo 1 Adapter.
CP_w057_ccs1AdapterBtnPress.m: CCS cable button not intended to stop charging

CP_w058_acChargeRetryPending.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) will delay an attempt to AC charge.
CP_w058_acChargeRetryPending.m: Charging does not start or is interrupted.

CP_w059_swcanError.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to establish single wire controller area network (SWCAN) communications. .
CP_w059_swcanError.m: Charging does not start or is interrupted.

CP_w060_lostCommsPCS.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) loses communication with the power conversion system (PCS).

CP_w061_uhfReceiverMIA.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the ultra-high frequency (UHF) receiver is missing (MIA).
CP_w061_uhfReceiverMIA.m: The CP door cannot be opened with the Tesla charge handle button.

CP_w062_scOutOfService.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the vehicle is connected to a Supercharger post that is out of service.
CP_w062_scOutOfService.m: Supercharging is unavailable at that post.

CP_w063_scUpdateInProgress.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the vehicle is connected to a Supercharger post that is updating.
CP_w063_scUpdateInProgress.m: Supercharging is unavailable at that post.

CP_w064_superchargingBlocked.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that Supercharging is blocked because it is not enabled on the vehicle.

CP_w066_proxLatchedIdlePilot.d: Control pilot signal remains in state B1, indicating the charge station is connected to the vehicle but is not ready to charge the vehicle, for too long while a charge cable is plugged into the charge
CP_w066_proxLatchedIdlePilot.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted unexpectedly.

CP_w067_gbdcConnFault.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that a GB (Charging standard used in China) DC charger has an issue with the connector.

CP_w068_doorSensorMismatch.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the inductive sensor and potentiometer readings of the CP door position do not match.
CP_w068_doorSensorMismatch.m: The charge port door may not open when requested.

CP_w069_doorInductiveSensorError.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a conversion error on the CP door inductive sensor.
CP_w069_doorInductiveSensorError.m: The CP door may not function as expected.

CP_w070_doorInductiveSensorReset.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) reset the CP door inductive sensor due to a I2C communication or conversion error.
CP_w070_doorInductiveSensorReset.m: The CP door may not function as expected.

CP_w071_exiDecodeFailure.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to read the received Combined Charging System (CCS) vehicle to grid (V2G) messages.
CP_w071_exiDecodeFailure.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w072_v2gEvccTimeout.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charging session is taking longer to startup than expected and exceeds the maximum time frame.
CP_w072_v2gEvccTimeout.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w073_iecComboShutdown.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Combined Charging System (CCS) charge session was aborted.
CP_w073_iecComboShutdown.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w074_failedToEstablishV2gComm.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to communicate with the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE).

CP_w075_v2gCommsFailure.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not receive the expected Combined Charging System (CCS) vehicle to grid (V2G) messages within the expected timeframe.
CP_w075_v2gCommsFailure.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w077_invalidMacAddress.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an invalid MAC address.
CP_w077_invalidMacAddress.m: Combined charging system (CCS) DC charging may not start.

CP_w078_latchNotDisengagedCold.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the latch is not disengaged when it is expected to be disengaged, and may be frozen.
CP_w078_latchNotDisengagedCold.m: The CP latch is frozen in the engaged state, blocking cable insertion and removal.

CP_w079_cableNotSecuredCold.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge cable latch is disengaged when it is expected to be engaged, and may be frozen.
CP_w079_cableNotSecuredCold.m: The CP latch is frozen in the disengaged state, and as a result AC charging is limited and DC charging does not start.

CP_w082_powerOnReset.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a reset was completed via a power cycle. This alert is for logging purposes and is set every time a power cycle occurs.
CP_w082_powerOnReset.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

CP_w084_proxPeDisconnected_gb.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the GB DC or AC protective earth (PE) busbar is missing.
CP_w084_proxPeDisconnected_gb.m: GB DC charging does not start.

CP_w085_dcPinTempFaulted.d: The charge port inlet pin temperature has reached an unacceptable level (greatly exceeds normal operating range), causing DC charging to stop.
CP_w085_dcPinTempFaulted.m: DC charging will be interrupted and remain unavailable until a charge cable cycle occurs (disconnect and reconnect) and the charge port inlet pin temp

CP_w086_dcPinTempIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an open circuit on the DC pin thermistor.

CP_w089_plcConfigMismatch.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Gateway (GTW) configuration for power line communication (PLC) does not match the CP ECU hardware configuration.
CP_w089_plcConfigMismatch.m: The GTW may be unable to update the PLC modem during firmware updates.

CP_w090_ccsEvseLowIso.d: During a DC fast charging session at a Combined Charging System (CCS) charging station, the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) reports low isolation resistance.
CP_w090_ccsEvseLowIso.m: CCS charging may not start or may be interrupted.

CP_w091_wrongSuperchargerHandle.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) on a combined charging system (CCS) vehicle detects a Supercharger communicating via single-wire controller area network (SWCAN).
CP_w091_wrongSuperchargerHandle.m: Type 2 (non-CCS) DC charging does not start.

CP_w092_modemAppLoadFailed.d: The Power Line Communication (PLC) modem application for the Charge Port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not load after a maximum number of automatic retries. DC fast charging will be unavaila
CP_w092_modemAppLoadFailed.m: DC fast charging will be unavailable at Combined Charging System (CCS) charge stations.

CP_w093_modemLoadedWithReset.d: The power line communication (PLC) modem successfully booted its application but first required the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) to reset.
CP_w093_modemLoadedWithReset.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

CP_w094_inductiveResetSuccessful.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects communication between the CP ECU and the inductive sensor was successfully recovered via a reset.
CP_w094_inductiveResetSuccessful.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

CP_w095_thermalDcLimitActive.d: The charge port DC pin temperatures have entered a region where its thermal foldback limits are dynamically changing.
CP_w095_thermalDcLimitActive.m: DC charge current may be limited

CP_w099_cableStateUnknown.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to confirm the state of the charging cable.
CP_w099_cableStateUnknown.m: Charging may not start or may be interrupted. Before entering drive, confirm the charge cable is not connected to the vehicle.

CP_w100_inletHarnessIdIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects unexpected voltage at the inlet harness identifier pins or is unable to identify the type of inlet harness.
CP_w100_inletHarnessIdIrrational.m: No expected impact on vehicle functionality. Other alert(s) indicating a specific condition affecting charging functionality may be present.

CP_w101_wcFoldbackActive.d: The Gen 2 Wall Connector reports a temperature high enough that AC charging should begin to fold back.
CP_w101_wcFoldbackActive.m: Charge rate reduced - Wall connection hot

CP_w102_wcOvertempFault.d: The Gen 2 Wall Connector reports a high temperature at which charging should stop.
CP_w102_wcOvertempFault.m: Unable to charge - Wall connection too hot

CP_w103_proximityPeDisconnected.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the protective earth connection may be missing.

CP_w107_chademoFoldbackActive.d: The CHAdeMO adapter reports a temperature at which charging should begin to fold back.

CP_w108_chademoOvertempFault.d: The CHAdeMO adapter reports a temperature at which charging should stop.

CP_w109_thermistorIrrational.d: Thermistor input is open or short circuit.
CP_w109_thermistorIrrational.m: Charging and heating functions do not work as expected.

CP_w110_thermalVelocityHigh.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an unexpected rapid rise in temperature.

CP_w120_comboAdapterFoldback.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detected that the Combined Charging System (CCS) Combo 1 Adapter temperature is higher than expected. The charging rate may be reduced.

CP_w121_PLCRLY_lostCommsCP.d: The power line communication relay (PLCRLY) is no longer receiving messages from the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU). This alert is used only by PLCRLY.
CP_w121_PLCRLY_lostCommsCP.m: PLCRLYs on single-phase CP vehicles will not allow combined charging system (CCS) charging above 370A.

CP_w131_evseCommTimeout.d: Communication times out between the charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) and the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE).
CP_w131_evseCommTimeout.m: Charging session was not started in time

CP_w132_contractAuthTimeout.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) is still in an authentication state.

CP_w133_proxNeverLatched.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge cable proximity did not latch within the expected timeframe.
CP_w133_proxNeverLatched.m: Unable to charge - Vehicle connection issue

CP_w134_plcModemNotExpected.d: The power line communication (PLC) modem establishes communication successfully when it is expected to be depopulated.
CP_w134_plcModemNotExpected.m: Combined charging system (CCS) charging does not work.

CP_w135_sdpAttemptsFailed.d: The combined charging system (CCS) charging session is not started due to communication failures during the electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) discovery protocol (SDP).

CP_w138_swcanCommsAcEvseFaulted.d: AC electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) communicating over single wire controller area network (SWCAN) reports that it has encountered an issue.
CP_w138_swcanCommsAcEvseFaulted.m: Charging does not start or is interrupted.

CP_w139_pilotFaulted.d: An electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) using pulse width modulation (PWM) pilot reports that it has encountered an issue.

CP_w140_superchargerFaulted.d: A Supercharger post communicating over single wire controller area network (SWCAN) reports that it has encountered an issue.
CP_w140_superchargerFaulted.m: Unable to charge - Supercharger reports an error

CP_w141_chademoAdapterFault.d: A CHAdeMO adapter communicating over single wire controller area network (SWCAN) reports that it has encountered an issue.

CP_w142_gbdcScConnFault.d: A GB (Charging standard used in China) DC Supercharger reports an issue with the connector.
CP_w142_gbdcScConnFault.m: Unable to charge - Supercharger reports an error

CP_w143_unsupportedChargeAdapter.d: The charge port detected an unsupported charging adapter.
CP_w143_unsupportedChargeAdapter.m: Charging adapter has electric arc flash hazard

CP_w145_modemAppLoadFailedNA.d: The Power Line Communication (PLC) modem application for the Charge Port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) does not load after a maximum number of automatic retries. DC fast charging will be unavaila
CP_w145_modemAppLoadFailedNA.m: Cannot CCS/3rd-party NACS DC fast charge

CP_w146_ccsEvseMalfunction.d: The Combined Charging System (CCS) charge station reports that it has experienced an unrecoverable error. Charging may not start or may be interrupted, with a charge cable cycle (disconnect, then reco

CP_w153_thermalAcLimitActive.d: The charge port inlet pin temperature has reached a level where limiting AC charge current is necessary until temperature returns to normal operating range.

CP_w154_acChargingStoppedOT.d: The charge port inlet pin temperature has reached an unacceptable level (greatly exceeds normal operating range), causing AC charging to stop.
CP_w154_acChargingStoppedOT.m: AC charging will be interrupted and remain unavailable until a charge cable cycle occurs (disconnect and reconnect) and the charge port inlet pin temp

CP_w156_gbdcPrechargingConcern.d: The vehicle controller detects that precharging the high voltage (HV) DC bus may be unsuccessful, which may lead to the inability to close the HV contactors and a complete loss of vehicle power. Charg
CP_w156_gbdcPrechargingConcern.m: DC Fast Charging may prevent vehicle restart

DAS_w073_issueEscalatedLDW.d: The Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detects a lane departure event, sending an escalated Lane Departure Warning (LDW) alert for human intervention.

DAS_w194_accDriverResumeRqrd.m: Press accelerator pedal to resume cruise control

DAS_w212_lcUnavailableStrikeOut.m: Autosteer unavailable for the rest of this drive

DAS_w217_lcUnavailableSpeeding.m: Autosteer unavailable for the rest of this drive

DAS_w219_lcTempUnavailableSpeed.m: Autosteer is speed restricted on this road

DAS_w229_alcUltrasoundDamaged.d: Automatic lane change (ALC) is initiated by the driver but ALC is unavailable due to Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detecting damage to ultrasonic sensors.

DAS_w230_alcUltrasoundBlocked.d: Automatic lane change (ALC) is initiated by the driver but ALC is unavailable due to Autopilot electronic control unit (ECU) detecting blocked ultrasonic sensors.

DFLKF_a001_vbusUndervoltage.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) input voltage sensor providing a value below lower bound threshold.
DFLKF_a001_vbusUndervoltage.m: DFLK may not function as expected.

DFLKF_a002_vbusOvervoltage.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) input voltage sensor providing a value above upper bound threshold.
DFLKF_a002_vbusOvervoltage.m: DFLK may not function as expected.

DFLKF_a003_boardOverTemp.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) temperature sensor providing a value above threshold.

DFLKF_a004_positionSensorNotReady.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) coil position sensor exceeds the maximum number of failures to correctly write to configuration registers.
DFLKF_a004_positionSensorNotReady.m: DFLK may not function as expected.

DFLKF_a005_positionSensorConversionError.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) coil position sensor has at least one conversion error.
DFLKF_a005_positionSensorConversionError.m: DFLK may not function as expected.

DFLKF_a007_coilControlUnavailable.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) functionality is unavailable. Locker actuation disabled.

DFLKF_a008_diUnitCanMIA.d: Drive Inverter unit (DIx) controller area network (CAN) communication to the differential locker (DFLK) is missing (MIA).
DFLKF_a008_diUnitCanMIA.m: DFLK coil unable to communicate to the DIx.

DFLKF_a010_positionSensorMIA.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) I2C communication with coil position sensor is missing (MIA).
DFLKF_a010_positionSensorMIA.m: DFLK coil unable to determine position.

DFLKF_a011_positionSensorReadingIrration.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) Coil Position Sensor is reporting the exact same value repeatedly, indicating the sensor has been disconnected.

DFLKF_a013_diRPMCanMIA.d: Drive Inverter (DI) controller area network (CAN) communication to the differential locker (DFLK) is missing (MIA).
DFLKF_a013_diRPMCanMIA.m: DFLK coil unable to determine engagement state.

DFLKF_a015_NVMMError.d: A condition affecting Differential Locker (DFLK) non-volatile memory (NVM) is detected by the non-volatile memory manager.
DFLKF_a015_NVMMError.m: Non-volatile memory records may be incorrect or missing.

DFLKF_a016_NVMMRecordError.d: A condition affecting a particular Differential Locker (DFLK) non-volatile memory (NVM) record is detected by the non-volatile memory manager.
DFLKF_a016_NVMMRecordError.m: The particular non-volatile memory record may be incorrect or missing.

DFLKF_a018_coilOvercurrent.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) measured coil current exceeds overcurrent limit.
DFLKF_a018_coilOvercurrent.m: DFLK unable to properly control coil current

DFLKF_a020_diMIA.d: At least one message the Differential Locker (DFLK) expects to receive from the Drive Inverter (DI) is not received.
DFLKF_a020_diMIA.m: DFLK may lose ability to control the coil

DFLKF_a021_selfTestFailed.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) power on self test failed.
DFLKF_a021_selfTestFailed.m: DFLK may lose ability to control the coil

DFLKF_a023_torqueTrapped.d: Torque trapped condition in differential locker detected.
DFLKF_a023_torqueTrapped.m: Locking differential not disengaged - Drive with caution

DFLKF_a025_positionUncalibratedOrCalCorr.d: Position sensor has not yet been calibrated or calibration is corrupted.
DFLKF_a025_positionUncalibratedOrCalCorr.m: Differential Locker (DFLK) functionality will be disabled. Position sense will report as unknown.

DFLKF_a026_currentSensorIrrational.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) current sensor is reporting an irrational value.
DFLKF_a026_currentSensorIrrational.m: DFLK is unable to properly sense current

DFLKF_a027_inputVoltageSensorIrrational.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) input voltage sensor is reporting an irrational value.
DFLKF_a027_inputVoltageSensorIrrational.m: DFLK is unable to properly sense input voltage

DFLKF_a028_pcbTempSensorIrrational.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) printed circuit board (PCB) temperature sensor is reporting an irrational value.
DFLKF_a028_pcbTempSensorIrrational.m: DFLK is unable to properly sense PCB temperature

DFLKF_a029_vcLVStateMIA.d: Vehicle Controller (VC) LV Power State controller area network (CAN) communication to the differential locker (DFLK) is missing (MIA).
DFLKF_a029_vcLVStateMIA.m: DFLK is unable to determine whether the vehicle controller expects it to be receiving LV power.

DFLKF_a030_diSystemMIA.m: DFLK is unable to utilize the unlocked probability signal from DI System and may not be able to auto-calibrate.

DFLKR_a007_coilControlUnavailable.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) functionality is unavailable. Locker actuation disabled.

DFLKR_a008_diUnitCanMIA.d: Drive Inverter unit (DIx) controller area network (CAN) communication to the differential locker (DFLK) is missing (MIA).
DFLKR_a008_diUnitCanMIA.m: DFLK coil unable to communicate to the DIx.

DFLKR_a028_pcbTempSensorIrrational.d: Differential Locker (DFLK) printed circuit board (PCB) temperature sensor is reporting an irrational value.
DFLKR_a028_pcbTempSensorIrrational.m: DFLK is unable to properly sense PCB temperature

DFLKR_a029_vcLVStateMIA.d: Vehicle Controller (VC) LV Power State controller area network (CAN) communication to the differential locker (DFLK) is missing (MIA).
DFLKR_a029_vcLVStateMIA.m: DFLK is unable to determine whether the vehicle controller expects it to be receiving LV power.

DFLKR_a030_diSystemMIA.d: Drive Interface (DI) System controller area network (CAN) communication to the differential locker (DFLK) is missing (MIA).
DFLKR_a030_diSystemMIA.m: DFLK is unable to utilize the unlocked probability signal from DI System and may not be able to auto-calibrate.

DFLKR_a037_rcmMIA.d: One or more of the controller area network (CAN) messages the differential locker (DFLK) expects to receive from the Restraint Control Module (RCM) is not received, indicating the RCM may be unavailab
DFLKR_a037_rcmMIA.m: DFLK is unable to utilize the unlocked probability signal from RCM.

DIF_a001_hwPhaseAgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase A.

DIF_a002_hwPhaseBgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase B.

DIF_a003_hwPhaseCgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase C.

DIF_a004_hwPhaseApeak.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase A peak over current detected in hardware.

DIF_a005_hwPhaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase B.

DIF_a006_hwPhaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase C.

DIF_a007_statorAnomalyDetected.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor stator anomaly detected.

DIF_a010_unintendedReset.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) resets for an unintended reason, such as a watchdog timeout.
DIF_a010_unintendedReset.m: The DI ECU is unavailable while it resets.

DIF_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage is not ready. There may be a condition affecting the DIx control board.
DIF_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.m: DIx may not function as expected.

DIF_a012_hvilNotClosed.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVIL not CLOSED.

DIF_a013_eccError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a RAM read or embedded conveyor controller (ECC) flash fetch issue.
DIF_a013_eccError.m: This alert is for diagnostic logging purposes only.

DIF_a014_activeDamping.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error with Active Driveline Damping (ADD).
DIF_a014_activeDamping.m: Gear train and drive line thuds and noise typically dampened by ADD may be more noticeable.

DIF_a016_safeStateApplied.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage has applied a safe state.
DIF_a016_safeStateApplied.m: Some DIx features may be disabled, or may not function expected. The Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIF_a017_hwPedalMonitor.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that leads to the pedal monitor (PM) disabling powertrain.
DIF_a017_hwPedalMonitor.m: Vehicle drive torque is immediately disabled.

DIF_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery undervoltage event.
DIF_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.m: The DI ECU triggers limp mode (see separate limpMode alerts) and cannot produce torque or power.

DIF_a021_hwBusOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in hardware.
DIF_a021_hwBusOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIF_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a 5V supply is under voltage.
DIF_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.m: Readings from 5V sensors may become erratic.

DIF_a024_selfTest.d: The Switch Off Path Test (SOPT) for Drive Unit has failed. The pathway between Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) torque measurements are different.
DIF_a024_selfTest.m: PM may not be able to shut down torque produced by Drive Unit.

DIF_a025_phaseApeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase A.

DIF_a026_phaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase B.

DIF_a027_phaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase C.

DIF_a028_phaseArms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase A.
DIF_a028_phaseArms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIF_a029_phaseBrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase B.
DIF_a029_phaseBrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIF_a030_phaseCrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase C.
DIF_a030_phaseCrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIF_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase A.
DIF_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase A hardware may need investigation.

DIF_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase B.
DIF_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase B hardware may need investigation.

DIF_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase A current sensor is irrational.
DIF_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIF_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase B current sensor is irrational.
DIF_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIF_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase current imbalance.
DIF_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIF_a036_busOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in software.
DIF_a036_busOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIF_a037_busUV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery under voltage.
DIF_a037_busUV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIF_a038_busVsensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery sensor bad.
DIF_a038_busVsensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode), faults (Model 3 only)

DIF_a040_difMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the front drive interface (DIF) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a040_difMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DIF.

DIF_a041_sdcMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) is not communicating to the Drive Inverter (DI), or there is an interface version mismatch. Service is needed.
DIF_a041_sdcMIA.m: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) may not be monitoring potential drive unit failures.

DIF_a043_outletOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet over temperature.
DIF_a043_outletOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIF_a044_outletUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet under temperature.
DIF_a044_outletUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIF_a045_outletSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet sensor bad.
DIF_a045_outletSensor.m: Unable to monitor DI coolant outlet sensor temperature

DIF_a046_statorOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator over temperature.

DIF_a047_statorSensor1.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator sensor 1 bad.
DIF_a047_statorSensor1.m: Stator sensor 2 will be used instead (if available)

DIF_a050_noStatorSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: No Stator sensor available.
DIF_a050_noStatorSensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIF_a052_dirMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the rear drive interface (DIR) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a052_dirMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DIR.

DIF_a053_unexpectedLatchState.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Hardware latched fault response in an unexpected state.
DIF_a053_unexpectedLatchState.m: In the event of a sudden LV loss at speed, latched fault response may not match expectations, exposing drive unit to unexpected torque

DIF_a054_driveInverterOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Inverter over temperature.

DIF_a055_trqCrossCheck.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) estimate different amounts of torque production, causing a Cross Check mismatch.
DIF_a055_trqCrossCheck.m: The vehicle may not accurately measure the level of torque being produced by the Drive Unit.

DIF_a056_ambientOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient over temperature.
DIF_a056_ambientOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIF_a057_ambientUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient under temperature.
DIF_a057_ambientUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIF_a058_ambientSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient sensor bad.
DIF_a058_ambientSensor.m: Unable to monitor ambient sensor temperature

DIF_a059_heatsinkOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink over temperature.

DIF_a060_heatsinkUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink under temperature.
DIF_a060_heatsinkUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIF_a061_heatsinkSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink sensor bad.
DIF_a061_heatsinkSensor.m: Unable to monitor heatsink sensor temperature

DIF_a062_systemLimpMode.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system limp mode.

DIF_a063_bbMIA.d: One or more of the messages the Drive Inverter Front (DIF) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) receives periodically from the Decoupled Brake Booster (BB) ECU is not received, indicating the BB ECU may be u
DIF_a063_bbMIA.m: Drive system may not function as expected.

DIF_a064_torqueIntervention.d: The torque production safety monitor has detected excess torque production and inhibited Drive Inverter (DIx) torque production for the remainder of the drive cycle.
DIF_a064_torqueIntervention.m: The torque production safety monitor will inhibit torque and excess heat production for the remainder of the drive cycle.

DIF_a069_rotorTempLimit.d: The rotor's temperature is too hot, limiting Drive Inverter (DIx) motor torque production.

DIF_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.d: The phase gate drive has detected four fault related signals from the induction motor or ten from the magnet motor.
DIF_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.m: The phase gate drive fault counter may block any power stage from enabling. The drive unit may not function as expected.

DIF_a073_motorSpeed.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) has detected the motor exceeding typical speed.
DIF_a073_motorSpeed.m: The motor is exceeding typical speed. The motor and Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIF_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.d: The motor speed measured by the Drive Unit resolver does not match the Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) wheel speed sensors.
DIF_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.m: The Drive Unit may not function as expected, including a reduction in vehicle power. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all

DIF_a076_lvSupplyOV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery overvoltage event.
DIF_a076_lvSupplyOV.m: No immediate impact. Could damage drive inverter LV hardware if LV is continuously too high.

DIF_a077_lvSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery power supply undervoltage event.
DIF_a077_lvSupplyUV.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIF_a078_adcRefLow.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference below the lower rational bound.
DIF_a078_adcRefLow.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIF_a079_adcRefHigh.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference above the upper rational bound.
DIF_a079_adcRefHigh.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIF_a086_activeDischargeOn.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Active discharge is on but bus voltage remains high.

DIF_a087_gtwMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the gateway (GTW) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a087_gtwMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the GTW.

DIF_a090_pmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the pedal monitor (PM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a090_pmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PM.

DIF_a091_espMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic stability program (ESP, also known as traction control) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a091_espMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the ESP.

DIF_a092_bmsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage (HV) battery management system (BMS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a092_bmsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the BMS.

DIF_a096_memoryError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory check error.
DIF_a096_memoryError.m: The DI processor may reset, and other functionality may be impacted due to memory corruption.

DIF_a097_eepromError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) read/write access error. Check for other alerts t
DIF_a097_eepromError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.

DIF_a098_intTimeTooLong.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a DIx thread executing later than expected. The DIx CPU may be diverting resources towards other tasks.
DIF_a098_intTimeTooLong.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

DIF_a103_lowFlow.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Low flow rate of fluid.

DIF_a107_fpgaError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA).
DIF_a107_fpgaError.m: The DIx Gate Drive Unit may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIF_a108_stateTrans.d: The Drive Unit is not able to transition to Drive state. There may be a condition preventing the Drive Unit from transitioning states.

DIF_a109_ahbWriteError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) processor has detected an internal exception. This alert does not on its own indicate a symptom or root cause.
DIF_a109_ahbWriteError.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

DIF_a110_brakeMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic parking brake (EPB) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a110_brakeMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EPB.

DIF_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) has reported phase sensor calibration is outside valid range.
DIF_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.m: This alert is primarily for logging purposes and appears in conjunction with other alerts.

DIF_a112_noPhaseCurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects no phase current.
