DIF_a112_noPhaseCurrent.m: Without phase current, DIx cannot produce motor torque to rotate the wheels. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all.

DIF_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.d: The three Drive Inverter (DIx) heat sink sensors are not functioning. The DIx heat sink sensors monitor temperature in the motor and fluid port.
DIF_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.m: Since it cannot detect temperature as expected, DIx may limit power output to an operational level. Drive Unit performance may be impacted.

DIF_a115_exceptionDataAbort2.d: The Drive Interface (DI) processor has detected an internal exception.
DIF_a115_exceptionDataAbort2.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

DIF_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.d: Drive Inverter Warning: High Speed Wear Counter Exceeded.
DIF_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.m: Silent alert to track mechanical wear

DIF_a117_diMIA.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DIF_a117_diMIA.m: DIx may not be able to communicate with the High Voltage Processor (HVP).

DIF_a119_hvpMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage processor (HVP) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a119_hvpMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the HVP, and active discharge may turn on.

DIF_a120_hvlinkMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVLINK CAN node MIA.

DIF_a121_motorControlRegulation.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor control is out of regulation.
DIF_a121_motorControlRegulation.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIF_a123_lossMotorControl.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Loss of motor control.
DIF_a123_lossMotorControl.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIF_a126_limpMode.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit limp mode triggered.

DIF_a127_gracefulPowerOff.d: Drive Unit Graceful Power Off (GPO) has been triggered. The Drive Unit will lose current input from the High Voltage (HV) battery.

DIF_a133_capacitorOT.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected the capacitor is over temperature.
DIF_a133_capacitorOT.m: The flow of current between the capacitors and DIx may not function as expected.

DIF_a134_wheelSpeedIrrational.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) receives irrational or missing data from a wheel speed sensor (WSS).
DIF_a134_wheelSpeedIrrational.m: This alert is primarily informational. If it persists, traction control may become unavailable.

DIF_a136_spiError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI), which monitors the pedal monitor.
DIF_a136_spiError.m: DIx may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIF_a140_eggright3MIA.d: The drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Etherloop Generalized Gateway Right (EGGRIGHT3) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a140_eggright3MIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EGGRIGHT3.

DIF_a144_configMismatch.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a Gateway configuration mismatch with the application currently running on the DI.
DIF_a144_configMismatch.m: The vehicle enters system limp mode and is unable to enter Drive.

DIF_a145_vcleftMIA.d: One or more of the messages the vehicle controller receives periodically from the Left Vehicle Controller (VCLEFT) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not received, indicating the VCLEFT ECU may be unava
DIF_a145_vcleftMIA.m: The vehicle controller is unable to monitor the state of the VCLEFT ECU.

DIF_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a quantity of torque production that indicates Drive Unit wear.
DIF_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.m: Powertrain requires service - OK to drive

DIF_a149_oilPumpFailure.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump failure.

DIF_a150_busVD.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has measured a voltage drop in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIF_a150_busVD.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIF_a152_linError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error communicating with the Local Interconnect Network (LIN).
DIF_a152_linError.m: DIx and the oil pump communicate through LIN. DIx may not communicate with the oil pump as expected.

DIF_a154_resolver.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) is not operating as expected. The resolver may have a condition affecting functionality.
DIF_a154_resolver.m: The resolver is not operating as expected. The DIx invertor control board may be unable to correctly detect the angle and speed of the rotor.

DIF_a155_vcfrontMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the front vehicle controller (VCFRONT) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a155_vcfrontMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCFRONT.

DIF_a155_vcsuperMIA.d: The drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Supermanifold Vehicle Controller (VCSUPER) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a155_vcsuperMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCSUPER.

DIF_a157_rcmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the restraints control module (RCM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a157_rcmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the RCM.

DIF_a158_ibstMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the iBooster (IBST, or brake assist) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a158_ibstMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the IBST.

DIF_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has detected a voltage anomaly in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIF_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIF_a161_epas3pMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic power assisted steering, gen 3 (EPAS3) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a161_epas3pMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EPAS3.

DIF_a172_statorOilTempOT.d: The drive inverter detects that the stator is over temperature for oil protection.

DIF_a174_unitMayNotRestart.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit may not allow drive on the next drive cycle due to HW damage.

DIF_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that prevents the drive unit(s) from generating excess heat.
DIF_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.m: The drive unit(s) cannot produce excess heat when requested.

DIF_a233_diMsgMissed.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has missed 3 to 5 messages from the main controller. DIx will maintain the last commanded torque value, then 0 Nm.
DIF_a233_diMsgMissed.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

DIF_a234_pcsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the power conversion system (PCS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a234_pcsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PCS.

DIF_a236_tasMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Tesla air suspension (TAS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIF_a236_tasMIA.m: Adaptive ride control may be unavailable

DIF_a240_clearFusesRoutine.d: This alert indicates the fuse clearing routine was attempted.

DIF_a242_spinDownLearning.d: Drive unit spindown learning calibration routine is in progress.

DIF_a244_mechSafeStateApplied.d: Alert for software trigger of mechanical safe state two-phase disconnect device.

DIF_a246_recoveryAtSpeedError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the at-speed recovery mechanism encountered an error.

DIF_a247_rotorOffsetError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the rotor is irrationally offset.
DIF_a247_rotorOffsetError.m: Diagnostic alert data only, may indicate reduction in torque or excess heat accuracy

DIF_a251_oilPumpService.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump service.

DIF_d085_driveInverterBoardFailure.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the board due to a switch short.
DIF_d085_driveInverterBoardFailure.m: There may be a temporary or permanent loss of drive unit function, reduction in vehicle torque output, or loss of propulsion.

DIF_d089_invalidTorqueCommand.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a Signal Not Available (SNA) or invalid torque command.
DIF_d089_invalidTorqueCommand.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function, or reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d104_resetUnintended.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an unintended reset.
DIF_d104_resetUnintended.m: There may be temporary loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d105_busVoltageSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an irrational bus voltage sensor reading.
DIF_d105_busVoltageSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d118_oilPumpCommsLost.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a communication lost with the oil pump.
DIF_d118_oilPumpCommsLost.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d124_oilPumpServiceRequired.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a low gearbox fluid pressure.
DIF_d124_oilPumpServiceRequired.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d125_currentSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an invalid or unavailable current sensor data.
DIF_d125_currentSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d129_oilPumpIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the oil pump.
DIF_d129_oilPumpIssue.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d130_positionSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an irrational position sensor reading.
DIF_d130_positionSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d131_currentSensorOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected that the current sensor offset has exceeded the expected range.
DIF_d131_currentSensorOffset.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d132_busUVIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an invalid bus voltage sensor output.
DIF_d132_busUVIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d135_rotatedStator.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a rotated stator.
DIF_d135_rotatedStator.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d137_currentSensorOutOfRange.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected current sensor readings that are out of range.
DIF_d137_currentSensorOutOfRange.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d138_inverterLVPowerSupplyLow.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected that the Low Voltage (LV) power supply is low.
DIF_d138_inverterLVPowerSupplyLow.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d139_unitEEPROMIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) Unit has detected an Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) error.
DIF_d139_unitEEPROMIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d141_coolingSystemIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the cooling system.
DIF_d141_coolingSystemIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d143_wheelSpeedCorrelationIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the wheel speed and motor speed correlation.
DIF_d143_wheelSpeedCorrelationIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d163_recoverableOvercurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a recoverable overcurrent event.
DIF_d163_recoverableOvercurrent.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d164_firmwareConfigMismatch.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a software incompatibility.
DIF_d164_firmwareConfigMismatch.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d165_statorOverTemperature.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected overtemperature issues with the stator.
DIF_d165_statorOverTemperature.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d166_rotorOverTemperature.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected overtemperature issues with the rotor.
DIF_d166_rotorOverTemperature.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIF_d167_tempSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected irrational data from the temperature sensor.
DIF_d167_tempSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_a001_hwPhaseAgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase A.

DIRE1L_a002_hwPhaseBgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase B.

DIRE1L_a003_hwPhaseCgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase C.

DIRE1L_a004_hwPhaseApeak.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase A peak over current detected in hardware.

DIRE1L_a005_hwPhaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase B.

DIRE1L_a006_hwPhaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase C.

DIRE1L_a007_statorAnomalyDetected.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor stator anomaly detected.

DIRE1L_a010_unintendedReset.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) resets for an unintended reason, such as a watchdog timeout.
DIRE1L_a010_unintendedReset.m: The DI ECU is unavailable while it resets.

DIRE1L_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage is not ready. There may be a condition affecting the DIx control board.
DIRE1L_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.m: DIx may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a012_hvilNotClosed.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVIL not CLOSED.

DIRE1L_a013_eccError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a RAM read or embedded conveyor controller (ECC) flash fetch issue.
DIRE1L_a013_eccError.m: This alert is for diagnostic logging purposes only.

DIRE1L_a014_activeDamping.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error with Active Driveline Damping (ADD).
DIRE1L_a014_activeDamping.m: Gear train and drive line thuds and noise typically dampened by ADD may be more noticeable.

DIRE1L_a016_safeStateApplied.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage has applied a safe state.
DIRE1L_a016_safeStateApplied.m: Some DIx features may be disabled, or may not function expected. The Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a017_hwPedalMonitor.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that leads to the pedal monitor (PM) disabling powertrain.
DIRE1L_a017_hwPedalMonitor.m: Vehicle drive torque is immediately disabled.

DIRE1L_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery undervoltage event.
DIRE1L_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.m: The DI ECU triggers limp mode (see separate limpMode alerts) and cannot produce torque or power.

DIRE1L_a021_hwBusOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in hardware.
DIRE1L_a021_hwBusOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1L_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a 5V supply is under voltage.
DIRE1L_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.m: Readings from 5V sensors may become erratic.

DIRE1L_a024_selfTest.d: The Switch Off Path Test (SOPT) for Drive Unit has failed. The pathway between Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) torque measurements are different.
DIRE1L_a024_selfTest.m: PM may not be able to shut down torque produced by Drive Unit.

DIRE1L_a025_phaseApeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase A.

DIRE1L_a026_phaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase B.

DIRE1L_a027_phaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase C.

DIRE1L_a028_phaseArms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase A.
DIRE1L_a028_phaseArms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE1L_a029_phaseBrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase B.
DIRE1L_a029_phaseBrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE1L_a030_phaseCrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase C.
DIRE1L_a030_phaseCrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE1L_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase A.
DIRE1L_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase A hardware may need investigation.

DIRE1L_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase B.
DIRE1L_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase B hardware may need investigation.

DIRE1L_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase A current sensor is irrational.
DIRE1L_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIRE1L_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase B current sensor is irrational.
DIRE1L_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIRE1L_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase current imbalance.
DIRE1L_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1L_a036_busOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in software.
DIRE1L_a036_busOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1L_a037_busUV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery under voltage.
DIRE1L_a037_busUV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1L_a038_busVsensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery sensor bad.
DIRE1L_a038_busVsensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode), faults (Model 3 only)

DIRE1L_a041_sdcMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) is not communicating to the Drive Inverter (DI), or there is an interface version mismatch. Service is needed.
DIRE1L_a041_sdcMIA.m: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) may not be monitoring potential drive unit failures.

DIRE1L_a043_outletOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet over temperature.
DIRE1L_a043_outletOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIRE1L_a044_outletUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet under temperature.
DIRE1L_a044_outletUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE1L_a045_outletSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet sensor bad.
DIRE1L_a045_outletSensor.m: Unable to monitor DI coolant outlet sensor temperature

DIRE1L_a046_statorOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator over temperature.

DIRE1L_a047_statorSensor1.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator sensor 1 bad.
DIRE1L_a047_statorSensor1.m: Stator sensor 2 will be used instead (if available)

DIRE1L_a050_noStatorSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: No Stator sensor available.
DIRE1L_a050_noStatorSensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1L_a053_unexpectedLatchState.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Hardware latched fault response in an unexpected state.
DIRE1L_a053_unexpectedLatchState.m: In the event of a sudden LV loss at speed, latched fault response may not match expectations, exposing drive unit to unexpected torque

DIRE1L_a054_driveInverterOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Inverter over temperature.

DIRE1L_a055_trqCrossCheck.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) estimate different amounts of torque production, causing a Cross Check mismatch.
DIRE1L_a055_trqCrossCheck.m: The vehicle may not accurately measure the level of torque being produced by the Drive Unit.

DIRE1L_a056_ambientOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient over temperature.
DIRE1L_a056_ambientOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIRE1L_a057_ambientUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient under temperature.
DIRE1L_a057_ambientUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE1L_a058_ambientSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient sensor bad.
DIRE1L_a058_ambientSensor.m: Unable to monitor ambient sensor temperature

DIRE1L_a059_heatsinkOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink over temperature.

DIRE1L_a060_heatsinkUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink under temperature.
DIRE1L_a060_heatsinkUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE1L_a061_heatsinkSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink sensor bad.
DIRE1L_a061_heatsinkSensor.m: Unable to monitor heatsink sensor temperature

DIRE1L_a062_systemLimpMode.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system limp mode.

DIRE1L_a064_torqueIntervention.d: The torque production safety monitor has detected excess torque production and inhibited Drive Inverter (DIx) torque production for the remainder of the drive cycle.
DIRE1L_a064_torqueIntervention.m: The torque production safety monitor will inhibit torque and excess heat production for the remainder of the drive cycle.

DIRE1L_a069_rotorTempLimit.d: The rotor's temperature is too hot, limiting Drive Inverter (DIx) motor torque production.

DIRE1L_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.d: The phase gate drive has detected four fault related signals from the induction motor or ten from the magnet motor.
DIRE1L_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.m: The phase gate drive fault counter may block any power stage from enabling. The drive unit may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a073_motorSpeed.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) has detected the motor exceeding typical speed.
DIRE1L_a073_motorSpeed.m: The motor is exceeding typical speed. The motor and Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.d: The motor speed measured by the Drive Unit resolver does not match the Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) wheel speed sensors.
DIRE1L_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.m: The Drive Unit may not function as expected, including a reduction in vehicle power. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all

DIRE1L_a076_lvSupplyOV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery overvoltage event.
DIRE1L_a076_lvSupplyOV.m: No immediate impact. Could damage drive inverter LV hardware if LV is continuously too high.

DIRE1L_a077_lvSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery power supply undervoltage event.
DIRE1L_a077_lvSupplyUV.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1L_a078_adcRefLow.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference below the lower rational bound.
DIRE1L_a078_adcRefLow.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIRE1L_a079_adcRefHigh.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference above the upper rational bound.
DIRE1L_a079_adcRefHigh.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIRE1L_a086_activeDischargeOn.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Active discharge is on but bus voltage remains high.

DIRE1L_a087_gtwMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the gateway (GTW) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1L_a087_gtwMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the GTW.

DIRE1L_a090_pmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the pedal monitor (PM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1L_a090_pmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PM.

DIRE1L_a091_ebsMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: EBS CAN node MIA.

DIRE1L_a092_bmsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage (HV) battery management system (BMS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1L_a092_bmsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the BMS.

DIRE1L_a096_memoryError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory check error.
DIRE1L_a096_memoryError.m: The DI processor may reset, and other functionality may be impacted due to memory corruption.

DIRE1L_a097_eepromError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) read/write access error. Check for other alerts t
DIRE1L_a097_eepromError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.

DIRE1L_a098_intTimeTooLong.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a DIx thread executing later than expected. The DIx CPU may be diverting resources towards other tasks.
DIRE1L_a098_intTimeTooLong.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

DIRE1L_a103_lowFlow.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Low flow rate of fluid.
DIRE1L_a103_lowFlow.m: Rear Axle 1 Left Motor power may be limited

DIRE1L_a107_fpgaError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA).
DIRE1L_a107_fpgaError.m: The DIx Gate Drive Unit may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a108_stateTrans.d: The Drive Unit is not able to transition to Drive state. There may be a condition preventing the Drive Unit from transitioning states.

DIRE1L_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) has reported phase sensor calibration is outside valid range.
DIRE1L_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.m: This alert is primarily for logging purposes and appears in conjunction with other alerts.

DIRE1L_a112_noPhaseCurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects no phase current.
DIRE1L_a112_noPhaseCurrent.m: Without phase current, DIx cannot produce motor torque to rotate the wheels. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all.

DIRE1L_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.d: The three Drive Inverter (DIx) heat sink sensors are not functioning. The DIx heat sink sensors monitor temperature in the motor and fluid port.
DIRE1L_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.m: Since it cannot detect temperature as expected, DIx may limit power output to an operational level. Drive Unit performance may be impacted.

DIRE1L_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.d: Drive Inverter Warning: High Speed Wear Counter Exceeded.
DIRE1L_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.m: Silent alert to track mechanical wear

DIRE1L_a117_diMIA.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DIRE1L_a117_diMIA.m: DIx may not be able to communicate with the High Voltage Processor (HVP).

DIRE1L_a119_hvpMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage processor (HVP) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1L_a119_hvpMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the HVP, and active discharge may turn on.

DIRE1L_a120_hvlinkMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVLINK CAN node MIA.

DIRE1L_a121_motorControlRegulation.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor control is out of regulation.
DIRE1L_a121_motorControlRegulation.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1L_a123_lossMotorControl.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Loss of motor control.
DIRE1L_a123_lossMotorControl.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1L_a126_limpMode.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit limp mode triggered.
DIRE1L_a126_limpMode.m: Rear Axle 1 Left Motor power reduced

DIRE1L_a127_gracefulPowerOff.d: Drive Unit Graceful Power Off (GPO) has been triggered. The Drive Unit will lose current input from the High Voltage (HV) battery.

DIRE1L_a133_capacitorOT.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected the capacitor is over temperature.
DIRE1L_a133_capacitorOT.m: The flow of current between the capacitors and DIx may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a136_spiError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI), which monitors the pedal monitor.
DIRE1L_a136_spiError.m: DIx may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIRE1L_a144_configMismatch.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a Gateway configuration mismatch with the application currently running on the DI.
DIRE1L_a144_configMismatch.m: The vehicle enters system limp mode and is unable to enter Drive.

DIRE1L_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a quantity of torque production that indicates Drive Unit wear.
DIRE1L_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.m: Powertrain requires service - OK to drive

DIRE1L_a149_oilPumpFailure.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump failure.

DIRE1L_a150_busVD.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has measured a voltage drop in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIRE1L_a150_busVD.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIRE1L_a152_linError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error communicating with the Local Interconnect Network (LIN).
DIRE1L_a152_linError.m: DIx and the oil pump communicate through LIN. DIx may not communicate with the oil pump as expected.

DIRE1L_a154_resolver.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) is not operating as expected. The resolver may have a condition affecting functionality.
DIRE1L_a154_resolver.m: The resolver is not operating as expected. The DIx invertor control board may be unable to correctly detect the angle and speed of the rotor.

DIRE1L_a155_vcleftsMIA.d: The Left Secondary Vehicle Controller (VCLEFTS) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DIRE1L_a155_vcleftsMIA.m: The Drive Unit may not operate as expected.

DIRE1L_a157_rcmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the restraints control module (RCM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1L_a157_rcmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the RCM.

DIRE1L_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has detected a voltage anomaly in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIRE1L_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIRE1L_a172_statorOilTempOT.d: The drive inverter detects that the stator is over temperature for oil protection.

DIRE1L_a174_unitMayNotRestart.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit may not allow drive on the next drive cycle due to HW damage.

DIRE1L_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that prevents the drive unit(s) from generating excess heat.
DIRE1L_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.m: The drive unit(s) cannot produce excess heat when requested.

DIRE1L_a234_pcsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the power conversion system (PCS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1L_a234_pcsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PCS.

DIRE1L_a240_clearFusesRoutine.d: This alert indicates the fuse clearing routine was attempted.

DIRE1L_a242_spinDownLearning.d: Drive unit spindown learning calibration routine is in progress.

DIRE1L_a244_mechSafeStateApplied.d: Alert for software trigger of mechanical safe state two-phase disconnect device.

DIRE1L_a246_recoveryAtSpeedError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the at-speed recovery mechanism encountered an error.

DIRE1L_a247_rotorOffsetError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the rotor is irrationally offset.
DIRE1L_a247_rotorOffsetError.m: Diagnostic alert data only, may indicate reduction in torque or excess heat accuracy

DIRE1L_a251_oilPumpService.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump service.

DIRE1L_d085_driveInverterBoardFailure.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the board due to a switch short.
DIRE1L_d085_driveInverterBoardFailure.m: There may be a temporary or permanent loss of drive unit function, reduction in vehicle torque output, or loss of propulsion.

DIRE1L_d089_invalidTorqueCommand.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a Signal Not Available (SNA) or invalid torque command.
DIRE1L_d089_invalidTorqueCommand.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function, or reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d104_resetUnintended.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an unintended reset.
DIRE1L_d104_resetUnintended.m: There may be temporary loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d105_busVoltageSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an irrational bus voltage sensor reading.
DIRE1L_d105_busVoltageSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d118_oilPumpCommsLost.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a communication loss with the oil pump.
DIRE1L_d118_oilPumpCommsLost.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d124_oilPumpServiceRequired.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a low gearbox fluid pressure.
DIRE1L_d124_oilPumpServiceRequired.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d125_currentSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an invalid or unavailable current sensor data.
DIRE1L_d125_currentSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d129_oilPumpIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the oil pump.
DIRE1L_d129_oilPumpIssue.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d130_positionSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an irrational position sensor reading.
DIRE1L_d130_positionSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d131_currentSensorOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected the current sensor offset exceeded the expected range.
DIRE1L_d131_currentSensorOffset.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d132_busUVIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an invalid bus voltage sensor output.
DIRE1L_d132_busUVIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d135_rotatedStator.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a rotated stator.
DIRE1L_d135_rotatedStator.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d137_currentSensorOutOfRange.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected current sensor readings that are out of range.
DIRE1L_d137_currentSensorOutOfRange.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d138_inverterLVPowerSupplyLow.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected that the Low Voltage (LV) power supply is low.
DIRE1L_d138_inverterLVPowerSupplyLow.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d139_unitEEPROMIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) Unit has detected an Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) error.
DIRE1L_d139_unitEEPROMIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d141_coolingSystemIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the cooling system.
DIRE1L_d141_coolingSystemIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d143_wheelSpeedCorrelationIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the wheel speed and motor speed correlation.
DIRE1L_d143_wheelSpeedCorrelationIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d163_recoverableOvercurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a recoverable overcurrent event.
DIRE1L_d163_recoverableOvercurrent.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d164_firmwareConfigMismatch.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a software incompatibility.
DIRE1L_d164_firmwareConfigMismatch.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d165_statorOverTemperature.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected overtemperature issues with the stator.
DIRE1L_d165_statorOverTemperature.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d166_rotorOverTemperature.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected overtemperature issues with the rotor.
DIRE1L_d166_rotorOverTemperature.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1L_d167_tempSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected irrational data from the temperature sensor.
DIRE1L_d167_tempSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_a001_hwPhaseAgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase A.

DIRE1R_a002_hwPhaseBgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase B.

DIRE1R_a003_hwPhaseCgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase C.

DIRE1R_a004_hwPhaseApeak.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase A peak over current detected in hardware.

DIRE1R_a005_hwPhaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase B.

DIRE1R_a006_hwPhaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase C.

DIRE1R_a007_statorAnomalyDetected.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor stator anomaly detected.

DIRE1R_a010_unintendedReset.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) resets for an unintended reason, such as a watchdog timeout.
DIRE1R_a010_unintendedReset.m: The DI ECU is unavailable while it resets.

DIRE1R_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage is not ready. There may be a condition affecting the DIx control board.
DIRE1R_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.m: DIx may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a012_hvilNotClosed.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVIL not CLOSED.

DIRE1R_a013_eccError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a RAM read or embedded conveyor controller (ECC) flash fetch issue.
DIRE1R_a013_eccError.m: This alert is for diagnostic logging purposes only.

DIRE1R_a014_activeDamping.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error with Active Driveline Damping (ADD).
DIRE1R_a014_activeDamping.m: Gear train and drive line thuds and noise typically dampened by ADD may be more noticeable.

DIRE1R_a016_safeStateApplied.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage has applied a safe state.
DIRE1R_a016_safeStateApplied.m: Some DIx features may be disabled, or may not function expected. The Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a017_hwPedalMonitor.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that leads to the pedal monitor (PM) disabling powertrain.
DIRE1R_a017_hwPedalMonitor.m: Vehicle drive torque is immediately disabled.

DIRE1R_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery undervoltage event.
DIRE1R_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.m: The DI ECU triggers limp mode (see separate limpMode alerts) and cannot produce torque or power.

DIRE1R_a021_hwBusOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in hardware.
DIRE1R_a021_hwBusOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1R_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a 5V supply is under voltage.
DIRE1R_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.m: Readings from 5V sensors may become erratic.

DIRE1R_a024_selfTest.d: The Switch Off Path Test (SOPT) for Drive Unit has failed. The pathway between Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) torque measurements are different.
DIRE1R_a024_selfTest.m: PM may not be able to shut down torque produced by Drive Unit.

DIRE1R_a025_phaseApeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase A.

DIRE1R_a026_phaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase B.

DIRE1R_a027_phaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase C.

DIRE1R_a028_phaseArms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase A.
DIRE1R_a028_phaseArms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE1R_a029_phaseBrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase B.
DIRE1R_a029_phaseBrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE1R_a030_phaseCrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase C.
DIRE1R_a030_phaseCrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE1R_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase A.
DIRE1R_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase A hardware may need investigation.

DIRE1R_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase B.
DIRE1R_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase B hardware may need investigation.

DIRE1R_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase A current sensor is irrational.
DIRE1R_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIRE1R_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase B current sensor is irrational.
DIRE1R_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIRE1R_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase current imbalance.
DIRE1R_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1R_a036_busOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in software.
DIRE1R_a036_busOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1R_a037_busUV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery under voltage.
DIRE1R_a037_busUV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1R_a038_busVsensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery sensor bad.
DIRE1R_a038_busVsensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode), faults (Model 3 only)

DIRE1R_a041_sdcMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) is not communicating to the Drive Inverter (DI), or there is an interface version mismatch. Service is needed.
DIRE1R_a041_sdcMIA.m: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) may not be monitoring potential drive unit failures.

DIRE1R_a043_outletOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet over temperature.
DIRE1R_a043_outletOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIRE1R_a044_outletUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet under temperature.
DIRE1R_a044_outletUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE1R_a045_outletSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet sensor bad.
DIRE1R_a045_outletSensor.m: Unable to monitor DI coolant outlet sensor temperature

DIRE1R_a046_statorOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator over temperature.

DIRE1R_a047_statorSensor1.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator sensor 1 bad.
DIRE1R_a047_statorSensor1.m: Stator sensor 2 will be used instead (if available)

DIRE1R_a050_noStatorSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: No Stator sensor available.
DIRE1R_a050_noStatorSensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1R_a053_unexpectedLatchState.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Hardware latched fault response in an unexpected state.
DIRE1R_a053_unexpectedLatchState.m: In the event of a sudden LV loss at speed, latched fault response may not match expectations, exposing drive unit to unexpected torque

DIRE1R_a054_driveInverterOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Inverter over temperature.

DIRE1R_a055_trqCrossCheck.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) estimate different amounts of torque production, causing a Cross Check mismatch.
DIRE1R_a055_trqCrossCheck.m: The vehicle may not accurately measure the level of torque being produced by the Drive Unit.

DIRE1R_a056_ambientOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient over temperature.
DIRE1R_a056_ambientOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIRE1R_a057_ambientUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient under temperature.
DIRE1R_a057_ambientUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE1R_a058_ambientSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient sensor bad.
DIRE1R_a058_ambientSensor.m: Unable to monitor ambient sensor temperature

DIRE1R_a059_heatsinkOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink over temperature.

DIRE1R_a060_heatsinkUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink under temperature.
DIRE1R_a060_heatsinkUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE1R_a061_heatsinkSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink sensor bad.
DIRE1R_a061_heatsinkSensor.m: Unable to monitor heatsink sensor temperature

DIRE1R_a062_systemLimpMode.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system limp mode.

DIRE1R_a064_torqueIntervention.d: The torque production safety monitor has detected excess torque production and inhibited Drive Inverter (DIx) torque production for the remainder of the drive cycle.
DIRE1R_a064_torqueIntervention.m: The torque production safety monitor will inhibit torque and excess heat production for the remainder of the drive cycle.

DIRE1R_a069_rotorTempLimit.d: The rotor's temperature is too hot, limiting Drive Inverter (DIx) motor torque production.

DIRE1R_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.d: The phase gate drive has detected four fault related signals from the induction motor or ten from the magnet motor.
DIRE1R_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.m: The phase gate drive fault counter may block any power stage from enabling. The drive unit may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a073_motorSpeed.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) has detected the motor exceeding typical speed.
DIRE1R_a073_motorSpeed.m: The motor is exceeding typical speed. The motor and Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.d: The motor speed measured by the Drive Unit resolver does not match the Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) wheel speed sensors.
DIRE1R_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.m: The Drive Unit may not function as expected, including a reduction in vehicle power. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all

DIRE1R_a076_lvSupplyOV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery overvoltage event.
DIRE1R_a076_lvSupplyOV.m: No immediate impact. Could damage drive inverter LV hardware if LV is continuously too high.

DIRE1R_a077_lvSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery power supply undervoltage event.
DIRE1R_a077_lvSupplyUV.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1R_a078_adcRefLow.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference below the lower rational bound.
DIRE1R_a078_adcRefLow.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIRE1R_a079_adcRefHigh.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference above the upper rational bound.
DIRE1R_a079_adcRefHigh.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIRE1R_a086_activeDischargeOn.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Active discharge is on but bus voltage remains high.

DIRE1R_a087_gtwMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the gateway (GTW) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1R_a087_gtwMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the GTW.

DIRE1R_a090_pmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the pedal monitor (PM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1R_a090_pmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PM.

DIRE1R_a091_ebsMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: EBS CAN node MIA.

DIRE1R_a092_bmsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage (HV) battery management system (BMS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1R_a092_bmsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the BMS.

DIRE1R_a096_memoryError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory check error.
DIRE1R_a096_memoryError.m: The DI processor may reset, and other functionality may be impacted due to memory corruption.

DIRE1R_a097_eepromError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) read/write access error. Check for other alerts t
DIRE1R_a097_eepromError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.

DIRE1R_a098_intTimeTooLong.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a DIx thread executing later than expected. The DIx CPU may be diverting resources towards other tasks.
DIRE1R_a098_intTimeTooLong.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

DIRE1R_a103_lowFlow.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Low flow rate of fluid.
DIRE1R_a103_lowFlow.m: Rear Axle 1 Right Motor power may be limited

DIRE1R_a107_fpgaError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA).
DIRE1R_a107_fpgaError.m: The DIx Gate Drive Unit may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a108_stateTrans.d: The Drive Unit is not able to transition to Drive state. There may be a condition preventing the Drive Unit from transitioning states.

DIRE1R_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) has reported phase sensor calibration is outside valid range.
DIRE1R_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.m: This alert is primarily for logging purposes and appears in conjunction with other alerts.

DIRE1R_a112_noPhaseCurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects no phase current.
DIRE1R_a112_noPhaseCurrent.m: Without phase current, DIx cannot produce motor torque to rotate the wheels. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all.

DIRE1R_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.d: The three Drive Inverter (DIx) heat sink sensors are not functioning. The DIx heat sink sensors monitor temperature in the motor and fluid port.
DIRE1R_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.m: Since it cannot detect temperature as expected, DIx may limit power output to an operational level. Drive Unit performance may be impacted.

DIRE1R_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.d: Drive Inverter Warning: High Speed Wear Counter Exceeded.
DIRE1R_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.m: Silent alert to track mechanical wear

DIRE1R_a117_diMIA.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DIRE1R_a117_diMIA.m: DIx may not be able to communicate with the High Voltage Processor (HVP).

DIRE1R_a119_hvpMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage processor (HVP) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1R_a119_hvpMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the HVP, and active discharge may turn on.

DIRE1R_a120_hvlinkMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVLINK CAN node MIA.

DIRE1R_a121_motorControlRegulation.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor control is out of regulation.
DIRE1R_a121_motorControlRegulation.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE1R_a123_lossMotorControl.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Loss of motor control.
DIRE1R_a123_lossMotorControl.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE1R_a126_limpMode.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit limp mode triggered.
DIRE1R_a126_limpMode.m: Rear Axle 1 Right Motor power reduced

DIRE1R_a127_gracefulPowerOff.d: Drive Unit Graceful Power Off (GPO) has been triggered. The Drive Unit will lose current input from the High Voltage (HV) battery.

DIRE1R_a133_capacitorOT.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected the capacitor is over temperature.
DIRE1R_a133_capacitorOT.m: The flow of current between the capacitors and DIx may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a136_spiError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI), which monitors the pedal monitor.
DIRE1R_a136_spiError.m: DIx may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIRE1R_a144_configMismatch.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a Gateway configuration mismatch with the application currently running on the DI.
DIRE1R_a144_configMismatch.m: The vehicle enters system limp mode and is unable to enter Drive.

DIRE1R_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a quantity of torque production that indicates Drive Unit wear.
DIRE1R_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.m: Powertrain requires service - OK to drive

DIRE1R_a149_oilPumpFailure.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump failure.

DIRE1R_a150_busVD.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has measured a voltage drop in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIRE1R_a150_busVD.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIRE1R_a152_linError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error communicating with the Local Interconnect Network (LIN).
DIRE1R_a152_linError.m: DIx and the oil pump communicate through LIN. DIx may not communicate with the oil pump as expected.

DIRE1R_a154_resolver.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) is not operating as expected. The resolver may have a condition affecting functionality.
DIRE1R_a154_resolver.m: The resolver is not operating as expected. The DIx invertor control board may be unable to correctly detect the angle and speed of the rotor.

DIRE1R_a155_vcleftsMIA.d: The Left Secondary Vehicle Controller (VCLEFTS) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DIRE1R_a155_vcleftsMIA.m: The Drive Unit may not operate as expected.

DIRE1R_a157_rcmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the restraints control module (RCM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1R_a157_rcmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the RCM.

DIRE1R_a159_oilPumpB.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump operation abnormal.

DIRE1R_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has detected a voltage anomaly in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIRE1R_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIRE1R_a172_statorOilTempOT.d: The drive inverter detects that the stator is over temperature for oil protection.

DIRE1R_a174_unitMayNotRestart.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit may not allow drive on the next drive cycle due to HW damage.

DIRE1R_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that prevents the drive unit(s) from generating excess heat.
DIRE1R_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.m: The drive unit(s) cannot produce excess heat when requested.

DIRE1R_a234_pcsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the power conversion system (PCS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE1R_a234_pcsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PCS.

DIRE1R_a240_clearFusesRoutine.d: This alert indicates the fuse clearing routine was attempted.

DIRE1R_a242_spinDownLearning.d: Drive unit spindown learning calibration routine is in progress.

DIRE1R_a244_mechSafeStateApplied.d: Alert for software trigger of mechanical safe state two-phase disconnect device.

DIRE1R_a246_recoveryAtSpeedError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the at-speed recovery mechanism encountered an error.

DIRE1R_a247_rotorOffsetError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the rotor is irrationally offset.
DIRE1R_a247_rotorOffsetError.m: Diagnostic alert data only, may indicate reduction in torque or excess heat accuracy

DIRE1R_a251_oilPumpService.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump service.

DIRE1R_d085_driveInverterBoardFailure.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the board due to a switch short.
DIRE1R_d085_driveInverterBoardFailure.m: There may be a temporary or permanent loss of drive unit function, reduction in vehicle torque output, or loss of propulsion.

DIRE1R_d089_invalidTorqueCommand.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a Signal Not Available (SNA) or invalid torque command.
DIRE1R_d089_invalidTorqueCommand.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function, or reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d104_resetUnintended.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an unintended reset.
DIRE1R_d104_resetUnintended.m: There may be temporary loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d105_busVoltageSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an irrational bus voltage sensor reading.
DIRE1R_d105_busVoltageSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d118_oilPumpCommsLost.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a communication loss with the oil pump.
DIRE1R_d118_oilPumpCommsLost.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d124_oilPumpServiceRequired.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a low gearbox fluid pressure.
DIRE1R_d124_oilPumpServiceRequired.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d125_currentSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an invalid or unavailable current sensor data.
DIRE1R_d125_currentSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d129_oilPumpIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the oil pump.
DIRE1R_d129_oilPumpIssue.m: The drive unit function may be degraded and there may be a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d130_positionSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an irrational position sensor reading.
DIRE1R_d130_positionSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d131_currentSensorOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected the current sensor offset exceeded the expected range.
DIRE1R_d131_currentSensorOffset.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d132_busUVIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an invalid bus voltage sensor output.
DIRE1R_d132_busUVIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d135_rotatedStator.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a rotated stator.
DIRE1R_d135_rotatedStator.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d137_currentSensorOutOfRange.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected current sensor readings that are out of range.
DIRE1R_d137_currentSensorOutOfRange.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d138_inverterLVPowerSupplyLow.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected that the Low Voltage (LV) power supply is low.
DIRE1R_d138_inverterLVPowerSupplyLow.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d139_unitEEPROMIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) Unit has detected an Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) error.
DIRE1R_d139_unitEEPROMIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d141_coolingSystemIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the cooling system.
DIRE1R_d141_coolingSystemIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d143_wheelSpeedCorrelationIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected an issue with the wheel speed and motor speed correlation.
DIRE1R_d143_wheelSpeedCorrelationIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d163_recoverableOvercurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a recoverable overcurrent event.
DIRE1R_d163_recoverableOvercurrent.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d164_firmwareConfigMismatch.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected a software incompatibility.
DIRE1R_d164_firmwareConfigMismatch.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d165_statorOverTemperature.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected overtemperature issues with the stator.
DIRE1R_d165_statorOverTemperature.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d166_rotorOverTemperature.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected overtemperature issues with the rotor.
DIRE1R_d166_rotorOverTemperature.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE1R_d167_tempSensorIssue.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) has detected irrational data from the temperature sensor.
DIRE1R_d167_tempSensorIssue.m: There may be a loss of drive unit function and a reduction in vehicle torque output.

DIRE2_a001_hwPhaseAgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase A.

DIRE2_a002_hwPhaseBgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase B.

DIRE2_a003_hwPhaseCgateDrive.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a condition affecting functionality at the gate driver on Phase C.

DIRE2_a004_hwPhaseApeak.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase A peak over current detected in hardware.

DIRE2_a005_hwPhaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase B.

DIRE2_a006_hwPhaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) current sensor hardware comparator has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) on Phase C.

DIRE2_a007_statorAnomalyDetected.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor stator anomaly detected.

DIRE2_a010_unintendedReset.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) resets for an unintended reason, such as a watchdog timeout.
DIRE2_a010_unintendedReset.m: The DI ECU is unavailable while it resets.

DIRE2_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage is not ready. There may be a condition affecting the DIx control board.
DIRE2_a011_swPowerStageNotReady.m: DIx may not function as expected.

DIRE2_a012_hvilNotClosed.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVIL not CLOSED.

DIRE2_a013_eccError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a RAM read or embedded conveyor controller (ECC) flash fetch issue.
DIRE2_a013_eccError.m: This alert is for diagnostic logging purposes only.

DIRE2_a014_activeDamping.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error with Active Driveline Damping (ADD).
DIRE2_a014_activeDamping.m: Gear train and drive line thuds and noise typically dampened by ADD may be more noticeable.

DIRE2_a016_safeStateApplied.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) power stage has applied a safe state.
DIRE2_a016_safeStateApplied.m: Some DIx features may be disabled, or may not function expected. The Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIRE2_a017_hwPedalMonitor.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that leads to the pedal monitor (PM) disabling powertrain.
DIRE2_a017_hwPedalMonitor.m: Vehicle drive torque is immediately disabled.

DIRE2_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery undervoltage event.
DIRE2_a018_hwLVSupplyUV.m: The DI ECU triggers limp mode (see separate limpMode alerts) and cannot produce torque or power.

DIRE2_a021_hwBusOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in hardware.
DIRE2_a021_hwBusOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE2_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a 5V supply is under voltage.
DIRE2_a022_hw5vSupplyUV.m: Readings from 5V sensors may become erratic.

DIRE2_a024_selfTest.d: The Switch Off Path Test (SOPT) for Drive Unit has failed. The pathway between Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) torque measurements are different.
DIRE2_a024_selfTest.m: PM may not be able to shut down torque produced by Drive Unit.

DIRE2_a025_phaseApeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase A.

DIRE2_a026_phaseBpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase B.

DIRE2_a027_phaseCpeak.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an overcurrent event (or peak) in Phase C.

DIRE2_a028_phaseArms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase A.
DIRE2_a028_phaseArms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE2_a029_phaseBrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase B.
DIRE2_a029_phaseBrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE2_a030_phaseCrms.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor Root Mean Squared (RMS) current has exceeded the expected RMS threshold on Phase C.
DIRE2_a030_phaseCrms.m: DIx may not function as expected, including vehicle power being limited.

DIRE2_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase A.
DIRE2_a031_phaseAcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase A hardware may need investigation.

DIRE2_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected the current sensor offset has exceeded the threshold on Phase B.
DIRE2_a032_phaseBcurrentOffset.m: This alert is intended for logging purposes. Phase B hardware may need investigation.

DIRE2_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase A current sensor is irrational.
DIRE2_a033_phaseAcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIRE2_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Phase B current sensor is irrational.
DIRE2_a034_phaseBcurrentSensor.m: If the alert asserts at the beginning of a drive cycle, DIx may not enable. If the alert asserts during a drive cycle, DIx may be disabled. DIx may no

DIRE2_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Phase current imbalance.
DIRE2_a035_phaseCurrentBalance.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE2_a036_busOV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery over voltage detected in software.
DIRE2_a036_busOV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE2_a037_busUV.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery under voltage.
DIRE2_a037_busUV.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIRE2_a038_busVsensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Battery sensor bad.
DIRE2_a038_busVsensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode), faults (Model 3 only)

DIRE2_a041_sdcMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) is not communicating to the Drive Inverter (DI), or there is an interface version mismatch. Service is needed.
DIRE2_a041_sdcMIA.m: The Secondary Disconnect Controller (SDC) may not be monitoring potential drive unit failures.

DIRE2_a043_outletOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet over temperature.
DIRE2_a043_outletOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIRE2_a044_outletUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet under temperature.
DIRE2_a044_outletUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE2_a045_outletSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Outlet sensor bad.
DIRE2_a045_outletSensor.m: Unable to monitor DI coolant outlet sensor temperature

DIRE2_a046_statorOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator over temperature.

DIRE2_a047_statorSensor1.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Stator sensor 1 bad.
DIRE2_a047_statorSensor1.m: Stator sensor 2 will be used instead (if available)

DIRE2_a050_noStatorSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: No Stator sensor available.
DIRE2_a050_noStatorSensor.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE2_a053_unexpectedLatchState.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Hardware latched fault response in an unexpected state.
DIRE2_a053_unexpectedLatchState.m: In the event of a sudden LV loss at speed, latched fault response may not match expectations, exposing drive unit to unexpected torque

DIRE2_a054_driveInverterOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Inverter over temperature.

DIRE2_a055_trqCrossCheck.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) and Pedal Monitor (PM) estimate different amounts of torque production, causing a Cross Check mismatch.
DIRE2_a055_trqCrossCheck.m: The vehicle may not accurately measure the level of torque being produced by the Drive Unit.

DIRE2_a056_ambientOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient over temperature.
DIRE2_a056_ambientOT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too high.

DIRE2_a057_ambientUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient under temperature.
DIRE2_a057_ambientUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE2_a058_ambientSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Ambient sensor bad.
DIRE2_a058_ambientSensor.m: Unable to monitor ambient sensor temperature

DIRE2_a059_heatsinkOT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink over temperature.

DIRE2_a060_heatsinkUT.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink under temperature.
DIRE2_a060_heatsinkUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIRE2_a061_heatsinkSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink sensor bad.
DIRE2_a061_heatsinkSensor.m: Unable to monitor heatsink sensor temperature

DIRE2_a062_systemLimpMode.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system limp mode.

DIRE2_a064_torqueIntervention.d: The torque production safety monitor has detected excess torque production and inhibited Drive Inverter (DIx) torque production for the remainder of the drive cycle.
DIRE2_a064_torqueIntervention.m: The torque production safety monitor will inhibit torque and excess heat production for the remainder of the drive cycle.

DIRE2_a069_rotorTempLimit.d: The rotor's temperature is too hot, limiting Drive Inverter (DIx) motor torque production.

DIRE2_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.d: The phase gate drive has detected four fault related signals from the induction motor or ten from the magnet motor.
DIRE2_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.m: The phase gate drive fault counter may block any power stage from enabling. The drive unit may not function as expected.

DIRE2_a073_motorSpeed.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) has detected the motor exceeding typical speed.
DIRE2_a073_motorSpeed.m: The motor is exceeding typical speed. The motor and Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIRE2_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.d: The motor speed measured by the Drive Unit resolver does not match the Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) wheel speed sensors.
DIRE2_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.m: The Drive Unit may not function as expected, including a reduction in vehicle power. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all

DIRE2_a076_lvSupplyOV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery overvoltage event.
DIRE2_a076_lvSupplyOV.m: No immediate impact. Could damage drive inverter LV hardware if LV is continuously too high.

DIRE2_a077_lvSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery power supply undervoltage event.
DIRE2_a077_lvSupplyUV.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIRE2_a078_adcRefLow.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference below the lower rational bound.
DIRE2_a078_adcRefLow.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIRE2_a079_adcRefHigh.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference above the upper rational bound.
DIRE2_a079_adcRefHigh.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIRE2_a086_activeDischargeOn.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Active discharge is on but bus voltage remains high.

DIRE2_a087_gtwMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the gateway (GTW) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE2_a087_gtwMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the GTW.

DIRE2_a090_pmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the pedal monitor (PM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE2_a090_pmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PM.

DIRE2_a091_ebsMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: EBS CAN node MIA.

DIRE2_a092_bmsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage (HV) battery management system (BMS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIRE2_a092_bmsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the BMS.

DIRE2_a096_memoryError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory check error.
DIRE2_a096_memoryError.m: The DI processor may reset, and other functionality may be impacted due to memory corruption.

DIRE2_a097_eepromError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) read/write access error. Check for other alerts t
DIRE2_a097_eepromError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.
