DIR_a060_heatsinkUT.m: No direct impact. Could damage drive inverter if temperature is continuously too low.

DIR_a061_heatsinkSensor.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Heatsink sensor bad.
DIR_a061_heatsinkSensor.m: Unable to monitor heatsink sensor temperature

DIR_a062_systemLimpMode.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system limp mode.

DIR_a063_bbMIA.d: One or more of the messages the Drive Inverter Rear (DIR) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) receives periodically from the Decoupled Brake Booster (BB) ECU is not received, indicating the BB ECU may be un
DIR_a063_bbMIA.m: Drive system may not function as expected.

DIR_a064_torqueIntervention.d: The torque production safety monitor has detected excess torque production and inhibited Drive Inverter (DIx) torque production for the remainder of the drive cycle.
DIR_a064_torqueIntervention.m: The torque production safety monitor will inhibit torque and excess heat production for the remainder of the drive cycle.

DIR_a069_rotorTempLimit.d: The rotor's temperature is too hot, limiting Drive Inverter (DIx) motor torque production.

DIR_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.d: The phase gate drive has detected four fault related signals from the induction motor or ten from the magnet motor.
DIR_a072_gateDriveFaultCounter.m: The phase gate drive fault counter may block any power stage from enabling. The drive unit may not function as expected.

DIR_a073_motorSpeed.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) has detected the motor exceeding typical speed.
DIR_a073_motorSpeed.m: The motor is exceeding typical speed. The motor and Drive Unit may not function as expected.

DIR_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.d: The motor speed measured by the Drive Unit resolver does not match the Anti-lock Braking System (ABS) wheel speed sensors.
DIR_a075_motorSpeedMismatch.m: The Drive Unit may not function as expected, including a reduction in vehicle power. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all

DIR_a076_lvSupplyOV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery overvoltage event.
DIR_a076_lvSupplyOV.m: No immediate impact. Could damage drive inverter LV hardware if LV is continuously too high.

DIR_a077_lvSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery power supply undervoltage event.
DIR_a077_lvSupplyUV.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIR_a078_adcRefLow.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference below the lower rational bound.
DIR_a078_adcRefLow.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIR_a079_adcRefHigh.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference above the upper rational bound.
DIR_a079_adcRefHigh.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DIR_a086_activeDischargeOn.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Active discharge is on but bus voltage remains high.

DIR_a087_gtwMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the gateway (GTW) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a087_gtwMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the GTW.

DIR_a090_pmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the pedal monitor (PM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a090_pmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PM.

DIR_a091_espMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic stability program (ESP, also known as traction control) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a091_espMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the ESP.

DIR_a092_bmsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage (HV) battery management system (BMS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a092_bmsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the BMS.

DIR_a096_memoryError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory check error.
DIR_a096_memoryError.m: The DI processor may reset, and other functionality may be impacted due to memory corruption.

DIR_a097_eepromError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) read/write access error. Check for other alerts t
DIR_a097_eepromError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.

DIR_a098_intTimeTooLong.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a DIx thread executing later than expected. The DIx CPU may be diverting resources towards other tasks.
DIR_a098_intTimeTooLong.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

DIR_a103_lowFlow.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Low flow rate of fluid.

DIR_a107_fpgaError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA).
DIR_a107_fpgaError.m: The DIx Gate Drive Unit may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIR_a108_stateTrans.d: The Drive Unit is not able to transition to Drive state. There may be a condition preventing the Drive Unit from transitioning states.

DIR_a109_ahbWriteError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) processor has detected an internal exception. This alert does not on its own indicate a symptom or root cause.
DIR_a109_ahbWriteError.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

DIR_a110_brakeMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic parking brake (EPB) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a110_brakeMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EPB.

DIR_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory (EEPROM) has reported phase sensor calibration is outside valid range.
DIR_a111_badPhaseSensorCalib.m: This alert is primarily for logging purposes and appears in conjunction with other alerts.

DIR_a112_noPhaseCurrent.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects no phase current.
DIR_a112_noPhaseCurrent.m: Without phase current, DIx cannot produce motor torque to rotate the wheels. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected, or at all.

DIR_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.d: The three Drive Inverter (DIx) heat sink sensors are not functioning. The DIx heat sink sensors monitor temperature in the motor and fluid port.
DIR_a113_noFuncHeatsinkSensor.m: Since it cannot detect temperature as expected, DIx may limit power output to an operational level. Drive Unit performance may be impacted.

DIR_a115_exceptionDataAbort2.d: The Drive Interface (DI) processor has detected an internal exception.
DIR_a115_exceptionDataAbort2.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

DIR_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.d: Drive Inverter Warning: High Speed Wear Counter Exceeded.
DIR_a116_highSpeedWearCounter.m: Silent alert to track mechanical wear

DIR_a117_diMIA.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DIR_a117_diMIA.m: DIx may not be able to communicate with the High Voltage Processor (HVP).

DIR_a119_hvpMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage processor (HVP) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a119_hvpMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the HVP, and active discharge may turn on.

DIR_a120_hvlinkMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: HVLINK CAN node MIA.

DIR_a121_motorControlRegulation.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Motor control is out of regulation.
DIR_a121_motorControlRegulation.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DIR_a123_lossMotorControl.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Loss of motor control.
DIR_a123_lossMotorControl.m: Unable to produce torque/power from drive unit

DIR_a126_limpMode.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit limp mode triggered.

DIR_a127_gracefulPowerOff.d: Drive Unit Graceful Power Off (GPO) has been triggered. The Drive Unit will lose current input from the High Voltage (HV) battery.

DIR_a133_capacitorOT.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected the capacitor is over temperature.
DIR_a133_capacitorOT.m: The flow of current between the capacitors and DIx may not function as expected.

DIR_a134_wheelSpeedIrrational.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) receives irrational or missing data from a wheel speed sensor (WSS).
DIR_a134_wheelSpeedIrrational.m: This alert is primarily informational. If it persists, traction control may become unavailable.

DIR_a136_spiError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected an error communicating with the Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI), which monitors the pedal monitor.
DIR_a136_spiError.m: DIx may not be able to receive data from the Pedal Monitor. The vehicle's drive cycle may not function as expected.

DIR_a140_eggright3MIA.d: The drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Etherloop Generalized Gateway Right (EGGRIGHT3) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a140_eggright3MIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EGGRIGHT3.

DIR_a144_configMismatch.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a Gateway configuration mismatch with the application currently running on the DI.
DIR_a144_configMismatch.m: The vehicle enters system limp mode and is unable to enter Drive.

DIR_a145_vcleftMIA.d: One or more of the messages the vehicle controller receives periodically from the Left Vehicle Controller (VCLEFT) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not received, indicating the VCLEFT ECU may be unava
DIR_a145_vcleftMIA.m: The vehicle controller is unable to monitor the state of the VCLEFT ECU.

DIR_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has detected a quantity of torque production that indicates Drive Unit wear.
DIR_a147_highTorqueWearLimit.m: Powertrain requires service - OK to drive

DIR_a149_oilPumpFailure.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Oil pump failure.

DIR_a150_busVD.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has measured a voltage drop in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIR_a150_busVD.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIR_a152_linError.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an error communicating with the Local Interconnect Network (LIN).
DIR_a152_linError.m: DIx and the oil pump communicate through LIN. DIx may not communicate with the oil pump as expected.

DIR_a154_resolver.d: The Drive Unit resolver (motor speed sensor) is not operating as expected. The resolver may have a condition affecting functionality.
DIR_a154_resolver.m: The resolver is not operating as expected. The DIx invertor control board may be unable to correctly detect the angle and speed of the rotor.

DIR_a155_vcfrontMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the front vehicle controller (VCFRONT) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a155_vcfrontMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCFRONT.

DIR_a155_vcsuperMIA.d: The drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Supermanifold Vehicle Controller (VCSUPER) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a155_vcsuperMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCSUPER.

DIR_a157_rcmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the restraints control module (RCM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a157_rcmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the RCM.

DIR_a158_ibstMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the iBooster (IBST, or brake assist) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a158_ibstMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the IBST.

DIR_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.d: The High Voltage (HV) bus has detected a voltage anomaly in the HV battery pack. The Drive Inverter (DIx) may not operate as expected.
DIR_a160_busVoltageAnomaly.m: DIx may not operate as expected. Drive torque may be restricted.

DIR_a161_epas3pMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic power assisted steering, gen 3 (EPAS3) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a161_epas3pMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EPAS3.

DIR_a172_statorOilTempOT.d: The drive inverter detects that the stator is over temperature for oil protection.

DIR_a174_unitMayNotRestart.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Unit may not allow drive on the next drive cycle due to HW damage.

DIR_a178_spindownLearningBrakeAllowed.d: Spindown learning is complete. This alert indicates to the driver that they can press the brake to finish the routine.

DIR_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that prevents the drive unit(s) from generating excess heat.
DIR_a202_excessHeatUnavailable.m: The drive unit(s) cannot produce excess heat when requested.

DIR_a233_diMsgMissed.d: The Drive Inverter (DIx) has missed 3 to 5 messages from the main controller. DIx will maintain the last commanded torque value, then 0 Nm.
DIR_a233_diMsgMissed.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes. No expected impact on vehicle functionality.

DIR_a234_pcsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the power conversion system (PCS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a234_pcsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PCS.

DIR_a236_tasMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Tesla air suspension (TAS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DIR_a236_tasMIA.m: Adaptive ride control may be unavailable

DIR_a240_clearFusesRoutine.d: This alert indicates the fuse clearing routine was attempted.

DIR_a242_spinDownLearning.d: Drive unit spindown learning calibration routine is in progress.

DIR_a244_mechSafeStateApplied.d: Alert for software trigger of mechanical safe state two-phase disconnect device.

DIR_a246_recoveryAtSpeedError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) detects the at-speed recovery mechanism encountered an error.

DI_a040_difMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DIF.

DI_a040_dire2MIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: DIRE2 CAN node MIA.

DI_a042_pmfMIA.m: The PM system is unable to determine the PMF state.

DI_a043_pmrMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the rear propulsion monitor (PMR) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a043_pmrMIA.m: The PM system is unable to determine the PMR state.

DI_a044_pmre1rMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: PMRE1R CAN node MIA.

DI_a044_pmrelMIA.m: The PM system is unable to determine the PMREL state.

DI_a045_pmre2MIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: PMRE2 CAN node MIA.

DI_a045_pmrerMIA.m: The PM system is unable to determine the PMRER state.

DI_a046_direlMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the rear-left drive inverter (DIREL) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a046_direlMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DIREL.

DI_a047_direrMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the rear-right drive inverter (DIRER) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a047_direrMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DIRER.

DI_a051_batteryOvercurrent.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a high voltage (HV) battery overcurrent event in software. .
DI_a051_batteryOvercurrent.m: Shift out of Park is blocked.

DI_a052_dirMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the rear drive interface (DIR) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a052_dirMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DIR.

DI_a062_systemLimpMode.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system limp mode.

DI_a063_systemGracefulPowerOff.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that requires it to trigger system graceful power off (GPO). Another vehicle alert should be present to indicate that conditi

DI_a064_pmsrmUnitMiaWithHvDown.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that a permanent magnet drive unit has gone missing while the high voltage (HV) system is not up.
DI_a064_pmsrmUnitMiaWithHvDown.m: The DI ECU cannot determine the state of a permanent magnet drive unit.

DI_a071_vcsecMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the security controller is missing (MIA).
DI_a071_vcsecMIA.m: The installed tires cannot be determined.

DI_a076_lvSupplyOV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery overvoltage event.
DI_a076_lvSupplyOV.m: No immediate impact. Could damage drive inverter LV hardware if LV is continuously too high.

DI_a077_lvSupplyUV.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a low voltage (LV) battery power supply undervoltage event.
DI_a077_lvSupplyUV.m: Drive unit enters unit limp mode (see DI_a126_limpMode)

DI_a078_adcRefLow.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference below the lower rational bound.
DI_a078_adcRefLow.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DI_a079_adcRefHigh.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) channel reference above the upper rational bound.
DI_a079_adcRefHigh.m: The vehicle is blocked from shifting out of Park.

DI_a080_accelSynch.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a discrepancy between accelerator pedal sensor track 1 and track 2, indicating that the DI may not be able to accurately track pedal posi
DI_a080_accelSynch.m: PULL OVER IMMEDIATELY - Unable to drive

DI_a081_accelTrack1.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an issue with the accelerator pedal sensor track 1, indicating that the DI may not be able to accurately track pedal position.
DI_a081_accelTrack1.m: PULL OVER IMMEDIATELY - Unable to drive

DI_a082_accelTrack2.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an issue with the accelerator pedal sensor track 2, indicating that the DI may not be able to accurately track pedal position.
DI_a082_accelTrack2.m: PULL OVER IMMEDIATELY - Unable to drive

DI_a083_brakePedalSensor.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition affecting the brake pedal sensor.
DI_a083_brakePedalSensor.m: This alert informs service of irrational pedal sensor states.

DI_a085_proximityInEnable.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) reports proximity voltage that indicates that there is a charge cable connected to the vehicle.

DI_a086_dpbMIA.d: One or more of the messages the Drive Inverter (DI) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) receives periodically from the Decoupled Power Brake (DPB) ECU is not received, indicating the DPB ECU may be unavaila
DI_a086_dpbMIA.m: There is no communication between the DPB and DI ECUs. This may impact traction and vehicle dynamics control performance. The vehicle should be driven

DI_a087_gtwMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the gateway (GTW) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a087_gtwMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the GTW.

DI_a089_sccmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the steering column control module (SCCM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a089_sccmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the SCCM and may be unable to shift gears.

DI_a090_pmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the pedal monitor (PM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a090_pmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PM.

DI_a091_ebsMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: EBS CAN node MIA.

DI_a091_espMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic stability program (ESP, also known as traction control) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a091_espMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the ESP.

DI_a092_bmsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the high voltage (HV) battery management system (BMS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a092_bmsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the BMS.

DI_a096_memoryError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a memory check error.
DI_a096_memoryError.m: The DI processor may reset, and other functionality may be impacted due to memory corruption.

DI_a097_eepromError.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) read/write access error. Check for other alerts t
DI_a097_eepromError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.

DI_a098_eepromManagerError.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) error. Check for other alerts that may be set as a result.
DI_a098_eepromManagerError.m: The DI is not able to store any record in EEPROM where the error is located, which impacts functionality on the next boot up.

DI_a103_bbMIA.d: One or more of the messages the Drive Inverter (DI) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) receives periodically from the Decoupled Brake Booster (BB) ECU is not received, indicating the BB ECU may be unavaila
DI_a103_bbMIA.m: Regenerative-friction brake torque blending functionality may be limited or unavailable.

DI_a104_proximityIrrational.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) reports irrational proximity voltage to the drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU), indicating that the CP latch may not have disengaged.

DI_a104_trailerBrakeBackupState.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects Trailer Brake Control functionality may be reduced.

DI_a105_cpMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the charge port (CP) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a105_cpMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the CP.

DI_a107_trailerBrakeFaulted.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects Trailer Brake Control functionality is unavailable.

DI_a109_ahbWriteError.d: The Drive Interface (DI) processor has detected an internal exception. This alert does not on its own indicate a symptom or root cause.
DI_a109_ahbWriteError.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

DI_a110_brakeMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic parking brake (EPB) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a110_brakeMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EPB.

DI_a110_ppbMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: PPB CAN node MIA.

DI_a111_sasMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: SAS CAN node MIA.

DI_a113_lowBrakeMCPressure.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a sustained low brake master cylinder pressure health metric, indicating there is low brake stiffness.
DI_a113_lowBrakeMCPressure.m: The brake pedal may feel softer and require additional travel to achieve the same braking force.

DI_a115_exceptionDataAbort2.d: The Drive Interface (DI) processor has detected an internal exception.
DI_a115_exceptionDataAbort2.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

DI_a116_motorRecovered.d: Drive Inverter (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) has successfully recovered after an unexpected reset event.
DI_a116_motorRecovered.m: A motor experienced a reset - OK to drive

DI_a120_regenBlendingUnavailable.d: Brake pedal regenerative brake (regen) torque is unavailable.

DI_a121_crawlTorqueInStandstill.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that crawl torque has been active at standstill for a prolonged period.
DI_a121_crawlTorqueInStandstill.m: Trail Assist remains active when stopped

DI_a123_trailAssistUnavailable.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Trail Assist feature is unavailable.

DI_a124_cruiseFault.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that affects cruise control.

DI_a125_noBatteryPower.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the drive system is receiving no power from the high voltage (HV) battery.
DI_a125_noBatteryPower.m: The vehicle enters system graceful power off (GPO).

DI_a126_dire1lMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: DIRE1L CAN node MIA.

DI_a126_trailAssistDeactivated.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects an abnormal Trail Assist behavior resulting in automatic deactivation.

DI_a127_dire1rMIA.d: Drive Inverter Warning: DIRE1R CAN node MIA.

DI_a127_pepsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Primary Electric Power Steering (PEPS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a127_pepsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PEPS.

DI_a128_psfaMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Primary Steering Feedback Actuator (PSFA) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a128_psfaMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the PSFA.

DI_a129_epbNotApplied.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that prevents the electronic parking brake (EPB) from being set while the vehicle is not moving.

DI_a130_aebFault.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that affects Automatic Emergency Braking (AEB).

DI_a131_highSpeedWearLimit.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) is limiting the top vehicle speed after a certain amount of time to protect the drive system from wear.
DI_a131_highSpeedWearLimit.m: After 1 minute above the upper speed threshold, the DI ECU slows the vehicle down to the threshold speed.

DI_a132_sepsMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Secondary Electric Power Steering (SEPS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a132_sepsMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the SEPS.

DI_a133_vehicleHoldTimedOut.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) automatically shifted to Park after vehicle was in stationary hold for prolonged time.

DI_a134_wheelSpeedIrrational.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) receives irrational or missing data from a wheel speed sensor (WSS).
DI_a134_wheelSpeedIrrational.m: This alert is primarily informational. If it persists, traction control may become unavailable.

DI_a135_vehicleHoldFault.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that affects Vehicle Hold.

DI_a136_rear1rUnitDisabled.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Rear 1 right drive unit disabled.

DI_a136_ssfaMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Secondary Steering Feedback Actuator (SSFA) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a136_ssfaMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the SSFA.

DI_a137_noCapableDriveUnits.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that none of the vehicle's drive units are capable of producing torque.

DI_a138_frontUnitDisabled.d: The drive inverter (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to command a unit torque request to the front drive inverter (DIF) ECU because the front drive unit is disabled. This alert is set in ad
DI_a138_frontUnitDisabled.m: Front motor disabled - OK to drive

DI_a138_rear2UnitDisabled.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Rear 2 drive unit disabled.

DI_a139_rear1lUnitDisabled.d: Drive Inverter Warning: Rear 1 left drive unit disabled.

DI_a139_rearUnitDisabled.d: The drive inverter (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) is unable to command a unit torque request to the rear drive inverter (DIR) ECU because the rear drive unit is disabled. This alert is set in addi
DI_a139_rearUnitDisabled.m: Rear motor disabled - Avoid steep grades

DI_a140_eggright3MIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Etherloop Generalized Gateway Right (EGGRIGHT3) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a140_eggright3MIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EGGRIGHT3.

DI_a140_ptcMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the positive temperature coefficient (PTC) heater controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a140_ptcMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to determine the state of the PTC heater.

DI_a141_dasMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the driver assistance system (DAS) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a141_dasMIA.m: Some driver assistance features are unavailable.

DI_a143_mcuLimitActive.d: The media control unit (MCU) is limiting the top speed and power of the drive unit(s).

DI_a144_configMismatch.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a Gateway configuration mismatch with the application currently running on the DI.
DI_a144_configMismatch.m: The vehicle enters system limp mode and is unable to enter Drive.

DI_a145_vcleftMIA.d: One or more of the messages the vehicle controller receives periodically from the Left Vehicle Controller (VCLEFT) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not received, indicating the VCLEFT ECU may be unava
DI_a145_vcleftMIA.m: The vehicle controller is unable to monitor the state of the VCLEFT ECU.

DI_a146_pbrkPanicEpbFault.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that there is an issue with the electronic parking brake (EPB) during parking.

DI_a147_vcdoorflMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCDOORFL.

DI_a148_vcdoorfrMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Front Right Door Vehicle Controller (VCDOORFR) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a148_vcdoorfrMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCDOORFR.

DI_a149_vcdoorrlMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Rear Left Door Vehicle Controller (VCDOORRL) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a149_vcdoorrlMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCDOORRL.

DI_a150_coldBrakeTempWarning.d: On a vehicle equipped with the carbon ceramic brake package, the drive inverter (DI) detects the ambient temperature is below the rated temperature for the brakes. Braking performance may be significa
DI_a150_coldBrakeTempWarning.m: Cold ambient temperature detected - Use caution

DI_a151_vcdoorrrMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the Rear Right Door Vehicle Controller (VCDOORRR) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a151_vcdoorrrMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCDOORRR.

DI_a153_vcrightMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the right vehicle controller (VCRIGHT) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a153_vcrightMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCRIGHT.

DI_a155_vcfrontMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the front vehicle controller (VCFRONT) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a155_vcfrontMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the VCFRONT.

DI_a155_vcleftsMIA.d: The Left Secondary Vehicle Controller (VCLEFTS) node is Missing in Action (MIA) on Controller Area Network (CAN) for one or more messages.
DI_a155_vcleftsMIA.m: The Drive Unit may not operate as expected.

DI_a155_vcsuperMIA.d: One or more of the messages the Drive Interface (DI) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) receives periodically from the Supermanifold Vehicle Controller (VCSUPER) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is not receiv
DI_a155_vcsuperMIA.m: The DI is unable to monitor the state of the VCSUPER ECU.

DI_a156_dflkMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that a differential locker (DFLKF or DFLKR) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a156_dflkMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the DFLKF or DFLKR.

DI_a157_rcmMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) or drive inverter (DIx) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the restraints control module (RCM) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a157_rcmMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the RCM.

DI_a158_ibstMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the iBooster (IBST, or brake assist) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a158_ibstMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the IBST.

DI_a160_fastLearnComplete.d: The drive inveter (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the fast learning initiated by resetting mileage since last tire service has finished.
DI_a160_fastLearnComplete.m: This alert indicates the vehicle has very quickly learned the new tire sizes after a customer reset their tire service mileage or service updated tire

DI_a161_epas3pMIA.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the electronic power assisted steering, gen 3 (EPAS3) controller area network (CAN) node is missing (MIA).
DI_a161_epas3pMIA.m: The DI ECU is unable to monitor the state of the EPAS3.

DI_a162_shiftDenied.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) receives a shift request but detects a condition that prevents the request from being honored.
DI_a162_shiftDenied.m: User unable to shift until clear condition is resolved.

DI_a163_shiftMotorSpeed.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) receives a shift request from Drive to Reverse or Reverse to Drive but denies it because the vehicle speed is too high.
DI_a163_shiftMotorSpeed.m: Cannot shift gears - Vehicle speed is too high

DI_a164_brakeOverride.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) decreases torque because it detects that the brake and accelerator pedal were pressed at the same time.

DI_a166_driverLeft.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the driver may no longer be in the driver's seat while the vehicle is not in Park.
DI_a166_driverLeft.m: Vehicle automatically parked to prevent rollaway

DI_a167_keyNotAuthenticated.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) does not detect a valid key (key card, key fob, phone as key, or Tesla mobile app).

DI_a168_proximityDriveDenial.d: The reported proximity voltage indicates that there is a charge cable connected to the vehicle while the vehicle is in a drive state (Drive or Reverse).

DI_a169_regenOffOverride.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) enables regenerative braking and disables traction control because Dyno Mode is active.
DI_a169_regenOffOverride.m: Dyno mode enabled - Do not drive

DI_a170_tireConfigUpdated.d: The drive inveter (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the tire configuration has been updated.
DI_a170_tireConfigUpdated.m: This alert indicates the vehicle is quickly learning the new tire configuration in order to improve the driving experience and reduce loss of regen or

DI_a171_driveRailNotRequested.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) cannot confirm that a vehicle controller ECU requested a drive state, and as a result blocks shifting into Drive or Reverse.

DI_a172_accelPressedInNP.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the accelerator was pressed while the vehicle is in Park or Neutral.
DI_a172_accelPressedInNP.m: Shift into D or R to drive

DI_a173_bothPedalsPressed.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that both the accelerator pedal and the brake pedal were pressed at the same time.

DI_a174_notOkToStartDrive.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the low voltage (LV) supply is too low to shift into Drive.

DI_a175_crsNotAvailable.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition  that prevents it from engaging cruise control.

DI_a176_EBRreleased.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) releases the brakes during an external brake request (EBR) because it detects that the vehicle may be skidding.

DI_a177_tireFitmentChanged.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a significant change in tire circumference ratio from front to rear axle, between square (similar circumference) and staggered (different
DI_a177_tireFitmentChanged.m: If the tire fitment change detection is a false positive (no change in front to rear tire circumference ratio), user may experience more traction cont

DI_a178_steeringAngleOffsetFault.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a steering angle offset that exceeds plausibility.

DI_a179_steeringAngleOffsetWarning.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a steering angle offset that is approaching the plausibility threshold.

DI_a180_crsSeatbeltUnbuckled.d: Cruise control is unavailable because the drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects that the driver's seatbelt is unbuckled.

DI_a181_rearMotorMode.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) only sending torque to the rear drive axle.

DI_a182_frontMotorMode.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) is only sending torque to the front drive inverter (DIF) ECU.

DI_a183_holdReleaseRqrd.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) informs customer of Vehicle Hold release required.

DI_a184_autoparkCanceled.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) informs customer that Autopark was canceled.

DI_a185_autoparkAborted.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a condition that causes it to cancel Autopark.

DI_a186_steeringAngleOffsetService.d: The Drive Interface (DI) ECU detects a steering angle offset that is due for maintenance during service.
DI_a186_steeringAngleOffsetService.m: Tire wear and vehicle dynamics will continue to degrade if steering angle offset continues to increase, eventually exceeding the warning threshold.

DI_a187_pedalMisapplication.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) reduces acceleration because it detects a potential pedal misapplication (when the user presses the accelerator in Drive or Reverse when they mea

DI_a189_garageShiftWithPedal.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a shift request when the accelerator pedal is pressed.
DI_a189_garageShiftWithPedal.m: Cannot shift gears - Accelerator pedal is pressed

DI_a190_tireRotationRecommended.d: The vehicle sensors have detected the tire tread depth in the rear tires exceeds a threshold of 1.5mm relative to the front tires. The vehicle may be overdue for a tire rotation.
DI_a190_tireRotationRecommended.m: Rear tire tread depth low - Schedule service

DI_a191_brakeShiftReq.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) detects a shift request the requires the brake pedal be pressed.
DI_a191_brakeShiftReq.m: Press brake pedal to shift gears

DI_a192_invalidOdometer.d: The drive interface (DI) electronic control unit (ECU) reports that the odometer was not learned by the drive unit and the odometer reading may be incorrect.

DI_a193_vdcBrakeTorqueFr.d: Drive Inverter (DI) does not receive proper front brake torque signals from the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) module.
DI_a193_vdcBrakeTorqueFr.m: Traction control and stability control may not operate as expected due to this error. The vehicle should be driven with extreme caution if these syste

DI_a194_vdcBrakeTorqueRe.d: Drive Inverter (DI) does not receive proper rear brake torque signals from the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) module.
DI_a194_vdcBrakeTorqueRe.m: Traction control and stability control may not operate as expected due to this error. The vehicle should be driven with extreme caution if these syste

DI_a198_vdcEspMCPressAndSteering.d: The Drive Inverter (DI) is not receiving proper brake master cylinder pressure or steering angle signals from the Electronic Stability Program (ESP) and/or Electric Power Assisted Steering (EPAS).

EPASTP_a002_adcMuxErr.d: This alert is reported by the ADCDriverCD_TC3x component when Analog-Digital Converter (ADC) multiplexer testing failed, the root cause also can be reference problems or short-to-GND/short-to-supply t

EPASTP_a006_swrhResetByInvalidErrid.d: This alert is reported by the SwResetHandler_R12 (SWRH) component when there is a RAM failure: the stored error is not visible OR SW systematic failure: error not stored or not handled.

EPASTP_a007_tsmcCoreTempDiff.d: This alert is reported by the TemperatureSensorMCUCore_TC3x (TSMC) component when there is a MCU internal failure (temperature sensor or other failure which generates uneven heating of the MCU).

EPASTP_a011_mcusscMonbistErr.d: This alert is reported by the MCUSafetyStartupChecks_TC3x (MCUSSC) component when there is a MCU internal failure (The Secondary Monitor and Standby SMU Built in Self Test failed).

EPASTP_a012_mcusscMbistEccErr.d: This alert is reported by the MCUSafetyStartupChecks_TC3x (MCUSSC) component when there is a MCU internal failure. (Memory Built-In-SelfTest failed).

HVPACK3_a057_PackCtrCloseFailNoVoltageMa.d: Tried to close pack contactors but failed because a voltage match was not maintained.

HVPACK3_a058_PackCtrOpenedUnexpectedly.d: Pack contactor exited the economized state unexpectedly. Possibly insufficient coil drive current.
HVPACK3_a058_PackCtrOpenedUnexpectedly.m: Vehicle may lose propulsion or be unable to support LV system.

HVPACK3_a059_PackCtrFellOpenLowDriveVolt.d: Contactor opened unexpectedly due to insufficient contactor drive voltage.
HVPACK3_a059_PackCtrFellOpenLowDriveVolt.m: Vehicle may lose propulsion or be unable to support LV system.

HVPACK3_a060_PackPosCtrCloseBlockNoVolta.d: Did not try to close positive contactor because a voltage match was not achieved.
HVPACK3_a060_PackPosCtrCloseBlockNoVolta.m: Positive pack contactor will remain open.

HVPACK3_a062_PackPosCtrCloseFailNoCoilCu.d: Tried to close positive contactor but failed because the coil drive current was too low.

HVPACK3_a063_PackNegCtrCloseFailNoCoilCu.d: Tried to close negative contactor but failed because the coil drive current was too low.
HVPACK3_a063_PackNegCtrCloseFailNoCoilCu.m: Negative pack contactor will remain open.

HVPACK3_a064_PackPosCtrCloseFailNoVoltag.d: Tried to close positive contactor but failed because a voltage match was not maintained.
HVPACK3_a064_PackPosCtrCloseFailNoVoltag.m: Positive pack contactor will remain open.

HVPACK3_a065_PackNegCtrCloseFailNoVoltag.d: Tried to close negative contactor but failed because a voltage match was not maintained.
HVPACK3_a065_PackNegCtrCloseFailNoVoltag.m: Negative pack contactor will remain open.

HVPACK3_a066_PackPosCtrFellOpenLowCoilCu.d: Positive contactor opened unexpectedly due to insufficient coil drive current.
HVPACK3_a066_PackPosCtrFellOpenLowCoilCu.m: Vehicle may lose propulsion or be unable to support LV system.

HVPACK3_a067_PackNegCtrFellOpenLowCoilCu.d: Negative contactor opened unexpectedly due to insufficient coil drive current.
HVPACK3_a067_PackNegCtrFellOpenLowCoilCu.m: Vehicle may lose propulsion or be unable to support LV system.

HVPACK3_a068_PackPosCtrOpenBlockedPyroDe.d: Positive contactor was blocked from opening due to high current. Pyro deployed instead.
HVPACK3_a068_PackPosCtrOpenBlockedPyroDe.m: The positive pyro fuse will be deployed.

HVPACK3_a069_PackNegCtrOpenBlockedPyroDe.d: Negative contactor was blocked from opening due to high current. Pyro deployed instead.
HVPACK3_a069_PackNegCtrOpenBlockedPyroDe.m: The negative pyro fuse will be deployed.

HVPACK3_a070_PackPosCtrOpenFailAuxStillC.d: Positive contactor is expected to be open but aux reports them as still closed.
HVPACK3_a070_PackPosCtrOpenFailAuxStillC.m: Firmware will try to open the contactor again.

HVPACK3_a071_PackNegCtrOpenFailAuxStillC.d: Negative contactor is expected to be open but aux reports them as still closed.
HVPACK3_a071_PackNegCtrOpenFailAuxStillC.m: Firmware will try to open the contactor again.

HVPACK3_a077_ShuntUnderTemperature.m: This alert is primarily informational and is used for logging purposes.

HVP_w004_CrashEvent.d: Warns if the crash signal is detected by HVP.

HVP_w005_OverDchgCurrentFault.d: Warns if the pack discharge is above max discharge current limit.
HVP_w005_OverDchgCurrentFault.m: The contactors will open immdediately or pyro fuse may blow

HVP_w006_OverChargeCurrentFault.d: Warns if the pack charge current is above max charge current limit.
HVP_w006_OverChargeCurrentFault.m: The DC charging will end and contactors may open immdediately or pyro may disconnect

HVP_w007_OverCurrentFault.d: Warns if the pack current (discharge or charge) is above max current limit.

HVP_w008_OverTemperatureFault.d: A pack module temperature is above maximum temperature limit.
HVP_w008_OverTemperatureFault.m: The conactors may open immdediately or pyro fuse may blow

HVP_w009_OverVoltageFault.d: A brick voltage is above maximum voltage limit.
HVP_w009_OverVoltageFault.m: The conactors may open immdediately or pyro fuse may blow

HVP_w010_UnderVoltageFault.d: A brick voltage is below minimum voltage limit.
HVP_w010_UnderVoltageFault.m: The conactors may open immdediately or pyro fuse may blow

HVP_w011_PrimaryBmbMiaFault.d: Warns if the voltage and temperature readings from primary BMB chain are mia.
HVP_w011_PrimaryBmbMiaFault.m: The secondary bmb chain is used exclusively for voltage and temperature protoection

HVP_w012_BmbDataIntegrityLoss.d: Data integrity loss due to Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) chain double break.
HVP_w012_BmbDataIntegrityLoss.m: BMB sensor data is likely being substituted to replace data missing due to the communication issue

HVP_w013_BmbCommunication.d: A communication interruption has been detected in the Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) chain.
HVP_w013_BmbCommunication.m: May indicate a loss of BMB sensor data

HVP_w014_BmsMiaOnHviFault.d: Warns if the BMS node is mia on HVI CAN.
HVP_w014_BmsMiaOnHviFault.m: CAN messages are not received from BMS or other nodes on vehicle

HVP_w018_powerCyclingPcs.d: The PCS is MIA and the HVP is power cycling it in an attempt to recover it.
HVP_w018_powerCyclingPcs.m: The PCS will be power cycled and temporarily no longer support the LV system

HVP_w019_CpFault.d: The charge port (CP) electronic control unit (ECU) is detected as not operational, making the vehicle unable to charge.
HVP_w019_CpFault.m: Vehicle will be unable to charge.

HVP_w020_ShuntHwMiaFault.d: Warns if the shunt current reading is not avaialble.

HVP_w021_PyroMiaFault.d: The vehicle detects a condition affecting one or more of the pyrotechnic (pyro) fuses.

HVP_w022_PyroMiaWarning.d: Alerts if PyroLeakTest pyro diagnostic has cross warning threshold.

HVP_w024_Supply12vFault.d: Warns if the low voltage battery is below minimum voltage threshold.
HVP_w024_Supply12vFault.m: The contactors may not remain closed

HVP_w025_VerSupplyFault.d: Warns if the Energy reserve voltage supply is below minimum voltage threshold.
HVP_w025_VerSupplyFault.m: The pyro diagnostics may fail, the contactors will not close

HVP_w026_HvilFault.d: Sets if an HVIL fault is detected. Contactors cannot close (car will no longer drive) if the contactors are currently open. If no repairs were performed and the alert clears on its own, there is risk
HVP_w026_HvilFault.m: Contactors will not close, it may open if vehicle not in drive

HVP_w028_PackVoltMismatchFault.d: Warns if the pack voltage doesn't match approximately with sum of brick voltages.

HVP_w029_EnsMiaFault.d: Warns if the ENS line is not connected to HVC.
HVP_w029_EnsMiaFault.m: The crash signal may not be detected by HVP

HVP_w030_PackPosCtrArcFault.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) detects a series arc at the positive pack contactor.
HVP_w030_PackPosCtrArcFault.m: The pyro fuse may blow. The vehicle may be unable to drive or charge.

HVP_w031_packNegCtrArcFault.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) detects a series arc at the negative pack contactor.
HVP_w031_packNegCtrArcFault.m: The pyro fuse may blow. The vehicle may be unable to drive or charge.

HVP_w032_ShuntHwAndBmsMiaFault.d: Warns if the HVP detectes both BMS and Shunt MIA while pack contactors closed.

HVP_w033_fcContHwFault.d: Warns if the HVP detects unintended loss of contactor control (V or I).

HVP_w034_cpMismatchFault.d: Warns if the CP version mismatch.

HVP_w035_packContHwFault.d: Warns if the HVP detects unintended loss of contactor control (V or I).

HVP_w036_pyroFuseBlown.d: Alerts if the HV pyro fuse is blown.

HVP_w037_pyroFuseFailedToBlow.d: Alerts if the HV pyro fuse is blown.

HVP_w038_CpilFault.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected the Charge Port HV Interlock Loop (CPIL) has a condition affecting functionality.

HVP_w039_PackContactorFellOpen.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected a pack contactor has opened during a closed state.
HVP_w039_PackContactorFellOpen.m: The pack contactor state will appear as open. The vehicle powertrain may unexpectedly lose high voltage power under load.

HVP_w040_FcContactorFellOpen.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected a Fast Charge (FC) contactor has opened during a closed state.
HVP_w040_FcContactorFellOpen.m: The FC contactors will open. The vehicle may not be able to DC charge. The current charging session may be unexpectedly terminated as the FC contactor

HVP_w041_packCtrCloseBlocked.d: Warn if pack contactors are not allowed to close.
HVP_w041_packCtrCloseBlocked.m: While this alert is set, HV contactors will not be able to close. This will prevent drive, charge and 12V support and eventually the 12V battery will

HVP_w042_fcCtrCloseBlocked.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) is blocking the Fast Charge (FC) contactors from closing.
HVP_w042_fcCtrCloseBlocked.m: The FC contactors are unable to close. The vehicle will not be able to DC charge.

HVP_w043_packContactorForceOpen.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has forced the pack contactors open despite the pack contactor state being closed.
HVP_w043_packContactorForceOpen.m: The HVP opened the pack contactors. High voltage will be isolated to just the battery.

HVP_w044_fcContactorForceOpen.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has forced the Fast Charge (FC) contactors open despite the FC contactor state being closed.
HVP_w044_fcContactorForceOpen.m: The HVP opened the FC contactors. The vehicle may not be able to Supercharge or DC charge.

HVP_w045_dcLinkOverVoltage.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected the DC-link voltage has exceeded the voltage mismatch threshold while closing the pack contactors.
HVP_w045_dcLinkOverVoltage.m: The HVP will stop closing the pack contactors. The pack contactors will open.

HVP_w046_shuntOverTemperature.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected the shunt temperature has exceeded the high threshold value.
HVP_w046_shuntOverTemperature.m: There may be a high impedance joint. After a prolonged time at a high temperature, the properties of the manganin may be impacted.

HVP_w047_passivePyroDeploy.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) hardware circuit board has detected and triggered the pyrotechnic fuse on the battery. The High Voltage (HV) system has been isolated to the battery.
HVP_w047_passivePyroDeploy.m: The HVP pyrotechnic fuse has triggered. High voltage has been isolated to the HV battery.

HVP_w048_swUnsupportedBmbAsicType.d: Unsupported battery monitoring board (BMB) application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) configuration.
HVP_w048_swUnsupportedBmbAsicType.m: The high voltage processor may be unable to obtain battery pack measurements.

HVP_w049_packCtrCloseFailed.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected a pack contactor has entered blocked state.
HVP_w049_packCtrCloseFailed.m: The pack contactors did not close. The High Voltage System may not function as expected.

HVP_w050_fcCtrCloseFailed.d: The Fast Charge (FC) contactor is unable to close. This results in the inability to Fast Charge.
HVP_w050_fcCtrCloseFailed.m: The vehicle is unable to fast charge, but AC charging is not impacted.

HVP_w051_shuntThermistorMia.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) has detected the shunt thermistor sense line is Missing in Action (MIA), indicating it may not be connected to the High Voltage Controller (HVC).
HVP_w051_shuntThermistorMia.m: The shunt thermistor may have a condition affecting functionality. The thermistor may not detect shunt temperature as expected.

HVP_w053_hvVoltageSensorRationality.d: The High Voltage (HV) battery pack voltage significantly differs from the DC Link voltage while the contactors are closed.
HVP_w053_hvVoltageSensorRationality.m: The HV voltage sense circuit does not operate as expected.

HVP_w054_swMissingConfigBlock.d: Incorrect or Missing configuration block programmed.

HVP_w055_swMissingPartNumber.d: Incorrect or Missing part number programmed.

HVP_w056_contactorFailedToOpen.d: One or more fast charge (FC) or pack contactors in the high voltage battery failed to open when requested.
HVP_w056_contactorFailedToOpen.m: Pyro fuse will blow, which will cut off all high voltage (including charging)

HVP_w058_BmbVrefBad.d: Indicates a Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) reference voltage has continuously been out of bounds.
HVP_w058_BmbVrefBad.m: Other BMB voltage signals will be substituted given the reference voltage is unreliable

HVP_w060_StackVoltageSense.d: Indicates a mismatch between a Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) stack voltage and the sum of its brick voltages.

HVP_w062_BmbOtpConfigError.d: A Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) device has an unrecognized One-Time-Programmable (OTP) memory contents.
HVP_w062_BmbOtpConfigError.m: May indicate a BMB device hardware issue which could result in unreliable measurement data

HVP_w067_HvilCalNodeMismatch.d: The High Voltage Interlock Loop (HVIL) type in the High Voltage Processor's (HVP) calibration block or firmware file does not match the expected configuration.

HVP_w068_shuntUnexpectedThermistor.d: The High Voltage Processor (HVP) detects the shunt thermistor is present when the HVP calibration block has shunt Temperature Coefficients of Resistance (TCRs) for a shunt without a thermistor.
HVP_w068_shuntUnexpectedThermistor.m: The High Voltage Controller (HVC) will measure the pack current less accurately affecting algorithms such as pack energy estimation. No immediate vehi

HVREX_a017_ShuntAsicCalNotUsed.m: The default shunt ASIC gain will be used for high voltage (HV) current sensing. Shunt current measurement accuracy may be reduced.

HVREX_a019_ShuntTcrCalNotUsed.m: The default shunt TCR coefficients will be used for high voltage (HV) current sensing. Shunt current measurement accuracy may be reduced.

HVREX_a055_BmbStatusRegError.d: A Battery Monitoring Board (BMB) device status register error indication has been detected.

HVREX_a081_PackOverTemperatureFault.d: A battery pack module temperature is above the fault temperature limit.
HVREX_a081_PackOverTemperatureFault.m: Unable to drive or charge - PULL OVER SAFELY

UI_a001_DriverDoorOpen.d: The driver door is detected as open when the vehicle is shifted into a gear other than Park, which may lead to the driver door being open while driving.

UI_a002_DoorOpen.d: The passenger or rear door(s) (except for Model X Falcon door(s)) is detected as open when the vehicle is shifted into a gear other than Park, which may lead to the passenger or rear door(s) being ope

UI_a003_TrunkOpen.d: The rear trunk is detected as open when the vehicle is shifted into a gear other than Park, which may lead to the rear trunk being open while driving.

UI_a004_FrunkOpen.d: The front trunk primary or secondary latch is detected as open when the vehicle is shifted into a gear other than Park, which may lead to the front trunk being open while driving.

UI_a005_HeadlightsOnDoorOpen.d: Driver door open and master light switch is on position ON.

UI_a007_SoftPackConfigMismatch.d: High Voltage battery software configuration does not match the vehicle configuration.

UI_a009_SquashfsError.d: A SquashFS (file system) error was detected.
UI_a009_SquashfsError.m: A SquashFS error may cause a reboot cycle leading to a blank and unresponsive display.

UI_a010_MapsMissing.d: Navigation map data is missing and the corresponding file system was not mounted. Install latest map data.

UI_a011_IncorrectMap.d: Version file present therefore some maps exist, but not inside the expected region code directory.

UI_a012_NotOnPrivateProperty.d: Summon not available, vehicle not on private property.

UI_a013_TPMSHardWarning.d: Air pressure of at least one tire is very low, may have a puncture. Check and inflate tire(s).
UI_a013_TPMSHardWarning.m: Air pressure in tires very low

UI_a014_TPMSSoftWarning.d: The air pressure of the tire(s) is below the recommended threshold, which is determined by the Tire Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS) electronic control unit (ECU). This may lead to reduced tire adhes
UI_a014_TPMSSoftWarning.m: Air pressure below recommendation for tires

UI_a015_TPMSOverPressureWarning.d: Air pressure of at least one tire is above recommendation. Deflate tire(s).
UI_a015_TPMSOverPressureWarning.m: Air pressure over recommendation for tires

UI_a017_TPMSSystemFault.d: Tire pressure sensors unable to measure tire pressure. Triggers tell tale light on the cluster.

UI_a018_SlipStartOn.d: Traction control is in Slip Start mode.

UI_a019_ParkBrakeFault.d: Park brake (EPB) detected a fault such as disconnected harness or electrical short, etc. whilst not applied or is not available. Triggers amber tell tale light on the cluster.

UI_a020_SteeringReduced.d: The primary or secondary Electronic Power Assisted Steering module (EPAS3P or EPAS3S) detects a condition affecting its functionality or reduced ability to provide power steering assistance while driv

UI_a021_RearSeatbeltUnbuckled.d: At least one of the rear seat belt is unbuckled whilst an occupant is detected.

UI_a022_ApeFusesEtc.d: Driver Assistance ECU (APE) is not fused or not locked or missing credentials.
UI_a022_ApeFusesEtc.m: Driver Assistance ECU is in an unsecured state (unfused)

UI_a023_CellInternetCheckFailed.d: The cell link does not pass the internet check, which may lead to a loss of cellular connectivity and mobile app control.
UI_a023_CellInternetCheckFailed.m: There may be a loss of cellular connectivity and mobile app control.

UI_a024_WifiInternetCheckFailed.d: The Wi-Fi link does not pass the internet check, which may cause the vehicle to be unable to connect to the internet.
UI_a024_WifiInternetCheckFailed.m: There may be a loss of Wi-Fi connectivity.

UI_a026_ModemResetLoopDetected.d: The cellular modem is in a reset loop, which may lead to a loss of cellular connectivity.
UI_a026_ModemResetLoopDetected.m: There is a loss of cellular connectivity.

UI_a027_AutoSteerMIA.d: Missing CAN message from Driver Assistance ECU (DAS) or Cruise or Autopilot state went to FAULT.

UI_a028_FrontTrunkPopupClosed.d: The front trunk / frunk is open while driving, and has not been closed after the driver dismissed multiple reminders to close it.
UI_a028_FrontTrunkPopupClosed.m: The vehicle may be limited to 15 MPH / 24 KPH for the entire drive cycle.

UI_a029_ModemMIA.d: One or more of the messages the User Interface (UI) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) receives periodically from the cellular modem is not received, indicating the cellular modem may be unavailable (MIA).
UI_a029_ModemMIA.m: The vehicle may intermittently or persistently loose cellular connectivity.

UI_a030_ModemVMCrash.d: The software running on the modem has encountered an unexpected or unrecoverable error, which may lead to a loss of cellular connectivity.
UI_a030_ModemVMCrash.m: There is a loss of cellular connectivity.

UI_a031_BrakeFluidLow.d: Indicates that the sensor at the master cylinder has detected low brake fluid.  Triggers red tell tale light on the cluster.
UI_a031_BrakeFluidLow.m: Potential danger of brakes not operating properly

UI_a032_CellModemRecoveryResets.d: The modem resets due to cell internet checks not passing. The modem runs internet checks every 600 seconds, and a modem reset occurs if the internet connectivity remains unavailable for 3,600 seconds,
UI_a032_CellModemRecoveryResets.m: There may a loss of cellular connectivity.

UI_a034_WakeupProblem.d: The User Interface (UI) PowerManager wakeup health-check did not pass because at least 1 peripheral (Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, cellular, touch screen, and display) is not ready or the PowerManager task was re

UI_a035_FrunkOpenChime.d: The front trunk primary latch is detected as open when the vehicle is shifted into a gear other than Park, which may lead to the front trunk being open while driving.

UI_a036_AudioWatchdog.d: Audio stack resetting UI within 1 minute of waking while vehicle parked, includes payload for reset reason.
UI_a036_AudioWatchdog.m: Vehicle audio and user interface (UI) may not behave as expected.

UI_a037_AudioWatchdogXrunStormError.d: Audio stack is resetting within 1 minute of the vehicle waking while parked. This is due to recurring XRUN messages present in the logs.
UI_a037_AudioWatchdogXrunStormError.m: Vehicle audio and user interface (UI) may not behave as expected.

UI_a038_AudioWatchdogA2bI2cLockupError.d: The A2B I2C bus experiences a lockup condition, which may cause audio devices on the A2B I2C bus to be inaccessible and may lead to a complete loss of audio.
UI_a038_AudioWatchdogA2bI2cLockupError.m: Audio devices on the A2B I2C bus may be inaccessible, and there may be a complete loss of audio.

UI_a039_AudioA2bNeedRediscovery.d: A condition was detected on the audio A2B primary node and now requires a new device discovery over the A2B bus, which may lead to a temporary interruption of audio. This alert only sets if the vehicl
UI_a039_AudioA2bNeedRediscovery.m: There may be a temporary interruption of audio.

UI_a045_ECallSystemCheckFailed.d: The cellular modem detects the emergency call (eCall) self-test did not pass. During vehicle start-up and when the drive rail is turned ON, the emergency call (eCall) system will attempt 1 self-test w

UI_a046_BackupCameraStreamError.d: Backup camera reported an error when streaming. There is a high probability of a HW fault or disconnected harness.
UI_a046_BackupCameraStreamError.m: Backup camera / Autopilot functionality not available or limited.

UI_a047_CellRoamingDisallowed.d: Out of data roaming area, Data will be re-enabled after entering roaming area.

UI_a048_AudioPremiumAmpCheckFailed.d: The status of the premium amplifier could not be read over the I2C bus, which may present the premium amplifier from performing as designed.
UI_a048_AudioPremiumAmpCheckFailed.m: The premium amplifier may not perform as designed and a new A2B discovery will occur.

UI_a049_BrakeShiftRequired.d: There is a shift request, but the drive rail is not ON and the brake pedal is not pressed, which may prevent the vehicle from being shifted from the Park gear.
UI_a049_BrakeShiftRequired.m: Press brake pedal to shift gears

UI_a050_BackupCameraIPUTimeout.d: Failed to start the backup-camera stream from UI.
UI_a050_BackupCameraIPUTimeout.m: Backup camera / Autopilot functionality not available or limited.

UI_a051_BackupCameraFrameTimeout.d: The User Interface (UI) is unable to read 10 consecutive frames from the backup camera, which may cause the backup camera to not perform as designed.
UI_a051_BackupCameraFrameTimeout.m: The backup camera may not perform as designed.

UI_a052_KernelPanicReported.d: The operating system encounters an error, preventing the system from running as expected and causing the UI to reboot.

UI_a053_QtCarExitError.d: The User Interface (UI) main application, also known as QtCar, has encountered an unexpected or unrecoverable error causing it to reboot.

UI_a054_AudioBoostPowerBad.d: The external amplifier's boost power has detected an issue during initialization.
UI_a054_AudioBoostPowerBad.m: The external amplifier may not function as expected. There may be a loss of audio.

UI_a065_WiFiRecoveryReset.d: The connection manager detected a condition requiring the Wi-Fi link to be reset, which may lead to a loss of Wi-Fi connectivity.
UI_a065_WiFiRecoveryReset.m: There may be a loss of Wi-Fi connectivity.

UI_a066_GpuHangDetected.d: The User Interface (UI) Electronic Control Unit (ECU) detects the Graphical Processing Unit (GPU) experiences a software error known as a GPU hang.
UI_a066_GpuHangDetected.m: The center display may be blank, frozen, or have unintended artifacts on the User Interface (UI).

UI_a067_WarnOnI915.d: The Driver Assistance Electronic Control Unit (ECU) has detected an issue with the Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) driver. A message containing 'WARN_ON(i915)' may be present in the logs.
UI_a067_WarnOnI915.m: The User Interface (UI) may not behave as expected. The UI screen may be blank.

UI_a068_VideoUnexpectedExit.m: Camera video stream unavailable or malfunctioning.

UI_a069_VideoUDPTimeout.d: The video process for the backup camera is not receiving the user datagram protocol (UDP) packets within the required time frame, which may lead to the camera video stream being unavailable.
UI_a069_VideoUDPTimeout.m: The camera video streams for the backup camera may be unavailable.

UI_a070_VideoPipelineWarning.d: The Media Control Unit (MCU) has detected a warning from the video pipeline. A video pipeline warning was posted on the Controller Area Network (CAN) bus.
UI_a070_VideoPipelineWarning.m: The rear view camera video stream may not function as expected. The video feed may be unavailable or not functioning as expected.

UI_a071_VideoPipelineError.d: The Media Control Unit (MCU) has detected an error from the video pipeline. A video pipeline error was posted on the Controller Area Network (CAN) bus.
UI_a071_VideoPipelineError.m: The rear view camera video stream may not function as expected. The video feed may be unavailable or not functioning as expected.

UI_a072_ECallAnalogMicFailed.d: The Ecall audio configurator 'Ecall Analog microphone' test failed.
UI_a072_ECallAnalogMicFailed.m: Ecall voice issues or Ecall unavailable

UI_a073_ECallDigitalMicFailed.d: The Ecall audio configurator 'Ecall Digital microphone' test failed.
UI_a073_ECallDigitalMicFailed.m: Ecall voice issues or Ecall unavailable

UI_a074_EMMCHangDetected.d: eMMC hanged and was not able to be read or written to.
UI_a074_EMMCHangDetected.m: UI may behave not as designed or screen may be blank.

UI_a075_BackupCameraInitError.m: Backup camera / Autopilot functionality not available or limited.

UI_a076_NumFalseManualECalls.d: An eCall has been cancelled manually with no passengers present and all doors closed.
UI_a076_NumFalseManualECalls.m: The eCall feature may not be functioning as expected.

UI_a077_AudioA2bMicChannelSwap.d: The A2B microphone channels are in a swapped state.

UI_a079_DashcamUsbDriveFull.m: Dashcam unable to write - USB drive is full

UI_a080_DashcamUsbDriveSlow.m: Some clips not saved - USB too slow for Dashcam

UI_a081_CellModemSearchExpired.m: There is a loss of cellular connectivity.

UI_a082_NumModemRebootStuckInECall.d: The cellular modem has been rebooted due to an active eCall lasting longer than 1,800 seconds, the backstop timer value.
